## рҹ…ҡ Priority K-early вҖ” е°ҚеӨ–йғЁзҪІзҙ…з·ҡе®үе…ЁпјҲи·Ҝз·ҡ C з¬¬ 2 ж®өпјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҡзҸҫиЎҢ auth й җиЁӯ `OMNISIGHT_AUTH_MODE=open`пјҲз„ЎиӘҚиӯүпјүгҖҒdefault admin еҜҶзўјзӮәеёёж•ё `omnisight-admin`гҖҒ`authenticate_password` з„ЎйҖҹзҺҮйҷҗеҲ¶гҖӮдёүиҖ…зӮәе°ҚеӨ–йғЁзҪІеүҚеҝ…й Ҳи§ЈжҺүзҡ„зҙ…зҮҲгҖӮ

### K1. й җиЁӯй…ҚзҪ®еј·еҢ– + йғЁзҪІжӘўжҹҘ
> **еҜҰжё¬зҸҫжіҒпјҲ2026-04-16 й©—иӯүпјү**пјҡ`backend/config.py` L296-329 `validate_startup_config` е·ІйғЁеҲҶеҜҰдҪңвҖ”вҖ”`debug=false` жҷӮжңғ hard-fail ж“ӢдёӢ `OMNISIGHT_AUTH_MODE=open` иҲҮ `OMNISIGHT_ADMIN_PASSWORD=omnisight-admin`пјҢдҪҶ `OMNISIGHT_DEBUG=true` е…ЁйҖҖеҢ–зӮә warningгҖӮ`backend/auth.py` L274-291 `ensure_default_admin()` д»ҚжңғиҮӘеӢ•е»ә `admin@omnisight.local` / `omnisight-admin`пјҢеҜҰжё¬ `POST /api/v1/auth/login` еҸҜзӣҙжҺҘд»Ҙи©Ій җиЁӯеҜҶзўјеҸ–еҫ— admin session + HttpOnly cookieпјҲSameSite=laxпјҢз„Ў Secure flagпјүгҖӮеүҚз«Ҝ `/login` е°ҺжөҒжӯЈеёёпјҲ`next` query param еӣһеҺҹй ҒпјүпјҢдҪҶ**з„Ўд»»дҪ•гҖҢйҰ–ж¬Ўзҷ»е…Ҙеј·еҲ¶ж”№еҜҶзўјгҖҚй—ңеҚЎ**вҖ”вҖ”е°ҺжөҒеҒҡе°ҚгҖҒcredential baseline жңӘйҺ–пјҢйҖҷжҳҜзӣ®еүҚжңҖеӨ§е°ҚеӨ–йғЁзҪІзҙ…з·ҡгҖӮ
- [x] Startup self-checkпјҡиӢҘ `ENV=production` дҪҶ `OMNISIGHT_AUTH_MODE != strict` вҶ’ жӢ’зө•е•ҹеӢ•пјҲжҳҺзўәйҢҜиӘӨиЁҠжҒҜ + йҖҖеҮәзўј 78пјүпјӣзӣ®еүҚеҸӘзңӢ `settings.debug` flagпјҢйңҖзҚЁз«Ӣ `ENV=production` еҲӨж–·йҒҝе…Қ debug ж„ҸеӨ–иў«иЁӯ true з№һйҒҺ
- [x] иӢҘ default admin еҜҶзўјд»ҚзӮә `omnisight-admin` вҶ’ е•ҹеӢ•жҷӮеј·еҲ¶жЁҷиЁҳ `users.must_change_password=1`пјҢзҷ»е…ҘеҫҢд»»дҪ• API йҷӨ `POST /auth/change-password` е…Ёеӣһ 428 Precondition RequiredпјӣеүҚз«Ҝ `/login` жҲҗеҠҹ response жӘўжё¬жӯӨж——жЁҷиҮӘеӢ•е°Һеҗ‘ `/settings/change-password`
- [x] Docker `Dockerfile.backend` / compose prodпјҡй җиЁӯ env `OMNISIGHT_AUTH_MODE=strict`
- [x] ж–Үд»¶ `docs/ops/security_baseline.md`пјҡеҲ—еҮәйғЁзҪІеүҚ checklistпјҲstrict modeгҖҒж”№еҜҶзўјгҖҒbearer token еғ…йҷҗ CI зҷҪеҗҚе–® IPпјү
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡе•ҹеӢ•жЁЎејҸжӘўжҹҘгҖҒжңӘж”№еҜҶзўјжҷӮ API жӢ’зө• 428гҖҒж”№е®ҢеҜҶзўјеҫҢж——жЁҷжё…йҷӨ
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

### K2. зҷ»е…ҘйҖҹзҺҮйҷҗеҲ¶ + еёіиҷҹйҺ–е®ҡ
- [x] `backend/rate_limit.py`пјҡin-process token bucketпјҲжңӘдҫҶ I еӨҡ worker жҷӮжҸӣ RedisпјүвҖ” й җиЁӯ `/auth/login` жҜҸ IP 5/minгҖҒжҜҸ email 10/hour
- [x] `users` иЎЁеҠ  `failed_login_count`гҖҒ`locked_until`пјӣйҖЈзәҢ 10 ж¬ЎеӨұж•— вҶ’ йҺ– 15 еҲҶйҗҳпјҲжҢҮж•ё backoff дёҠйҷҗ 24hпјү
- [x] йҺ–е®ҡжңҹй–“ `authenticate_password` еӣһ `None` дё”дёҚиө° PBKDF2пјҲзңҒ CPUпјү
- [x] жҲҗеҠҹзҷ»е…Ҙ reset counterпјӣaudit_log иЁҳйҢ„ `auth.login.fail` / `auth.lockout`
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡrate limit з”ҹж•ҲгҖҒlockout йҮӢж”ҫгҖҒжҷӮй–“иЎ°жёӣ
- й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### K3. Cookie flags + CSP й©—иӯү
- [x] й©—жүҖжңү `Set-Cookie`пјҡsession вҶ’ `HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Lax`пјӣCSRF вҶ’ `Secure + SameSite=Lax`пјҲдёҚеҸҜ HttpOnlyпјҢеүҚз«ҜиҰҒи®Җпјү
- [x] еҠ е…Ҙ `secure.py` middlewareпјҲиӢҘз„ЎпјүиЁӯ CSPгҖҒ`X-Frame-Options=DENY`гҖҒ`Referrer-Policy=strict-origin`гҖҒ`Permissions-Policy`
- [x] CSP еҡҙж јжЁЎејҸпјҲnonce-basedпјүйҒҝе…Қ inline scriptпјӣеүҚз«Ҝ Next.js config й…ҚеҗҲ
- [x] E2E жё¬и©Ұпјҡ`curl -I` й©— response headerпјӣPlaywright й©— CSP йҳ»ж“Ӣ inline eval
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

**K-early зёҪй җдј°**пјҡ**2 day**гҖӮе®ҢжҲҗеҚіеҸҜе°ҚеӨ–йғЁзҪІдёҚжңғиў«з«ӢеҲ»жү“зҲҶгҖӮ

---

## рҹ…ҷ Priority J вҖ” Multi-session Single-user HardeningпјҲи·Ҝз·ҡ C з¬¬ 3 ж®өпјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҡе–®дәәдҪҶеӨҡиҷ•зҷ»е…ҘпјҲзӯҶйӣ» / жүӢж©ҹ / еӨҡ tabпјүжҷӮзӣ®еүҚжңү 7 йЎһй«”й©—е•ҸйЎҢпјҡSSE е…Ёеҹҹе»Јж’ӯгҖҒlocalStorage еҗ„ж©ҹеҷЁдёҚеҗҢжӯҘгҖҒ`_ModeSlot` е…Ёеҹҹе…ұз”ЁгҖҒworkflow_run дҪөзҷјз„ЎжЁӮи§ҖйҺ–гҖҒз„Ў session з®ЎзҗҶ UIгҖҒaudit з„Ў session_idпјҲе·Із”ұ S0 и§ЈжҺүпјүгҖҒoperation mode е…ЁеҹҹгҖӮжӯӨ phase иЈңйҪҠгҖӮ

### J1. SSE per-session filter
- [x] Event envelope еҠ  `session_id` + `broadcast_scope: session|user|global`
- [x] еүҚз«Ҝ SSE client жҜ”е°Қз•¶еүҚ `session_id` йҒҺжҝҫпјҲй җиЁӯеҸӘзңӢиҮӘе·ұ session и§ёзҷјзҡ„ + user-level йҖҡзҹҘпјү
- [x] UI toggleпјҡгҖҢйЎҜзӨәжүҖжңүжҲ‘зҡ„ session дәӢд»¶гҖҚ/гҖҢеғ…жң¬ sessionгҖҚ
- [x] жё¬и©ҰпјҡеӨҡ session fixture вҶ’ й©—иӯүйҒҺжҝҫжӯЈзўә
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

### J2. Workflow_run жЁӮи§ҖйҺ–
- [x] `workflow_runs` еҠ  `version INTEGER DEFAULT 0` + migration
- [x] Retry / cancel / update endpointsпјҡrequire `If-Match: <version>` headerпјӣversion дёҚз¬Ұеӣһ 409
- [x] еүҚз«ҜжҢүйҲ•жҢүдёӢжҷӮеё¶з•¶еүҚ versionпјӣ409 вҶ’ жҸҗзӨәгҖҢеҸҰдёҖиҷ•е·Ідҝ®ж”№пјҢи«ӢйҮҚж–°ж•ҙзҗҶгҖҚ
- [x] жё¬и©ҰпјҡдёҰзҷј retry еҸӘжңүдёҖеҖӢжҲҗеҠҹ
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

### J3. Session management UI
- [x] `components/omnisight/session-manager-panel.tsx`пјҡеҲ— S0 `/auth/sessions` зөҗжһңпјҲdevice / IP / created / last_seenпјү
- [x] жҜҸеҲ— Revoke жҢүйҲ• + гҖҢзҷ»еҮәе…¶д»–жүҖжңүиЈқзҪ®гҖҚжҢүйҲ•
- [x] з•¶еүҚ session жЁҷиЁҳ "This device"
- [x] E2E жё¬и©Ұпјҡrevoke еҫҢи©ІиЈқзҪ®дёӢж¬Ў API call еҫ— 401
- й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### J4. localStorage еӨҡ tab еҗҢжӯҘ
- [x] жүҖжңү `omnisight:*` keys еҠ  `user_id` еүҚз¶ҙпјҲеҫһзҷ»е…Ҙ context еҸ–пјү
- [x] `window.addEventListener('storage', ...)` и·Ё tab еҗҢжӯҘ spec / locale / wizard зӢҖж…Ӣ
- [x] йҰ–ж¬Ўијүе…Ҙ wizard еҲӨж–·ж”№жҹҘ server-side `user_preferences` иЎЁпјҲе…ұз”Ёйӣ»и…Ұз¬¬дәҢдҪҝз”ЁиҖ…дёҚиў«и·ійҒҺпјү
- [x] жё¬и©ҰпјҡPlaywright йӣҷ tab scenario
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

### J5. Per-session Operation Mode
- [x] Operation Mode еҫһе…Ёеҹҹ config жҗ¬еҲ° `sessions.metadata.operation_mode`
- [x] `_ModeSlot` и®ҖеҸ–ж”№зӮә per-sessionпјҲbudget д»ҚжҳҜе…Ёеҹҹжұ пјҢдҪҶ mode cap еҖӢеҲҘиЁҲз®—пјү
- [x] UI mode selector еҸӘеҪұйҹҝз•¶еүҚ sessionпјҢtooltip йЎҜзӨәгҖҢжӯӨиЁӯе®ҡеғ…еҪұйҹҝжң¬иЈқзҪ®гҖҚ
- [x] жё¬и©ҰпјҡA session turbo + B session supervised еҗ„иҮӘ mode cap з”ҹж•Ҳ
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

### J6. Audit UI её¶ session йҒҺжҝҫ
- [x] Audit жҹҘи©ўй ҒеҠ  session filterпјҲеҲ—иЎЁ + зӣ®еүҚ session еҝ«жҚ·йҲ•пјү
- [x] йЎҜзӨәжҜҸзӯҶ audit зҡ„ device / IPпјҲеҫһ session иҒҜзөҗпјү
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

**J зёҪй җдј°**пјҡ**3.5 day**

---

## рҹ…ҡ Priority K-rest вҖ” Auth Hardening е®Ңж•ҙзүҲпјҲи·Ҝз·ҡ C з¬¬ 4 ж®өпјү

### K4. Session rotation + binding
- [x] зҷ»е…ҘжҲҗеҠҹ / еҜҶзўји®Ҡжӣҙ / ж¬ҠйҷҗеҚҮзҙҡ вҶ’ з”ўж–° tokenпјҢиҲҠ token еҜ« `rotated_from` жҢҮеҗ‘ж–° tokenпјӣиҲҠ token grace 30s е…ҒиЁұ in-flight requestпјҢд№ӢеҫҢеӨұж•Ҳ
- [x] Session з¶Ғ UA hashпјҲйқһ IPпјҢз§»еӢ•з¶Іи·Ҝ IP еёёи®ҠпјүпјӣUA и®ҠжӣҙиЁҳиӯҰе‘ҠдҪҶдёҚеј·еҲ¶зҷ»еҮә
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡrotate жөҒзЁӢгҖҒgrace windowгҖҒUA и®ҠжӣҙиӯҰе‘Ҡ
- й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### K5. MFA (TOTP) + Passkey (WebAuthn) йӘЁжһ¶
- [x] `user_mfa` иЎЁпјҡmethod (totp/webauthn)гҖҒsecret/credentialгҖҒcreated_atгҖҒlast_used
- [x] TOTPпјҡenrollment QR + verify flowпјӣbackup codesпјҲ10 зө„пјҢе–®ж¬Ўпјү
- [x] WebAuthnпјҡ`py_webauthn` еҘ—д»¶гҖҒregister + authenticate endpoints
- [x] зҷ»е…ҘжөҒзЁӢпјҡеҜҶзўј OK вҶ’ иӢҘжңү MFA вҶ’ `mfa_required=true` response вҶ’ й©—йҖҡйҒҺжүҚ `create_session(mfa_verified=True)`
- [x] Strict mode еҸҜиЁӯ `require_mfa=True` еј·еҲ¶ admin / operator е•ҹз”Ё
- [x] UIпјҡSettings вҶ’ MFA з®ЎзҗҶй Ғ
- [x] жё¬и©ҰпјҡTOTP drift е®№еҝҚгҖҒbackup code е–®ж¬ЎжҖ§
- й җдј°пјҡ**2.5 day**

### K6. Bearer token per-key + зЁҪж ё
- [x] е»ўйҷӨе–®дёҖ `OMNISIGHT_DECISION_BEARER` envпјӣж”№ `api_keys` иЎЁпјҲidгҖҒnameгҖҒhashed_keyгҖҒscopesгҖҒcreated_byгҖҒlast_used_ipгҖҒenabledпјү
- [x] CLI / CI жҶ‘еҖӢеҲҘ key е‘јеҸ«пјӣaudit_log её¶ `session_id=bearer:<key_id>` еҸҜиҝҪ
- [x] Admin UI е»ә / rotate / revoke key
- [x] Migration иҲҠ envпјҡе•ҹеӢ•жҷӮеҒөжё¬ вҶ’ иҮӘеӢ•е»әдёҖзӯҶ `legacy-bearer` key дёҰзҷјиӯҰе‘ҠиҰҒжұӮзӣЎеҝ«жҸӣ
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡscope йҷҗеҲ¶пјҲеҸӘиғҪе‘јеҸ«зҷҪеҗҚе–® endpointпјүгҖҒrevoke еҚіжҷӮз”ҹж•Ҳ
- й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### K7. еҜҶзўјж”ҝзӯ– + Argon2id еҚҮзҙҡи·Ҝеҫ‘
- [x] еҜҶзўјеј·еәҰпјҡжңҖзҹӯ 12 еӯ—гҖҒzxcvbn score вүҘ 3
- [x] ж–°еҜҶзўјжҜ”е°Қжӯ·еҸІ 5 зӯҶпјҲ`password_history` иЎЁпјү
- [x] Hash ж јејҸж”ҜжҸҙ `argon2id$...`пјӣй©—иӯүжҷӮйӣҷи»ҢпјҲиҲҠ pbkdf2 й©—жҲҗеҠҹеҫҢиҮӘеӢ• rehash жҲҗ argon2idпјү
- [x] `argon2-cffi` дҫқиіҙеҠ е…Ҙ
- [x] жё¬и©ҰпјҡеҚҮзҙҡи·Ҝеҫ‘гҖҒиҲҠ hash д»ҚеҸҜй©—гҖҒдёӢж¬Ўзҷ»е…ҘиҮӘеӢ•еҚҮзҙҡ
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

**K-rest зёҪй җдј°**пјҡ**5 day**

**и·Ҝз·ҡ C зёҪй җдј°**пјҡS0 (0.5) + K-early (2) + J (3.5) + K-rest (5) = **11 day**

---

## рҹ…  Priority Q вҖ” Multi-Device ParityпјҲзӨҫзҫӨе№іеҸ°зҙҡеӨҡиЈқзҪ®й«”й©—пјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҲ2026-04-22 operator auditпјүпјҡPriority S + J + K-early + K-rest е·ІжҠҠгҖҢеӨҡ session еҸҜд»ҘдёҰеӯҳгҖҚгҖҢsession еҸҜиў«еҲ—иЎЁ / иёўжҺүгҖҚгҖҢper-session SSE filterгҖҚгҖҢUA hash bindingгҖҚгҖҢworkflow_run жЁӮи§ҖйҺ–гҖҚйҖҷдәӣ**ж©ҹжҲҝзҙҡ**еӨҡ session еҹәзӨҺжү“з©©пјҢдҪҶеҫһ**з”ўе“Ғзҙҡ**дҫҶзңӢйӮ„е·®дёҖжҲӘвҖ”вҖ”operator зҡ„жңҹжңӣжҳҜгҖҢеҗҢдёҖеёіиҷҹеңЁзӯҶйӣ» / жүӢж©ҹ / иҫҰе…¬е®ӨзҖҸиҰҪеҷЁеҗҢжҷӮзҷ»е…ҘпјҢдёүеҸ°йғҪиғҪзңӢеҲ°дёҖжЁЈзҡ„зӢҖж…ӢгҖҒеҒҡдёҖжЁЈзҡ„ж“ҚдҪңгҖҒеҫ—еҲ°дёҖжЁЈзҡ„еӣһжҮүпјҢе°ұеғҸ Facebook / Linear / Slack / NotionгҖҚгҖӮaudit жҜ”е°ҚеҫҢ 8 еҖӢзјәеҸЈпјҡ
>
> 1. **ж”№еҜҶзўјеҸӘ rotate з•¶еүҚ session**пјҲK4 зҡ„зҜ„еңҚ gapпјүвҖ”вҖ”жүӢж©ҹиў«еҒ· вҶ’ зӯҶйӣ»ж”№еҜҶзўј вҶ’ жүӢж©ҹ session йӮ„жҙ» 8 е°ҸжҷӮгҖӮжҘӯз•ҢдёҖиҮҙеҒҡжі•жҳҜ rotate жүҖжңү non-current sessionгҖӮ
> 2. **ж–°иЈқзҪ®зҷ»е…ҘжІ’ signal**вҖ”вҖ”дҪҝз”ЁиҖ…дёҚзҹҘйҒ“жңүж–°иЈқзҪ®дёҠз·ҡпјҢеёіиҷҹиў«зӣңжҷӮйҢҜйҒҺз¬¬дёҖжҷӮй–“иӯҰиҰәпјҲGoogle / Facebook / Apple йғҪеҒҡпјүгҖӮ
> 3. **Cross-device state sync жңӘзі»зөұжҖ§зӣӨй»һ**вҖ”вҖ”SharedKV (I10) + SSE broadcast е·ІиҰҶи“ӢгҖҢLLM keys / integration settings / workflow_runsгҖҚпјҢдҪҶ chat history / INVOKE ијёе…ҘиҚүзЁҝ / еҖӢдәәеҢ–еҒҸеҘҪпјҲlocale / themeпјүзӯүе…¶д»–и·Ҝеҫ‘жңӘйҖҗдёҖй©—йҒҺе“Әдәӣиө°еҫ—йҖҡгҖҒе“Әдәӣиө°дёҚйҖҡгҖӮ
> 4. **SSE event scope й җиЁӯжңӘ policy-review**вҖ”вҖ”J1 и®“жҜҸеҖӢ event еҸҜе®Је‘Ҡ `broadcast_scope: session|user|global`пјҢдҪҶжҜҸеҖӢ emit point зҡ„еҜҰйҡӣйҒёж“ҮжІ’жңүйӣҶдёӯ docпјҢжңүдәӣжҮүи©І `user` зҡ„еҸҜиғҪжҺӣ `session`гҖҒжңүдәӣжҮүи©І `session` зҡ„еҸҜиғҪжҺӣ `global`гҖӮ
> 5. **Active device presence indicator зјә**вҖ”вҖ”UI дёҠзңӢдёҚеҲ°гҖҢдҪ зӣ®еүҚеңЁе№ҫеҖӢиЈқзҪ®дёҠз·ҡгҖҚзҡ„йӣҶеҗҲжҖ§ж„ҹзҹҘпјҲSlack / Linear зҡ„з¶ зҮҲ + countпјүгҖӮ
> 6. **Draft persistence across devices зјә**вҖ”вҖ”INVOKE йқўжқҝ / chat ијёе…Ҙжү“дәҶдёҖеҚҠжҸӣиЈқзҪ®жңғж¶ҲеӨұпјҲGoogle Docs / Slack йғҪжҳҜеҚҠиҮӘеӢ•еҗҢжӯҘпјүгҖӮ
> 7. **Optimistic concurrency еҸӘиҰҶи“Ӣ workflow_runs**пјҲJ2 зҜ„еңҚпјүвҖ”вҖ”Tasks / Tenant secrets / Project settings зӯүе…¶д»– mutation endpoint е…©иЈқзҪ®еҗҢжҷӮж”№еҸҜиғҪеҫҢеҜ«иҰҶи“ӢеүҚеҜ«пјҲlast-write-winsпјүпјҢжІ’жңү 409 warningгҖӮ
> 8. **Multi-device E2E test зјә**вҖ”вҖ”J зі»еҲ—жҜҸеҖӢ item жңүе–®й …жё¬и©ҰпјҢдҪҶгҖҢзӯҶйӣ» + жүӢж©ҹе…©еҖӢ Playwright tab дёҰй–ӢгҖҚйҖҷзЁ®з«ҜеҲ°з«Ҝ scenario еҫһжңӘи·‘йҒҺгҖӮ
>
> **иЁӯиЁҲеҺҹеүҮ**пјҡиҰҒе°ҚйҪҠзҡ„жҳҜ**Linear / Slack / Notion йҖҷйЎһ SaaS е·Ҙе…·зҙҡзҡ„еӨҡиЈқзҪ® UX**пјҢдёҚжҳҜйҠҖиЎҢзҙҡзҡ„гҖҢж–°иЈқзҪ®еҝ…й ҲйҮҚж–° MFAгҖҚйӮЈзЁ®еҡҙж јй–ҖгҖӮе…·й«”иҰҸеүҮпјҡ
> - ж–°иЈқзҪ®зҷ»е…Ҙ вҶ’ йҖҡзҹҘгҖҒдҪҶдёҚж“ӢпјҲsecurity signal е„Әе…Ҳж–ј security frictionпјүгҖӮ
> - зӢҖж…Ӣи®ҠжӣҙпјҲsettings / task / workflowпјүвҶ’ жүҖжңүиЈқзҪ®еҜҰжҷӮеҗҢжӯҘпјҢд»»дёҖиЈқзҪ®еҸҜжҺҘеҠӣгҖӮ
> - з§ҒжңүдәӢд»¶пјҲжҹҗ session зҡ„ INVOKE stream / жң¬ж©ҹ logпјүвҶ’ еҸӘжҺЁзөҰеҺҹиЈқзҪ®гҖӮ
> - иЎқзӘҒз·ЁијҜ вҶ’ з”Ё optimistic lock 409 жҸҗзӨәпјҢиҖҢйқһе–®ж–№йқў last-write-winsгҖӮ
> - ж”№еҜҶзўј / MFA ж’ӨйҠ· / ж¬ҠйҷҗйҷҚзҙҡ вҶ’ з«ӢеҚіиёўе…¶д»–жүҖжңү sessionпјҲе®үе…Ёзҙ…з·ҡпјҢдёҚеҚ”е•ҶпјүгҖӮ
>
> **зӣёдҫқ**пјҡPriority S вң… / J1-J6 вң… / K4 session rotation infra вң… / I10 cross-worker Redis вң…гҖӮзЎ¬зӣёдҫқйӣ¶гҖӮQ жҳҜеңЁдёҠиҝ° foundation дёҠиЈң**з”ўе“Ғе®Ңж•ҙеәҰ**зҡ„дёҖеұӨпјҢеҸҜз«ӢеҚій–Ӣе·ҘгҖӮ
>
> **е’Ңе…¶д»– Priority зҡ„й—ңдҝӮ**пјҡQ жҳҜ Priority J зҡ„гҖҢз”ўе“Ғеҗ‘гҖҚе»¶дјёпјҲJ и§Ј 7 йЎһжҠҖиЎ“ bugгҖҒQ и§Ј 8 йЎһ UX/security gapпјүпјӣQ.1 жҳҜ K4 checklist зҡ„ gap-fillпјҲK4 еӢҫйҒёдҪҶиЎҢзӮәдёҚе®Ңж•ҙпјүпјӣQ.3-Q.4 и·Ё Priority зӣӨй»һпјӣQ.7 е»¶дјё J2 жЁӮи§ҖйҺ–пјӣQ.8 зӮә multi-device acceptance test harnessгҖӮ

е…©еҖӢеӢ•дҪңпјүпјӣ**#5 Chat history** вҖ” еҜҰйҡӣжҳҜ `list` дёҚжҳҜ dequeпјҲ`chat.py:30` `_history: list[OrchestratorMessage] = []`пјүпјҢеҗҢе•ҸйЎҢдҪҶ unbounded growthпјӣдё” `getChatHistory()` е·ІеңЁ `lib/api.ts:959-961` е°ҺеҮәдҪҶз„Ў `.tsx` ж¶ҲиІ»гҖҒжҳҜ orphan endpointпјӣ**#6 INVOKE** вҖ” TODO еҺҹеҒҮиЁӯгҖҢscope=session еҸӘжҺЁ originatorгҖҚдҪҶеҜҰжё¬ 10 еҖӢ `emit_invoke` call site (`invoke.py:316,322,337,379,585,612,897,1221,1237`) е…Ёз„Ў `session_id` / `broadcast_scope` еҸғж•ё вҶ’ й җиЁӯ `"global"` вҶ’ жҜҸеҖӢйҖЈз·ҡ client йғҪж”¶еҫ—еҲ°пјҲеҸҚеҗ‘ gapпјҡдёҚжҳҜз¶Ғеҫ—еӨӘз·ҠгҖҒжҳҜз¶Ғеҫ—еӨӘй¬ҶпјүгҖӮ**еҹәзӨҺжһ¶ж§Ӣи§ҖеҜҹ** (docs В§1.2)пјҡ`EventBus._deliver_local` (`events.py:81-105`) зҡ„ scope filter еҸӘ enforce `"tenant"`пјҢ`"user"` / `"session"` `broadcast_scope="user"` зҸҫеңЁеҸӘжҳҜ hintгҖҒдёҚжҳҜ security boundaryпјҲз•ҷзөҰ Q.4 #298 ж”¶ж–ӮпјүгҖӮ**Follow-up еӯҗд»»еӢҷжҺ’еәҸ**пјҲdocs В§4пјүпјҡP0 chat history жҗ¬ DBпјҲcheckbox #3 жң¬дҫҶе°ұжңүпјү+ P0 workflow_run SSE wiringпјҲ2hпјүпјӣP1 task CRUD emit иЈң create/deleteпјҲ1hпјү+ notifications read broadcastпјҲ1hпјү+ user prefs SSE pushпјҲ1hпјүпјӣP2 INVOKE scope split иҲҮ integration settings SSE з•ҷзөҰ Q.4 #298 policy sweep дёҖиө·еҒҡйҒҝе…ҚйҮҚе·ҘгҖӮеҗҲиЁҲ P0+P1 вүҲ 1.5 dayпјҢе°Қеҫ—дёҠеҺҹдј°гҖӮSOP Step 1 module-global auditпјҡжң¬ж¬ЎжҳҜгҖҢзҙ”зЁҪж ё + docs еҜ«е…ҘгҖҚз„Ўж–° code/state и®ҠеӢ•пјҢдҪҶ audit жң¬иә«жӯЈжҳҜеңЁжүҫеҲҘдәәзҡ„ module-global state е•ҸйЎҢвҖ”вҖ”#5 зҡ„ `_history` list е°ұжҳҜжңҖе…ёеһӢзҡ„з¬¬дёүзЁ®дёҚеҗҲж јзӯ”жЎҲпјҲж—ўжІ’ PG д№ҹжІ’ RedisпјҢд№ҹдёҚжҳҜгҖҢеҲ»ж„Ҹ per-workerгҖҚпјҢзҙ”зІ№ historical accidentпјүпјҢе·ІеҲ— P0 з”ұ checkbox #3 migration иҷ•зҗҶгҖӮ
  1. LLM provider keysпјҲвң… SharedKV + `provider_switch` eventпјҢе·Ій©— commit `8d626489`пјү
  2. Integration settingsпјҲGerrit / JIRA / GitHubпјүпјҲвҡ  PARTIALпјҡSharedKV еҜ«е…Ҙз©©пјҢдҪҶеҸӘжңү LLM еӯҗйӣҶи§ёзҷј `provider_switch` emitгҖҒGerrit/JIRA/GitHub-only з·ЁијҜз„Ў SSEпјӣfrontend еҸӘйқ й–Ӣ modal жҷӮ refetchпјҢдёҚжҳҜеҚіжҷӮпјү
  3. Workflow_run stateпјҲвқҢ GAPпјҡI2 RLS + J2 optimistic lock еӯҳеңЁпјҢдҪҶ `workflow_updated` event ж•ҙеҖӢ repo дёҚеӯҳеңЁгҖҒеҺҹзЁҝ вң… жЁҷиЁҳйңҖдёӢдҝ®пјү
  4. Task CRUDпјҲвҡ  PARTIALпјҡ`PATCH` emit `task_update`пјҢдҪҶ `POST` / `DELETE` з„Ў emitпјү
  5. **Chat history**пјҲвқҢ GAPпјҡ`_history` жҳҜ module-global **list**пјҲйқһ dequeпјүat `chat.py:30`пјҢи·Ё worker дёҚе…ұдә«гҖҒз„Ў SSEгҖҒfrontend з„Ўж¶ҲиІ»иҖ…вҖ”вҖ”дёүеұӨе…Ё missingпјӣжңҖеӨ§ remediation й …пјҢжҗ¬ DB иҰӢ checkbox #3пјү
  6. **INVOKE жҢҮд»Өзөҗжһң**пјҲвҡ  PARTIALпјҡеҜҰжё¬ `emit_invoke` е…ЁйғЁиө° `"global"` scope й җиЁӯпјҢдёҚжҳҜз¶Ғ sessionпјӣйңҖиҰҒжҢү action_type еҲҶ session (streaming) vs user (summary)пјӣз•ҷзөҰ Q.4 #298пјү
  7. User preferencesпјҲвқҢ GAPпјҡPG еҜ«е…Ҙ OKпјҢдҪҶз„Ў SSE emitпјӣJ4 `storage-bridge.tsx` зҡ„ `storage` event жҳҜ same-browser cross-tabгҖҒдёҚжҳҜ cross-deviceпјӣеҺҹзЁҝ вң… жЁҷиЁҳйңҖдёӢдҝ®пјү
  8. Notifications е·Іи®ҖзӢҖж…ӢпјҲвқҢ GAP е·ІзўәиӘҚпјҡ`POST /notifications/{id}/read` (`system.py:1442-1450`) з„Ў emitпјӣA иЈқзҪ®жЁҷе·Іи®ҖеҫҢ B иЈқзҪ® bell йңҖзӯүдёӢж¬Ў poll жүҚ decrementпјү

### Q.4 SSE event scope policy еҜ©иҰ– + еј·еҲ¶е®Је‘Ҡ (#298)

> **Carried-in spec from Q.3-SUB-7 (close-out 2026-04-24)** вҖ” `invoke` channel routing rule is **uniform `scope="user"` across all 24 `emit_invoke()` call sites** (9 in `invoke.py` + 5 in `webhooks.py` + 2 in `pipeline.py` + 2 in `intent_bridge.py` + 1 each in `integration.py`/`providers.py`/`orchestrator_gateway.py`/`orchestration_mode.py`/`merger_agent.py`/`merge_arbiter.py`). Audit hypothesised a session-vs-user split keyed by `action_type` ("`stream_chunk`/`agent_thinking` вҶ’ session"), but **no streaming-style action_type exists on the `invoke` channel today** вҖ” chat tokens flow via `EventSourceResponse` (HTTP body), not bus. Full call-site table + per-row rationale + acceptance criteria live in `docs/design/multi-device-state-sync.md` Path 6 вҶ’ "Q.3-SUB-7 close-out". Q.4 sweep should consume that table verbatim for the invoke slice.

й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

**Q зёҪй җдј°**пјҡ**6.5-7 day**

**еҮәиІЁзҜҖеҘҸе»әиӯ°**пјҡ
- **Wave 1пјҲеҝ…еҒҡпјҢ0.5 dayпјү**пјҡQ.1 ж”№еҜҶзўјиёўе…¶д»–иЈқзҪ® вҖ” жҳҜе®үе…Ёзҙ…з·ҡпјҢе–®йЎҶеҸҜзҚЁз«Ӣ ship дёҚзӯүе…¶д»– Q й …гҖӮ
- **Wave 2пјҲз”ўе“Ғе®Ңж•ҙеәҰпјҢ~2.5 dayпјү**пјҡQ.2 ж–°иЈқзҪ®йҖҡзҹҘ + Q.5 presence indicator + Q.8 E2E harness вҖ” е°Қ operator иҖҢиЁҖж„ҹеҸ—жңҖжҳҺйЎҜзҡ„дёүйЎҶгҖӮ
- **Wave 3пјҲеә•еұӨ hardeningпјҢ~3-4 dayпјү**пјҡQ.3 state sync зӣӨй»һ + Q.4 SSE scope policy + Q.6 draft + Q.7 optimistic lock ж“ҙејө вҖ” дёҚжҖҘдҪҶйҒІж—©иҰҒеҒҡпјҢзӯү Priority Y / Z / Y-prep еҮәе®ҢеҸҜд»ҘжҺ’йҖІдҫҶгҖӮ

**Non-goals**пјҲжҳҺзўәдёҚеҒҡпјҢйҒҝе…Қ scope ж“ҙејөпјүпјҡ
- вқҢ иЈқзҪ®й–“ handoff жҺҘеҠӣпјҲiOS Handoff зӯүзҙҡзҡ„ UXпјҢйҒҺеәҰ engineeringпјү
- вқҢ Presence йЎҜзӨәеҲҘзҡ„ user еңЁз·ҡпјҲи·Ё user зҡ„гҖҢиӘ°еңЁз·ҡгҖҚвҖ”еұ¬ chat app зү№жҖ§пјҢOmniSight дёҚжҳҜпјү
- вқҢ иҒІйҹі / йңҮеӢ•йҖҡзҹҘпјҲnative app еұӨпјҢWeb push зҚЁз«Ӣ priorityпјү
- вқҢ CRDT / OT йӣҷеҗ‘ mergeпјҲdraft з”Ё last-write-wins еӨ пјү
- вқҢ йҠҖиЎҢзҙҡеҡҙж ј MFAпјҲж–°иЈқзҪ®еҝ…йҮҚ MFA / ж•Ҹж„ҹж“ҚдҪң step-up MFAпјүвҖ”вҖ”е°ҚйҪҠ Linear / Slack зҡ„ијғй¬Ҷй–ҖпјҢжғіеҠ еҶҚй–Ӣж–° priority

## рҹ…ҳ Priority I вҖ” Multi-tenancy FoundationпјҲз·ҠжҺҘи·Ҝз·ҡ C д№ӢеҫҢпјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҡе®ҢжҲҗи·Ҝз·ҡ C еҫҢпјҢauth + session + audit еҹәзӨҺ hardenedпјҢжүҚйҒ©еҗҲжҠҠгҖҢе–®дәәеӨҡ sessionгҖҚж“ҙжҲҗгҖҢеӨҡз§ҹжҲ¶еӨҡ userгҖҚгҖӮжӯӨ phase жҳҜжӯЈејҸеӨҡдәәдёҠз·ҡзҡ„ gateгҖӮзӣёдҫқпјҡ**G4пјҲPostgresпјү** еҝ…й Ҳе®ҢжҲҗпјҲSQLite з„Ў RLSпјүгҖҒ**H1-H4aпјҲhost-awareпјү** еҝ…й Ҳе®ҢжҲҗпјҲI6 жүҚжңү token bucket еҸҜжӢҶ per-tenantпјүгҖҒ**S0 + K-early** еҝ…й Ҳе®ҢжҲҗпјҲauth baselineпјүгҖӮ

### I1. Schema: tenants + tenant_id ж¬„дҪҚ + еӣһеЎ«
- [x] ж–°еўһ `tenants` иЎЁпјҲid / name / plan / created_at / enabledпјү
- [x] `users` еҠ  `tenant_id`пјҲдёҖдәәдёҖ tenantпјӣжңӘдҫҶеӨҡ tenant з”Ё `user_tenant_membership` дёӯд»ӢиЎЁпјү
- [x] жүҖжңүжҘӯеӢҷиЎЁеҠ  `tenant_id`пјҡ`workflow_runs` / `debug_findings` / `decisions` / `event_log` / `audit_log` / `spec_*` / `artifacts` / `user_preferences`
- [x] Alembic йҒ·з§» + еӣһеЎ«и…іжң¬пјҲж—ўжңүиіҮж–ҷжӯёй җиЁӯ tenant `t-default`пјү
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡmigration е№ӮзӯүгҖҒеӣһеЎ«жӯЈзўә
- й җдј°пјҡ**3 day**

### I2. Query layer RLSпјҲSQLAlchemy global filterпјү
- [x] `backend/db_context.py`пјҡ`current_tenant_id()` context var
- [x] SQLAlchemy event listenerпјҡжүҖжңү SELECT иҮӘеӢ•жіЁе…Ҙ `WHERE tenant_id = :current`пјҲPostgres еҸҜж”№ RLS policyпјү
- [x] INSERT иҮӘеӢ•еЎ« `tenant_id = current`
- [x] Router еұӨ `require_tenant` dependency еҫһ user еҸ– tenant_id еЎһйҖІ context
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡи·Ё tenant жҹҘи©ўеӣһз©әгҖҒINSERT з„Ўжі•жҢҮе®ҡд»– tenant
- й җдј°пјҡ**2 day**

### I3. SSE per-tenant + per-user filterпјҲе»¶дјё J1пјү
- [x] Event envelope еҠ  `tenant_id`пјӣsubscriber иҮӘеӢ•з¶Ғз•¶еүҚ tenant
- [x] `broadcast_scope` пҝҪпҝҪе…… `tenant` йҒёй …
- [x] еӣһжӯёжё¬и©ҰпјҡA tenant зӣЈиҒҪеҸӘж”¶еҲ° A зҡ„дәӢд»¶
- й җдј°пјҡ**1.5 day**

### I4. Secrets per-tenant
- [x] `git_credentials` / `provider_keys` / `cloudflare_tokens`пјҲB12 з”ўзү©пјүе…Ёж”№ tenant-scoped иЎЁ
- [x] `backend/secrets.py` API еҠ  tenant_id з¶ӯеәҰ
- [x] Migrationпјҡж—ўжңүе…ұз”Ё credentials еҲҶзөҰ `t-default`
- [x] UIпјҡSettings й ҒеҲҶ tenant иҰ–ең–

### I5. Filesystem namespace
- [x] `data/tenants/<tid>/{artifacts,ingest,backups,workflow_runs}/`
- [x] жүҖжңүеҜ«и·Ҝеҫ‘еҮҪејҸжҺҘеҸ— tenant context
- [x] `_INGEST_ROOT` ж”№ `/tmp/omnisight_ingest/<tid>/`
- [x] Migration и…іжң¬жҗ¬ж—ўжңүжӘ”жЎҲеҲ° `t-default`
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡи·Ё tenant и·Ҝеҫ‘йҡ”йӣў
- й җдј°пјҡ**1.5 day**

### I6. Sandbox fair-shareпјҲDRF per-tenantпјү
- [x] H4a зҡ„ token bucket ж”№ per-tenantпјӣе…Ёеҹҹ CAPACITY_MAX з¶ӯжҢҒ 12
- [x] Dominant Resource FairnessпјҡжҜҸ tenant жӢҝеҲ° `CAPACITY_MAX / active_tenant_count` зҡ„дҝқиӯүжңҖдҪҺеҖј
- [x] з©әй–’жҷӮеҸҜи¶…з”Ёд»– tenant жңӘз”ЁйЎҚеәҰпјҢд»– tenant дҫҶжҷӮ 30s е…§и®“еҮә
- [x] Turbo еҠ  per-tenant cap йҳІе–® tenant зҚЁдҪ”
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡе…© tenant иІ ијүжЁЎж“¬гҖҒйӨ“жӯ»йҳІиӯ·
- й җдј°пјҡ**1.5 day**

### I7. Frontend tenant-aware
- [x] localStorage еүҚз¶ҙж”№ `omnisight:${tenantId}:${userId}:*`

- й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### I8. Audit log per-tenant hash chain
- [x] жҜҸ tenant зҚЁз«Ӣ genesis + chainпјҲPhase 53 hash chain ж”№ per-tenant еҲҶеІ”пјү
- [x] и·Ё tenant жҹҘи©ўе°ҒйҺ–пјҲadmin жҳҺзўәеҲҮ tenant жүҚиғҪзңӢпјү
- [x] й©—иӯүе·Ҙе…·ж”ҜжҸҙ per-tenant chain е®Ңж•ҙжҖ§
- й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### I9. Rate limit per-user / per-tenant
- [x] K2 зҡ„ rate limit ж“ҙе……з¶ӯеәҰпјҡper-IP + per-user + per-tenant
- [x] жҸӣ Redis token bucketпјҲзӮә I10 жә–еӮҷпјү
- [x] Quota configпјҡtenant.plan вҶ’ limits
- й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### I10. Multi-worker uvicorn + shared state
- [x] uvicorn `--workers N`пјҲN = CPU_cores / 2пјҢ16 core вҶ’ 8 workerпјү
- [x] Shared state жҗ¬ Redisпјҡ`_parallel_in_flight` / AIMD budget / SSE subscriber registry / rate limit
- [x] Sticky session иӢҘйңҖиҰҒпјҲSSE йҖЈз·ҡиҰҒй»Ҹ workerпјү
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡж»ҫеӢ•йҮҚе•ҹ worker з„ЎдәӢд»¶йҒәеӨұ
- й җдј°пјҡ**2 day**

**I зёҪй җдј°**пјҡ**16.5 day**

**зӣёдҫқ**пјҡI еҝ…й ҲеңЁ **G4 + H4a + S0 + K-early** е®ҢжҲҗеҫҢжүҚй–Ӣе·ҘпјӣI йҖІиЎҢдёӯ B12пјҲCF Tunnel wizardпјүиЁӯиЁҲйңҖй…ҚеҗҲ I4 ж”№ tenant-scopedгҖӮ

---

## рҹ…ң Priority M вҖ” Resource Hard IsolationпјҲSaaS зҙҡзЎ¬йӮҠз•Ңпјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҡI еҒҡе®ҢиіҮж–ҷ plane жҳҜзЎ¬йҡ”йӣўпјҲRLS / SSE filter / secrets / audit / и·Ҝеҫ‘пјүпјҢдҪҶиіҮжәҗеұӨд»ҚжҳҜгҖҢе…¬е№іжҺ’йҡҠгҖҚдёҚжҳҜгҖҢзЎ¬йӮҠз•ҢгҖҚгҖӮеӨҡз§ҹжҲ¶дёҰзҷјжҷӮд»Қжңғдә’зӣёжӢ–пјҡдёҖеҖӢ tenant зҡ„ compile еҗғж»ҝ CPU жңғи§ёзҷј AIMD derate и®“з„Ўиҫң tenant д№ҹйҷҚйҖҹпјӣзЈҒзўҹз„Ў quota жңғдә’еҗғпјӣdockerd е–®й»һе•ҹеӢ•еәҸеҲ—еҢ–пјӣprewarm pool е…ұз”ЁжңүзӢҖж…ӢжұЎжҹ“йўЁйҡӘпјӣLLM provider circuit breaker е…Ёеҹҹпјӣegress allowlist е…ұз”ЁгҖӮжӯӨ Phase иЈңйҪҠйҒ” SaaS зҙҡзЎ¬йҡ”йӣў + per-tenant иЁҲиІ»еҸҜиғҪгҖӮ
>
> зӣёдҫқпјҡ**I6пјҲDRF token bucketпјү** жҳҜ M1 ж¬ҠйҮҚжҳ е°„еҹәзӨҺпјӣ**I4пјҲsecrets per-tenantпјү** жҳҜ M3 circuit breaker per-key еҹәзӨҺпјӣ**I5пјҲfilesystem namespaceпјү** жҳҜ M2 quota еҹәзӨҺпјӣ**H1пјҲhost metricsпјү** жҳҜ M4 cgroup metrics ж“ҙеұ•еҹәзӨҺгҖӮ

### M1. Cgroup CPU/Memory зЎ¬йҡ”йӣўпјҲе°Қжҳ  DRF tokenпјү
- [x] Sandbox launch жҷӮ `docker run --cpus=<weight> --memory=<limit>`пјӣж¬ҠйҮҚз”ұ I6 DRF token жҠҳз®—пјҲ1 token вүҲ 1 core Г— 512MBпјү
- [x] `backend/container.py` `start_container()` жҺҘ `tenant_budget` еҸғж•ёпјҢиҮӘеӢ•з®— `--cpus` / `--memory`
- [x] Cgroup v2 `cpu.weight` й©—иӯүпјҡA tenant 4-token job + B tenant 1-token job еҗҢж ёдёҰи·‘ вҶ’ CPU жҷӮй–“ 4:1пјҲ`backend/cgroup_verify.py` + 4:1 ratio acceptance testпјү
- [x] OOM еҒөжё¬пјҡcontainer hit memory limit вҶ’ audit_log иЁҳйҢ„ `sandbox.oom` + еӣһ sandbox_result with errorпјҢдёҚеҪұйҹҝе…¶д»– tenantпјҲ`_oom_watchdog` + `sandbox_oom_total{tenant_id,tier}` metricпјү
- [x] жё¬и©ҰпјҡдёҰзҷј CPU жү“ж»ҝеҜҰжё¬гҖҒmemory limit зІҫзўәжҖ§гҖҒж¬ҠйҮҚе…¬е№іжҖ§пјҲ21 tests in `test_container_tenant_budget.py` + `test_cgroup_verify.py`пјү
- й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### M2. Per-tenant Disk Quota + LRU Cleanup
- [x] `data/tenants/<tid>/` еҠ  `quota.yaml`пјҡ`soft=5GB / hard=10GB`пјҲplan й©…еӢ•пјҢ`backend/tenant_quota.PLAN_DISK_QUOTAS` free/starter/pro/enterprise еӣӣзҙҡпјү
- [x] Background sweepпјҲжҜҸ 5 minпјүпјҡ`backend/tenant_quota.run_quota_sweep_loop()` per-tenant вҶ’ и¶… soft зҷј `tenant_storage_warning` SSEпјҲ30 min cooldownпјүпјӣи¶… hard `start_container` й җе…Ҳ raise `QuotaExceeded` вҶ’ workspaces router еӣһ 507 Insufficient Storage
- [x] LRU cleanupпјҡи¶… soft жҷӮиҮӘеӢ•еҲӘ `workflow_runs/` дёӢжңҖиҲҠзҡ„е®ҢжҲҗ runпјҲдҝқз•ҷжңҖиҝ‘ `keep_recent_runs` зӯҶ + жүҖжңү `.keep` жЁҷиЁҳ + `.in_progress` sentinel ж°ёдёҚеҲӘпјү

- [x] UIпјҡSettings вҶ’ Storage Quota еҚҖеЎҠпјҲ`integration-settings.tsx StorageQuotaSection`пјүпјҢйӣҷ bar (soft/hard) + еӯҗзӣ®йҢ„ breakdown + жүӢеӢ• LRU жҢүйҲ•
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡ28 й … вҖ” plan/quota.yaml/measure/check_hard/lru/cleanup_tmp/sweep/start_container gate/`/storage/*` REST

## рҹ…Ё-prep Priority Y-prep вҖ” Gerrit + JIRA Integration ж”¶е°ҫпјҲY й–Ӣе·ҘеүҚзҡ„зҸҫз¶І hardeningпјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҲ2026-04-22 operator auditпјүпјҡGerrit еңЁ commit `1ad3e69e` / `c9514ab2` еҫҢе·Із¶“ production-readyпјҲ5-step wizard зңҹ probeгҖҒе…Ҙз«ҷ webhook зңҹ dispatchгҖҒйӣҷ +2 submit rule зЎ¬й–ҳгҖҒreplication fan-out еҸҜз”ЁгҖҒ4-worker дёҖиҮҙжҖ§е·Ідҝ®пјүгҖӮJIRA еӨ–йҖҒ + intent bridge д№ҹеҸҜз”ЁгҖӮдҪҶ audit жүҫеҲ° 3 еҖӢе°ҸзјәеҸЈпјҢеұ¬ж–ј**зҸҫз¶ІдёҠз·ҡеүҚи©ІиЈңгҖҒдҪҶжІ’еӨ§еҲ°иҰҒзӯү Priority Y жүҚеҒҡ**зҡ„ж”¶е°ҫй …зӣ®вҖ”вҖ”жүҖд»ҘеҲҮеҮә Y-prepпјҢж”ҫеңЁ Priority Y зҡ„з¬¬дёҖйЎҶеүҚзҪ®е·ҘдҪңгҖӮ
>
> **Y-prep зҡ„зҜ„еңҚзҙ…з·ҡ**пјҡеҸӘиЈңзҸҫжңүеҠҹиғҪзјәеҸЈпјҢдёҚж“ҙејө scopeгҖӮжүҖжңүйңҖиҰҒж–° schema / ж–°з§ҹжҲ¶йҡ”йӣў / ж–° UI зҡ„й …зӣ®пјҲдҫӢеҰӮ JIRA per-tenant жҶ‘иӯүгҖҒJIRA вҶ’ agent automation е‘Ҫд»ӨйҖҡйҒ“пјүйғҪе»¶еҲ° Y жң¬й«”еҒҡгҖӮY-prep зҡ„жҜҸйЎҶ step йғҪжҮүи©ІиғҪеңЁ 1 дәә 1-2 еӨ©е…§зҚЁз«Ӣ shipпјҢдёҚзӯү Y1 зҡ„ schema migrationгҖӮ
>
> **зӣёдҫқ**пјҡз„ЎзЎ¬йҳ»еЎһпјҢеҸҜз«ӢеҚій–Ӣе·ҘгҖӮе®ҢжҲҗеҫҢ Gerrit / JIRA дёІжҺҘй«”й©—е°ұжҳҜгҖҢиЁӯе®Ңе°ұз”ҹз”ўеҸҜз”ЁгҖҚпјҢзӮә operator зңҹеҜҰдёҠз·ҡ Phase 26 йӢӘи·ҜгҖӮ

### Y-prep.1 Gerrit е…Ҙз«ҷ webhook дёүдәӢд»¶ routing жё¬и©Ұ (#287)

зҸҫеңЁ `test_webhooks.py:9-25` еҸӘй©— HMAC + gerrit_enabled й–ҳй–ҖпјҢжІ’й©—дёүеҖӢеҜҰйҡӣдәӢд»¶пјҲ`patchset-created` / `comment-added` / `change-merged`пјүжңүжІ’жңү dispatch еҲ° `webhooks.py:175-363` е°ҚжҮүзҡ„ handlerгҖӮProduction з¬¬дёҖйҖұйқ  debug log и§ҖеҜҹжҳҜжҡ«жҷӮж–№жЎҲпјҢй•·жңҹиҰҒжңү regression testгҖӮ

- [x] `test_webhooks.py::TestGerritEventRouting`пјҡдёүеҖӢжё¬и©ҰеҮҪејҸпјҢжҜҸеҖӢ POST дёҖеҖӢеҗҲжі• HMAC-signed payloadпјҢmock жҺүдёӢжёёпјҲ`_on_patchset_created` spawn agent / `_on_comment_added` file task / `_on_change_merged` push replicationпјүпјҢassert mock иў«е‘јеҸ«йҒҺдёҖж¬Ўдё”еҸғж•ёжӯЈзўәгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: new `TestGerritEventRouting` class in `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py` with 3 test functions вҖ” `test_patchset_created_dispatches_to_on_patchset_created`, `test_comment_added_dispatches_to_on_comment_added`, `test_change_merged_dispatches_to_on_change_merged`. Each signs a real HMAC-SHA256 payload with a scoped pytest-only secret, `monkeypatch.setattr`s all three handlers to `AsyncMock`, POSTs the event, asserts the matching handler was called once with the correct positional args (`(conn, body)` for `_on_patchset_created`, `(body,)` for the other two), and asserts the two sibling handlers were NOT called вҖ” the "each test doubles as a negative case for the other two events" contract. 6/6 tests in `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py` green against the `omnisight-pg-test` container (OMNI_TEST_PG_URL=postgresql://omni_test:omni_test_pw@localhost:5434/omni_test). Remaining Y-prep.1 sub-bullets (boundary tests for `Code-Review: -1` vs `+1`, `gerrit_replication_targets` empty/whitespace skip, p0 marker in pytest.ini) are still open.)*
- [x] жҜҸеҖӢжё¬и©ҰеҠ  negative caseпјҡдәӢд»¶еһӢеҲҘдёҚ match зҡ„ payload еҝ…й Ҳ **дёҚ** и§ёзҷј handlerпјҲe.g. `patchset-created` зҡ„ payload дёҚи©І trigger `_on_change_merged`пјүгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: new `TestGerritEventRoutingNegativeCases` class in `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py` promotes the negative contract to first-class parametrised tests. 6 parametrised cases cover every `(posted_event_type, forbidden_handler)` pair where the posted type must NOT route to the named handler вҖ” `patchset-createdвҶ’not__on_comment_added`, `patchset-createdвҶ’not__on_change_merged`, `comment-addedвҶ’not__on_patchset_created`, `comment-addedвҶ’not__on_change_merged`, `change-mergedвҶ’not__on_patchset_created`, `change-mergedвҶ’not__on_comment_added`. Plus one extra test `test_unknown_event_triggers_no_handler` for `ref-updated` (locks the else-branch at `webhooks.py:115-116` вҖ” unknown event must 200 but fire no handler). Each test signs a real HMAC-SHA256 payload so the signature verifier is exercised alongside the dispatcher. All 13 tests in `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py` green against `omnisight-pg-test` (OMNI_TEST_PG_URL=postgresql://omni_test:omni_test_pw@localhost:5434/omni_test). Rationale: the prior `TestGerritEventRouting` tests already asserted `assert_not_called` on sibling handlers, but that embedded coverage is fragile вҖ” a future refactor (e.g. registry-based dispatcher) could drop those lines while the positive assertions still pass. First-class parametrised negative tests survive that refactor.)*
- [x] йӮҠз•Ңпјҡ`comment-added` еҸӘеңЁ `Code-Review: -1` жҷӮ file taskпјҢ`+1` дёҚи©Іпјӣ`change-merged` еҸӘеңЁ `gerrit_replication_targets` йқһз©әжҷӮ fan-outпјӣз©әеӯ—дёІ / whitespace еҝ…й Ҳи·ійҒҺпјҲдёҚиғҪиӘӨи§ёзҷј `git push ""`пјүгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: two new classes in `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py` exercise the REAL handlers (dispatcher вҶ’ `_on_comment_added` / `_on_change_merged`) while mocking the leaf side-effects so the two business-logic invariants are observable in isolation. **`TestGerritCommentAddedReviewBoundary`** (7 tests, 1 happy path + 5 parametrised non-`-1` cases + 1 missing-approvals case): mocks `backend.routers.tasks._persist` + `backend.notifications.notify`; asserts `_persist_task` called once with `external_issue_id == change_id` and `"gerrit-review-fix"` in labels for `Code-Review: -1`; asserts `_persist_task.assert_not_called()` for `+1` / `1` / `0` / `+2` / `2` / missing-approvals. The parametrised neighbours (0 / +2) catch any future refactor that regresses to "non-positive" or "negative-value" semantics вҖ” the contract is exact-match-on-string-"-1", not a range. **`TestGerritChangeMergedReplicationTargetsBoundary`** (7 tests, 6 parametrised empty/whitespace cases + 1 positive control): mocks `backend.workspace._run` + `notify` + `intent_bridge.on_gerrit_change_merged` + the three `asyncio.create_task` background stubs (`_package_merged_artifacts` / `_save_merged_solution_to_l3` / `_trigger_ci_pipelines`); parametrises `gerrit_replication_targets` over `""` / `"   "` / `"\t\n"` / `","` / `", ,  ,"` / `" ,\t, \n, "`; asserts `_run.assert_not_called()` вҖ” not "no push" but NO SHELL CALL AT ALL (the `if not targets: return` at `webhooks.py:322` short-circuits before even the `git remote get-url` lookup, which is the correct invariant since that lookup ALSO uses an unvalidated target string). Positive control with `"origin-mirror, github-backup"` asserts exactly 4 `_run` calls (2 targets Г— (get-url + push)) and explicitly asserts `'git push ""' not in cmd` and `"git push ''" not in cmd` вҖ” the boundary's raison d'ГӘtre locked as a first-class assertion. All 27 tests in `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py` green in 7.05s against `omnisight-pg-test` (`OMNI_TEST_PG_URL=postgresql://omni_test:omni_test_pw@localhost:5434/omni_test`). Remaining Y-prep.1 sub-bullets: ~~HMAC real-sig fixture~~ (already done as part of the first sub-bullet вҖ” every test in this file signs with real `hmac.new(secret.encode(), raw, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()`), pytest.ini p0 marker still open.)*
- [x] жё¬и©Ұ fixture иҰҒз”Ёзңҹзҡ„ HMAC-SHA256 з°ҪеҗҚиҖҢйқһ mockпјҢзўәдҝқ `X-Gerrit-Signature` verifier е’Ң handler еҗҢжӯҘй©—йҒҺгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: two deliverables in `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py`. **(1) Two new pytest fixtures** promote the previously-module-level helpers to first-class fixture surfaces вҖ” `gerrit_sign` (returns the real `hmac.new(secret.encode(), raw, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()` callable, with a docstring contract that explicitly forbids replacing it with a Mock вҖ” "the assertion that this is the real primitive is what gives `gerrit_sign` fixture its contract value") and `gerrit_signed_post` (high-level fixture that POSTs to `/api/v1/webhooks/gerrit` with auto-signed `X-Gerrit-Signature` header, accepts `signature=<override>` for negative tests and `omit_signature=True` for the missing-header path). The existing `_sign` helper is kept in place so the prior 27 tests don't churn вҖ” the change is purely additive. **(2) New `TestGerritWebhookSignatureVerifier` class with 4 tests** that lock the verifierвҶ”handler synchronisation contract: `test_invalid_signature_rejected_no_dispatch` posts a bogus 64-char hex signature (`"deadbeef"*8`) and asserts 401 + all 3 handlers `assert_not_called`; `test_missing_signature_rejected_when_secret_configured` POSTs with no `X-Gerrit-Signature` header and asserts 401 + no dispatch (catches the regression "header is optional when secret configured"); `test_tampered_body_rejected_no_dispatch` signs body A but POSTs body B (same shape, different `change.id`) вҖ” proves the verifier digests the wire body, not a cached/empty buffer; `test_correct_signature_accepted_handler_dispatched` is the positive control through the SAME `gerrit_signed_post` fixture path used by the negative tests, so an "always-401" verifier regression can't sneak past either. **Why this contract is the bullet's deliverable**: the prior 27 tests all post correctly-signed payloads, so a verifier regression that always returns True (e.g. stray `scalar_ok = True`, flipped polarity at `webhooks.py:103`, deleted `compare_digest` "to speed tests up") would leave them green AND silently break prod. The 4 new tests prove the verifier is doing its job, completing the "fixture uses real HMAC AND the verifier is exercised in lock-step with the handler" loop. **Verification**: `OMNI_TEST_PG_URL=postgresql://omni_test:omni_test_pw@localhost:5434/omni_test PYTHONPATH=. python3 -m pytest backend/tests/test_webhooks.py -v --no-cov` вҶ’ **31/31 green** in 8.57s (3 pre-existing `TestWebhookEndpoint` + 3 `TestGerritEventRouting` + 7 `TestGerritEventRoutingNegativeCases` + 7 `TestGerritCommentAddedReviewBoundary` + 7 `TestGerritChangeMergedReplicationTargetsBoundary` + 4 NEW `TestGerritWebhookSignatureVerifier`). SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep zero-match. Remaining Y-prep.1 sub-bullet: pytest.ini p0 marker.)*
- [x] CI gateпјҡйҖҷдёүеҖӢжё¬и©ҰеҠ еҲ° `backend/pytest.ini` зҡ„ p0 markerпјҢдёҚеҮҶ skipгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: three deliverables вҖ” **(1) `backend/pytest.ini`** registers the new `p0` marker in the `markers =` section with a full docstring covering (a) which three tests it tags (`TestGerritEventRouting.test_patchset_created_dispatches_to_on_patchset_created` / `test_comment_added_dispatches_to_on_comment_added` / `test_change_merged_dispatches_to_on_change_merged`), (b) what "cannot be skipped" means mechanically (rejects `@pytest.mark.skip` + `@pytest.mark.skipif(True, ...)` at collection time; converts runtime `pytest.skip()` body calls into failures), and (c) the canonical way to run the gate (`pytest -m p0`). **(2) `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py`** вҖ” all three routing tests now carry `@pytest.mark.p0` immediately above the existing `@pytest.mark.asyncio` decorator, docstrings expanded to name-drop the p0 policy so future contributors don't silence a flake by adding `pytest.skip("fix later")`; `TestGerritEventRouting` class docstring gets a pointer to the conftest enforcement hook. **(3) `backend/tests/conftest.py`** wires two enforcement hooks вҖ” `pytest_collection_modifyitems` walks every collected item with a `p0` marker, iterates its markers, and for each `skip` / `skipif(truthy_literal)` appends to an offenders list; if non-empty raises `pytest.UsageError` (exit code 4 вҖ” "test config broken" not "test failed"), message lists every offender + nodeid so an author fixing multiple at once doesn't have to iterate; `skipif(False, ...)` / `skipif(None, ...)` stay allowed (a condition that provably never skips is benign and shouldn't trip the gate). Companion `pytest_runtest_makereport` is `@pytest.hookimpl(hookwrapper=True, tryfirst=True)`, narrowly scoped to `report.when == "call"` (setup/teardown skips like `pg_test_dsn` missing `OMNI_TEST_PG_URL` remain legitimate environment gates вҖ” the body-skip is the silent CI-gate hole we care about); flips `report.outcome` from `"skipped"` to `"failed"` and rewrites `longrepr` with a scannable `p0 CI-gate violation` prefix + the original skip reason preserved as breadcrumb. **Verification** вҖ” (a) `OMNI_TEST_PG_URL=postgresql://omni_test:omni_test_pw@localhost:5434/omni_test PYTHONPATH=. python3 -m pytest backend/tests/test_webhooks.py -v --no-cov -m p0` вҶ’ **3/3 green** (collects 31, deselects 28, runs the 3 p0-marked) in 2.31s; proves `-m p0` is a valid CI invocation and the three are correctly tagged. (b) full suite `python3 -m pytest backend/tests/test_webhooks.py -v --no-cov` вҶ’ **31/31 green** in 9.13s; no regression. (c) **Hook self-check** вҖ” temp file with `@pytest.mark.p0 @pytest.mark.skip(...)` вҶ’ pytest exits `EXIT_CODE=4` with `ERROR: p0 CI-gate violation: ...` message at collection; temp file with `@pytest.mark.p0` body calling `pytest.skip("...")` вҶ’ pytest exits `EXIT_CODE=1` with the test marked FAILED and longrepr `p0 CI-gate violation: ... called pytest.skip() during the test body. ... Original skip reason: ...`; temp file with benign `@pytest.mark.skipif(False, ...)` вҶ’ pytest exits `EXIT_CODE=0` with test PASSED (condition provably never skips, policy correctly allows). Scratch file removed. (d) SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/pytest.ini` + `backend/tests/conftest.py` + `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py`: 0 matches. **Scope discipline** вҖ” the enforcement is test-only code (zero prod churn); module-global audit: no new globals added, new hooks read pytest's item/marker surface (already-collected state), `UsageError` raised by value not stored.)*

### Y-prep.2 JIRA Webhook Secret Rotation API + UI жҢүйҲ• (#288)

Gerrit жңү `POST /runtime/git-forge/gerrit/webhook-secret/generate`пјҲ`integration.py:1518`пјүжҸҗдҫӣ one-time reveal зҡ„ rotate й«”й©—пјҢJIRA жІ’жңүе°ҚжҮү endpointгҖӮoperator rotate `jira_webhook_secret` зӣ®еүҚеҸӘиғҪиө° flat PUT /settingsпјҢжІ’жңүгҖҢеғ…жӯӨдёҖж¬ЎйЎҜзӨәжҳҺж–ҮгҖҚзҡ„ UXпјҢsecret жҙ©жјҸжҷӮдёҚе®№жҳ“еҝ«йҖҹијӘжӣҝгҖӮ

- [x] еҫҢз«Ҝпјҡ`POST /runtime/git-forge/jira/webhook-secret/generate`пјҢrequire_adminгҖӮmint 32-byte tokenгҖҒ`_apply_runtime_setting("jira_webhook_secret", new_secret)` иө° SharedKV йҸЎе°„гҖҒеӣһ `{secret, secret_masked, webhook_url, signature_header, signature_algorithm, note}` еҗҢ Gerrit зөҗж§ӢгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: new endpoint at `backend/routers/integration.py` вҖ” `@router.post("/git-forge/jira/webhook-secret/generate")` + `async def generate_jira_webhook_secret(request, _user=Depends(_au.require_admin))`. Mirrors the Gerrit generate endpoint (`integration.py:1540`) structurally вҖ” mints `_secrets.token_urlsafe(32)` (~43-char URL-safe base64, ~256 bits entropy), calls `_apply_runtime_setting("jira_webhook_secret", new_secret)` which writes both local `setattr(settings, ...)` AND Redis-backed `SharedKV` mirror (`jira_webhook_secret` was already in `_SHARED_KV_STR_FIELDS` line 118 вҖ” so peer workers pick up the rotated secret on their next `_overlay_runtime_settings()` call, no extra plumbing needed). Response shape is identical 7-field Gerrit contract: `{status, secret, secret_masked, webhook_url, signature_header, signature_algorithm, note}`. **Transport-accurate field values** (not blind copy): `signature_header="Authorization"` + `signature_algorithm="bearer-token"` вҖ” JIRA Cloud webhooks use `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header (current verifier at `backend/routers/webhooks.py:712`), not HMAC body signing like Gerrit; the field names are structural parity with Gerrit, the values reflect the real transport. **Minimal refactor**: `_derive_webhook_url(request)` gained an optional `path: str = _WEBHOOK_PATH` param вҖ” backward-compatible (existing 2 Gerrit callers pass no `path` arg, keep their default); the JIRA endpoint passes `_JIRA_WEBHOOK_PATH = "/api/v1/webhooks/jira"` explicitly. X-Forwarded-Proto / X-Forwarded-Host honoured so cloudflared-tunnel deploys return the externally-routable URL. **Step 1 audits**: (a) module-global state вҖ” none added; the existing SharedKV mirror (`jira_webhook_secret` already registered line 118) handles cross-worker coherence, qualifying under SOP audit rule #2 ("йҖҸйҒҺ PG / Redis еҚ”иӘҝ") вҖ” rotated secret on worker-A surfaces on worker-B via `_overlay_runtime_settings()` in its next settings read path. (b) read-after-write timing вҖ” no concurrency-model change; single POST вҶ’ `_apply_runtime_setting` вҶ’ return, no A-write-then-B-read race introduced. **Step 3 fingerprint grep**: `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` on modified file = 0 hits. **Live exercise** (not just import): handler invoked directly with mocked `Request` + forwarded headers вҶ’ 7-field response, `resp['secret']` 43-char, `resp['webhook_url']=='https://omni.example.com/api/v1/webhooks/jira'`, `resp['signature_header']=='Authorization'`, `resp['signature_algorithm']=='bearer-token'`, `settings.jira_webhook_secret == resp['secret']` verifies persistence into the SharedKV-mirrored field. **Regression check** neighbor `TestGerritWebhookInfo` 9/9 green in 2.89s against `omnisight-pg-test` вҖ” proves the `_derive_webhook_url` default-param refactor didn't break existing Gerrit URL derivation (honours X-Forwarded-*, default path `/api/v1/webhooks/gerrit` unchanged).)*
- [x] еүҚз«Ҝпјҡ`components/omnisight/integration-settings.tsx` JIRA SectionпјҲзңӢиө·дҫҶйӮ„еңЁ Notifications tabпјүеҠ дёҖйЎҶгҖҢROTATE WEBHOOK SECRETгҖҚжҢүйҲ•пјӣй»һдёӢеҺ» confirm dialog вҶ’ йЎҜзӨә one-time modalпјҲcopy-to-clipboardпјүвҶ’ й—ңй–үеҫҢеҸӘиғҪзңӢеҲ° masked previewпјҢе°ҚйҪҠ Gerrit Wizard Step 5 UXгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: new `JiraWebhookSecretRotateDialog` component in `components/omnisight/integration-settings.tsx` вҖ” four-phase state machine (`confirm` вҶ’ `rotating` вҶ’ `revealed` вҶ’ `error`). `confirm` phase surfaces a hardware-orange warning that rotation invalidates the old secret + Cancel/Rotate-Now buttons; `rotating` is a spinner; `revealed` shows plain `secret` in a `select-all` code block beside a Copy button + `webhook_url` + backend-provided `note`, with an emerald "SAVED & CLOSE" CTA; `error` surfaces the API message + Retry. **One-time-reveal safety:** while in `revealed` phase, the backdrop click is disabled (would otherwise silently destroy an unrecoverable value); only the deliberate Close / X / SAVED button dismisses. On dismiss, the masked preview (`вҖўвҖўвҖўвҖўabc4`) bubbles up via `onRotated` to parent `IntegrationSettings`, which stores it in `jiraLastRotatedMasked` and renders it inline beside the ROTATE button (`вҖўвҖўвҖў rotated вҖў вҖўвҖўвҖўвҖўabc4`) so the operator has a visual confirmation of the rotated state without exposing the plaintext again. Pre-rotate inline status falls back to `settingsData.webhooks.jira_secret` ("configured" or "not configured" вҖ” backend never returns a real mask via GET /settings, only the rotate response does; this is the same constraint the Gerrit wizard works around). **API client:** new `generateJiraWebhookSecret()` in `lib/api.ts` posting to `/runtime/git-forge/jira/webhook-secret/generate` вҖ” structural type-alias `JiraWebhookSecretRotateResult = GerritWebhookSecretRotateResult` since the backend intentionally returns the identical 7-field shape. **Button placement:** added to JIRA ISSUE TRACKING section (webhooks tab вҖ” task description hedged "looks still in Notifications tab"; actual tab is `webhooks` since `2026-04` consolidation) rather than the INBOUND WEBHOOK SECRETS Jira Secret password row, so the rotate UX sits with the rest of the JIRA knobs (URL/Token/Project) where an operator is already thinking about JIRA; the manual-paste password input stays in place for operators who pre-generate the secret out-of-band. **Type check:** `tsc --noEmit` produces 3 pre-existing errors on HEAD (GitForgeTokenTestResult index-signature miss at lines 3170/3217/3289) that are unrelated to this change; my changes introduce zero new TS errors. **Regression:** `vitest run test/components/integration-settings{,-sse}.test.tsx` вҶ’ 56/56 green in 4.10s вҖ” existing suite covers the surrounding modal contract and confirms no accidental breakage of settings / SSE / save / discard flows.)*
- [x] `test_integration_settings.py` еҠ  `test_jira_webhook_secret_rotate_one_time_reveal` + `test_jira_webhook_secret_requires_admin`гҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: new `TestJiraWebhookSecretRotate` class at `backend/tests/test_integration_settings.py` sits alongside `TestGerritWebhookInfo` in the same file. **Test 1 вҖ” `test_jira_webhook_secret_rotate_one_time_reveal`** pins the one-time-reveal contract end-to-end: POST returns the plain `secret` + 7-field paste-ready payload exactly once; `len(secret) >= 32` (token_urlsafe(32) ~43-char URL-safe base64, ~256 bits entropy); persists onto `settings.jira_webhook_secret`; mirrors into the SharedKV so peer workers converge (`captured["jira_webhook_secret"] == secret` assertion proves the `_apply_runtime_setting` mirror path actually fired вҖ” the cross-worker guarantee for the inbound `POST /api/v1/webhooks/jira` verifier on replica-B); `webhook_url` honours `X-Forwarded-Proto` / `X-Forwarded-Host` (cloudflared deploys); `signature_header=="Authorization"` + `signature_algorithm=="bearer-token"` (JIRA Bearer transport, not Gerrit HMAC вҖ” structural parity, accurate values); subsequent `GET /settings` surfaces only `webhooks.jira_secret=="configured"` вҖ” the plaintext does NOT appear anywhere in the response body (`secret not in info.text` belt-and-braces); two back-to-back POSTs yield different secrets and the later write wins, proving rotate-primitive semantics. **Test 2 вҖ” `test_jira_webhook_secret_requires_admin`** flips `OMNISIGHT_AUTH_MODE=strict` + pre-seeds a known `jira_webhook_secret`, then fires the POST without any session cookie: asserts `status_code in (401, 403)` AND that `settings.jira_webhook_secret` was NOT mutated (belt-and-braces: if a future refactor runs the handler body before the `require_admin` gate, the not-mutated check catches it even if the status code regresses). **Test-pollution audit**: the rotate handler writes to the Redis-backed `SharedKV` which normally persists across tests (leaking the rotated secret into `TestSettingsEndpoint::test_webhooks_block_reports_four_secret_states`'s `jira_secret==""` contract via the overlay). Fixed by `monkeypatch.setattr(_i._runtime_settings_kv, "set"|"get_all", ...)` вҖ” writes route into a test-local `captured` dict, overlay within the test still sees the write (so the cross-worker assertion + GET /settings "configured" assertion are both exercised) but the dict is dropped at fixture teardown so real SharedKV is never touched. Addresses the SOP Step-1 module-global-state audit rubric #2 ("coordinate via Redis") вҖ” this test isolates the write via monkeypatch rather than leaking it. Reproduced the leak first (`TestJiraWebhookSecretRotate::вҖҰrotateвҖҰ` followed by `TestSettingsEndpoint::test_webhooks_block_reports_four_secret_states` = 1 failed / 1 passed), then verified the fix (same pair = 2/2 green). **Regression run**: full `test_integration_settings.py` = 99/99 green in 16.72s (97 pre-existing + 2 new); focused rerun including `TestGerritWebhookInfo` + `TestSettingsEndpoint::test_webhooks_block_reports_four_secret_states` = 12/12 green in 3.43s вҖ” proves the new tests don't pollute the webhooks-block contract test or the Gerrit counterpart suite.)*
- [x] ж—ӢиҪүеҫҢе…¶д»– worker иҰҒиғҪз«ӢеҚіз”Ёж–° secret й©— HMACвҖ”вҖ”йҖҷеЎҠйқ зҸҫжңү SharedKV overlay е·Із¶“ coverпјҲ`_SHARED_KV_STR_FIELDS` жңү `jira_webhook_secret`пјүпјҢжё¬дёҖдёӢи·Ё worker зўәиӘҚз”ҹж•ҲгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: completed the SharedKV overlay loop on the READ side. Write side was already wired вҖ” `_apply_runtime_setting("jira_webhook_secret", ...)` in the rotate endpoint mirrors into the Redis-backed SharedKV (`jira_webhook_secret вҲҲ _SHARED_KV_STR_FIELDS` at line 118 of `backend/routers/integration.py`), so rotating on worker-A makes the new secret visible to all peers via Redis. But the inbound `POST /api/v1/webhooks/jira` verifier at `backend/routers/webhooks.py:704-731` read `settings.jira_webhook_secret` **directly** вҖ” no `_overlay_runtime_settings()` call вҖ” so worker-B's per-process singleton remained stale until some other request path (GET /runtime/settings, /runtime/providers) happened to trigger the overlay first. Under Caddy round-robin LB with 4 aggregate workers (2 replicas Г— OMNISIGHT_WORKERS=2), that's a non-deterministic "eventually consistent" window where JIRA's first few webhooks after rotate could 401 on any worker that hadn't received a settings read yet вҖ” the exact multi-worker shape the 2026-04-22 "GitHub/GitLab token ж¶ҲеӨұдәҶ" incident fixed at the settings-page level but left unfixed for inbound webhook verification. **Fix**: 1-line addition in `backend/routers/webhooks.py::jira_webhook` вҖ” `from backend.routers.integration import _overlay_runtime_settings; _overlay_runtime_settings()` executed BEFORE the `settings.jira_webhook_secret` read. Placement rationale: overlay before the 503 (empty secret) branch too, because worker-B booted from a `.env` with empty secret would 503 under the old code even though SharedKV holds the rotated value. Cost: single Redis HGETALL per inbound webhook (~1ms), guarded by try/except inside the overlay helper so Redis-down falls back to local settings rather than raising. Docstring expanded to document the write-side вҶ” read-side contract and why both halves are needed to close the cross-worker loop. **Test**: new `test_jira_webhook_secret_cross_worker_propagation` in `backend/tests/test_integration_settings.py::TestJiraWebhookSecretRotate` вҖ” 5-phase end-to-end simulation. Phase 1 POSTs to the rotate endpoint (simulated worker-A), captures the new secret + asserts both local settings and the SharedKV mirror hold it. Phase 2 uses `monkeypatch.setattr(settings, "jira_webhook_secret", "stale-from-env-boot")` to simulate worker-B's divergent in-memory state (the captured dict is the Redis stand-in shared by both simulated workers, matching prod's "SharedKV is the single source of truth across workers"). Phase 3 fires `POST /api/v1/webhooks/jira` with `Authorization: Bearer <new_secret>` + asserts 200 (not 401) вҖ” the critical cross-worker acceptance assertion; a regression where the overlay call is dropped from the webhook handler fails here instead of silently regressing inbound JIRA events in prod. Phase 3 also asserts the overlay's side-effect leaves `settings.jira_webhook_secret == new_secret` on the worker that received the webhook. Phase 4 fires the SAME webhook with the stale Bearer token + asserts 401 вҖ” proves the overlay is replacement, not additive, so the old secret is fully invalidated post-rotate. Phase 5 runs the rotate-again scenario: second rotate on simulated worker-A вҶ’ worker-B (again simulated stale) accepts the NEWER secret and rejects the previous one, locking rotate-primitive semantics end-to-end. The `captured` dict is scoped to the `monkeypatch` fixture so real SharedKV is never touched (same test-pollution guard pattern established by bullet #3). **Verification**: `TestJiraWebhookSecretRotate` 3/3 green in 2.50s against `omnisight-pg-test` (`OMNI_TEST_PG_URL=postgresql://omni_test:omni_test_pw@localhost:5434/omni_test`); full combined `test_webhooks.py + test_integration_settings.py` regression = 131/131 green in 25.20s вҖ” proves the overlay-call addition doesn't regress the 27 existing webhook tests (positive routing / negative routing / review-boundary / replication-targets-boundary / signature-verifier) nor the 100 existing integration-settings tests. **SOP Step 1 module-global audit** (rubric #2 вҖ” coordinate via Redis): the modification reads SharedKV (Redis-backed) via overlay and writes to per-worker `settings` singleton. All workers converge to the same value because they all read the same Redis hash; qualifies as rubric #2 "through Redis coordination". No new module-globals introduced; the existing `_runtime_settings_kv` singleton is the coordination anchor, already audited in prior bullets. **SOP Step 1 read-after-write timing**: no concurrency model change вҖ” single handler call runs overlay вҶ’ 503/401 check вҶ’ dispatch sequentially; no new A-write-then-B-read race. **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/routers/webhooks.py` + `backend/tests/test_integration_settings.py` = 0 hits. **Scope discipline**: only JIRA webhook handler touched; Gerrit/GitHub/GitLab webhooks have the same class of gap but are explicitly out of scope for Y-prep.2 вҖ” should be tracked as a follow-up task if the same class of silent rotate-lag bug is ever observed for those transports. **Note on "HMAC" in the task text**: JIRA Cloud uses Bearer-token auth (`Authorization: Bearer <secret>`), not HMAC body signing вҖ” the verifier at `webhooks.py:712` uses `_hmac.compare_digest` (timing-safe string compare) not `_hmac.new(...).hexdigest()`. The task description said "HMAC" loosely to mean "shared-secret inbound auth"; the test + fix cover the Bearer-token path which is what JIRA actually uses.)*

й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

### Y-prep.3 JIRA е…Ҙз«ҷ webhook дәӢд»¶и·Ҝз”ұеҷЁ (#289)

зҸҫеңЁ `webhooks.py:705-731` еҸӘ sync status еӣһ internal Task DBпјҲиӢҘжңү match зҡ„и©ұпјүпјҢжІ’жңүд»»дҪ• automation triggerгҖӮoperator еңЁ JIRA ticket з•ҷ comment жғіи§ёзҷј agentгҖҒstatus иҪү Done жғіи§ёзҷј artifact дёҠеӮігҖҒissue created жғіеҒҡ intakeвҖ”вҖ”йғҪжІ’иҫҰжі•гҖӮйҖҷйЎҶиЈңдёҖеҖӢеҸҜй…ҚзҪ®зҡ„дәӢд»¶ вҶ’ action и·Ҝз”ұгҖӮ

- [x] ж–° `backend/routers/webhooks.py::_on_jira_event()` dispatcherпјҢи§Јжһҗ JIRA webhook payload зҡ„ `webhookEvent` ж¬„дҪҚпјҲ`jira:issue_created` / `jira:issue_updated` / `comment_created` зӯүпјүгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: added `_on_jira_event(event)` dispatcher + three `_on_jira_*` handler stubs (`_on_jira_comment_created` / `_on_jira_issue_updated` / `_on_jira_issue_created`) to `backend/routers/webhooks.py`. Dispatcher wired into `jira_webhook` request path inside a try/except so dispatcher failure never blocks the existing status-sync path (both paths are independent вҖ” dispatcher fires for ALL events, status-sync only when a Task matches the issue key). Routes `comment_created` + `comment_updated` to the comment handler (so edited `/command` comments are re-evaluated); routes `jira:issue_updated` вҶ’ status-transition handler; routes `jira:issue_created` вҶ’ intake-label handler; unknown events log-and-return. Handler bodies are intentional stubs вҖ” the next Y-prep.3 bullet (`backend/jira_event_router.py`) fleshes out the 3 actions (CATC `/command` emit + artifact packaging on Done + `intent_bridge` intake). SOP Step 1: module-global audit clean (pure function, no module-level cache вҖ” each worker derives the same routing from the same payload, qualified answer #1); read-after-write timing clean (no concurrency model change вҖ” sequential dispatcher-then-status-sync). SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` on webhooks.py = 0 hits. Import sanity + 6-case runtime smoke (4 positive dispatch + unknown + missing-webhookEvent) all green. Full `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py` = 31/31 green in 8.29s against `omnisight-pg-test` вҖ” zero regression on existing Gerrit/Jira paths. Scope: dispatcher only; remaining Y-prep.3 bullets (jira_event_router module, audit events, config fields + UI, 3 handler tests, docs page) remain open and intentionally out of scope for this commit.)*
- [x] ж–° `backend/jira_event_router.py` е®ҡзҫ© event type вҶ’ handler жҳ е°„гҖӮдёүеҖӢ MVP handlerпјҡ
  - `comment_created` дё” comment body д»Ҙ `/<command>` й–Ӣй ӯ вҶ’ emit `jira_command` event еҲ° CATCпјҲO5 IntentSource е·Із¶“еҗғйҖҷеҖӢ topicпјүгҖӮ
  - `jira:issue_updated` дё” `status.to` и®ҠзӮәзү№е®ҡеҖјпјҲconfig whitelistпјҢdefault `["Done", "Closed"]`пјү вҶ’ trigger artifact packagingпјҲжІҝз”Ё Gerrit change-merged зҡ„ artifact pipelineпјүгҖӮ
  - `jira:issue_created` иӢҘ issue жңүзү№е®ҡ labelпјҲconfig `OMNISIGHT_JIRA_INTAKE_LABEL`пјҢdefault `omnisight-intake`пјү вҶ’ call intent_bridge е»әз«Ӣ CATC taskгҖӮ *(done 2026-04-24: new module `backend/jira_event_router.py` exposes `ROUTES` (str вҶ’ coro mapping) + `route(webhook_event, event)` + three async handlers `handle_comment_created` / `handle_issue_updated` / `handle_issue_created`. The `webhooks.py` stubs from the previous bullet now delegate via lazy import вҖ” dispatcher and action logic stay in separate files. Handler 1 (comment): `_extract_command()` parses `<prefix><word>[ args]` (default prefix `/`, configurable via `OMNISIGHT_JIRA_COMMAND_PREFIX`); positive path publishes `jira_command` on `events.bus` with `{issue_key, command, args, author, comment_id}` and writes a `jira.command_received` audit row; bare `/`, no prefix, and empty body all return `{status: ignored, reason: no_command_prefix}`. Handler 2 (status): `_extract_status_transition()` walks `changelog.items[]` for `field == "status"`; positive path spawns `_package_merged_artifacts(f"jira:{issue_key}", summary)` via `asyncio.create_task` (reuses Gerrit change-merged tarball pipeline) and writes `jira.status_transitioned` audit row; whitelist pulled from `OMNISIGHT_JIRA_DONE_STATUSES` CSV (default `Done,Closed`); non-whitelisted status returns `{status: ignored, reason: status_not_whitelisted}`. Handler 3 (intake): reads `issue.fields.labels[]`, matches against `OMNISIGHT_JIRA_INTAKE_LABEL` (default `omnisight-intake`); positive path calls `intent_bridge.on_intake_queued(parent=issue_key, vendor="jira", cards_with_task_ids=[], dag_id=...)` and writes `jira.intake_triggered` audit row; missing label returns `{status: ignored, reason: missing_intake_label}`. All three audit calls and the bus.publish are best-effort wrapped вҖ” a downed audit DB or bus never breaks the dispatcher path. SOP Step 1: module-global state audit clean (qualified answer #1 вҖ” `ROUTES` is an immutable mapping of Python constants, identical across uvicorn workers; no module-level cache; configuration re-read per-call from env so a rotate is visible immediately). Read-after-write timing audit clean (handlers run sequentially within `_on_jira_event`; no parallelism introduced; the `_package_merged_artifacts` background task is fire-and-forget and shares no writer with the sync path). SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` on `jira_event_router.py` + `webhooks.py` = 0 hits. Runtime smoke: 9-case dispatch (3 positive + 6 negative across all 3 handlers, plus unknown event + dispatcher integration) all green; full `backend/tests/test_webhooks.py` = 31/31 green in 9.99s against `omnisight-pg-test` DSN вҖ” zero regression on existing Gerrit/Jira routing/contract tests. Scope: handlers + routing table only; remaining Y-prep.3 bullets (audit events richer context, config promotion to `_SHARED_KV_STR_FIELDS` + Notifications-tab UI, dedicated 3-handler unit tests, `docs/integrations/jira-automation.md` page) remain open and intentionally out of scope for this commit. Audit rows are already emitted by this commit вҖ” the next bullet refines the per-tenant context wiring once Y4 lands real tenants.)*

This is the single-line Y4 swap-point вҖ” when real per-tenant JIRA routing lands, this one line becomes `set_tenant_id(derive_tenant_from_event(event))`. (2) `backend/jira_event_router.py::_audit` replaces the hardcoded `_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID` in the actor string with `current_tenant_id() or _DEFAULT_TENANT_ID` so audit rows self-document `actor=jira_event_router/<live-tenant>` without a schema change, and also stamps `after.tenant_id = <live-tenant>` so operators can grep by it. The core `audit.log` tenant plumbing (`tenant_insert_value()` вҶ’ `audit_log.tenant_id`) was already correct; the change here simply makes the intent and the audit-row-visible tenant id match on the wire. Both promoted into `_SHARED_KV_STR_FIELDS` in `backend/routers/integration.py` so a Notifications-tab edit on worker-A is mirrored via Redis SharedKV and picked up on workers B/C/D's next `_overlay_runtime_settings()` вҖ” same cross-worker-coherence guarantee as the rest of the JIRA family (`jira_webhook_secret`, `notification_jira_token`). Both also added to `_UPDATABLE_FIELDS` so the flat `PUT /runtime/settings` path accepts them, and exposed unmasked in the GET /runtime/settings response under `jira.intake_label` / `jira.done_statuses` (they're routing knobs, not credentials вҖ” masking would defeat the UI). `backend/jira_event_router.py::_intake_label()` and `_done_statuses()` now resolve in `settings вҶ’ env вҶ’ built-in default` precedence so a Notifications-tab save reconfigures the router on the same request without restart while keeping the existing `OMNISIGHT_JIRA_INTAKE_LABEL` / `OMNISIGHT_JIRA_DONE_STATUSES` env-var path working for headless deploys (and the env value seen during the previous bullet's tests still flows through). Module docstring updated to spell out the new precedence + cross-worker mirror so the next reader doesn't have to chase 3 files. All four side-effect seams (`_audit` / `_publish_bus` / `_package_merged_artifacts` / `intent_bridge.on_intake_queued`) are stubbed so the tests stay hermetic вҖ” no DB / Redis / pool dependency, runs in 0.22s standalone. AND (labels = "omnisight-intake" OR status changed TO ("Done","Closed") OR issueFunction in commented(...))` that mirrors the three handler triggers so JIRA evaluates the filter per-event and skips POST on no-match (single biggest bandwidth win), (b) the **only-tick-these-4-events** allowlist вҖ” `Issue created` / `Issue updated` / `Comment created` / `Comment edited` вҖ” matching `ROUTES.keys()` exactly, with explicit "leave all other event boxes UNTICKED" as the no-code event-allowlist firewall, (c) JIRA Server/DC paragraph noting `comment_updated` ticks directly on SDC (Cloud rolls into "Comment вҶ’ edited"), (d) verify-loop via SQL `SELECT id, action, entity_id, after_json::jsonb -> 'tenant_id' FROM audit_log WHERE action LIKE 'jira.%'` so operators can confirm the filter is producing exactly one audit row per real trigger; В§6 curl smoke recipe that hits `/api/v1/webhooks/jira` with the smallest valid `comment_created` body + the verify SQL to inspect the resulting audit row; В§7 troubleshooting matrix вҖ” 7 rows pairing observed symptom with root-cause diagnosis (401 / 503 / Issue-body-toggle / leading-whitespace / done-status CSV override / case-sensitive label spelling / duplicate webhook entries) вҖ” each row anchored to a real failure mode pulled from the dispatcher / handler return shapes, no padding rows; В§8 reference table cross-links to `webhooks.py:761` dispatcher / `jira_event_router.py:414` ROUTES / 3 test files (handlers / tenant_audit / webhooks) / `integration-settings.tsx` UI вҖ” single source of forward references for an operator who needs to read the source. Style choice вҖ” flat operator-facing prose + tables + JSON / SQL / JQL code blocks, no internal-design rationale; the design rationale lives in HANDOFF entries + module docstrings, this page is "what to do, what to expect, where to look when broken". Scope вҖ” single new file under `docs/integrations/` (directory created in this commit, no prior siblings вҖ” the directory itself is the deliverable's container; future per-system pages can sit alongside, e.g. `docs/integrations/gerrit-automation.md` once Y-prep.1 needs a parallel operator page). 0 prod code / 0 test / 0 migration / 0 env knob / 0 image / 0 schema; pure operator documentation. ## рҹ…© Priority Z вҖ” LLM Provider ObservabilityпјҲйӨҳйЎҚ / rate-limit / зңҹеҜҰз”ЁйҮҸеҸҜиҰ–еҢ–пјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҲ2026-04-22 operator auditпјүпјҡ9 е®¶ LLM provider е…ЁйғЁеҸӘеҒҡ**жң¬ең° token зөұиЁҲ**вҖ”вҖ”`backend/agents/llm.py:32-60` зҡ„ `TokenTrackingCallback` еҸӘжҠ“ response body зҡ„ `usage` ж¬„дҪҚпјҢе®Ңе…ЁжІ’и®Җ HTTP response header зҡ„ `x-ratelimit-*`пјӣ`backend/routers/system.py:1091-1100` зҡ„жҲҗжң¬иЎЁзЎ¬еҜ« 8 еҖӢ modelгҖҒе…¶д»– fallback (1.0, 3.0) USD/MпјҢж”№ rate иҰҒж”№ code йҮҚйғЁзҪІпјӣжІ’жңүд»»дҪ• code жү“йҒҺ provider зҡ„ balance APIпјӣUI `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` еҸӘйЎҜзӨәгҖҢжң¬ең°зҙҜиЁҲиҠұиІ»гҖҚиҖҢдёҚзҹҘйҒ“гҖҢprovider йӮЈйӮҠйӮ„жңүеӨҡе°‘ creditгҖҚгҖӮoperator е•Ҹ "API KEY йғҪдёІдәҶпјҢиғҪзңӢйӨҳйЎҚе—Һпјҹ"пјҢзӯ”жЎҲжҳҜдёҚиЎҢвҖ”вҖ”еҸӘиғҪзңӢиҮӘе·ұиҠұдәҶеӨҡе°‘гҖӮ
>
> **е•ҶжҘӯйӮҸијҜ**пјҡйҒӢзҮҹиҰҒеңЁ "еҝ«зҮ’е®Ң" д№ӢеүҚжұәе®ҡ "еҠ еҖјйӮ„жҳҜиҪүеӮҷжҸҙ provider"пјҢжІ’жңү balance / rate-limit еҸҜиҰ–еҢ–е°ұж°ёйҒ жҳҜжү“еҲ° 429 / 402 жүҚзҹҘйҒ“гҖӮ9 е®¶д№Ӣдёӯ**еҸӘжңү DeepSeek + OpenRouter** жңүе…¬й–Ӣзҡ„ balance API еҸҜд»Ҙз”Ё API key жҹҘпјҲAnthropic / xAI / Groq / Together е®ҳж–№з„ЎпјӣOpenAI / Google йңҖ session cookie жҲ– GCP иӘҚиӯүпјҢдёҚеҜҰз”Ёпјүпјӣrate-limit header еүҮжҳҜ**е№ҫд№ҺжҜҸе®¶йғҪеӣһ**пјҲAnthropic / OpenAI / Google / Groq йғҪжңүпјүпјҢеҸӘжҳҜжҲ‘еҖ‘дёҹжҺүжІ’и§ЈжһҗгҖӮ
>
> **зҜ„еңҚзҙ…з·ҡ**пјҡеҸӘеҒҡ "жҠҠиғҪжӢҝзҡ„иіҮиЁҠж’Ҳиө·дҫҶеұ•зӨә"пјҢдёҚеҒҡ dashboard scraping / дёҚеҒҡ session-cookie з«ҠеҸ– / дёҚеҒҡй җжё¬жҖ§иҮӘеӢ•еҠ еҖј / дёҚеҒҡи·Ё provider arbitrage иҮӘеӢ•еҲҮжҸӣгҖӮ
>
> **зӣёдҫқ**пјҡвң… йӣ¶гҖӮ`SharedKV`пјҲ`backend/shared_state.py:150`пјүе·ІжңүгҖҒ`TokenTrackingCallback`пјҲ`backend/agents/llm.py:32-60`пјүе·ІжңүгҖҒtoken-usage-stats UI е·ІжңүгҖҒ`/providers` endpoint е·ІжңүгҖӮжүҖжңү step йғҪжҳҜж“ҙе……ж—ўжңү codeгҖҒдёҚеӢ• schemaгҖҒдёҚеӢ• authгҖҒдёҚеӢ• tenant modelгҖӮ
>
> **зӮәд»Җйәје„Әе…ҲзҙҡеҫҖеүҚжҺЁ**пјҡ

### Z.1 Rate-limit header ж“·еҸ– + SharedKV е„Іеӯҳ (#290)

`TokenTrackingCallback` зӣ®еүҚеҸӘзңӢ bodyпјҢжҠҠ response header зҡ„ rate-limit иіҮиЁҠж•ҙеҖӢдёҹжҺүгҖӮиЈңдёҖдёӢе°ұжҜҸе®¶йғҪеҸ—зӣҠгҖӮ

- [x] ж”№ `backend/agents/llm.py:TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_end()` и®Җ response object зҡ„ header dictпјҲLangChain еә•еұӨ `openai.AsyncOpenAI` / `anthropic.AsyncAnthropic` йғҪжңүжӣқ `.response.headers`пјүгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24: `_extract_response_headers(response)` static method walks 5 candidate paths (`llm_output['headers']` / `llm_output['response_headers']` / `response_metadata['headers']` / flattened `response_metadata` with `x-ratelimit-*|anthropic-ratelimit-*|retry-after` keys / `generation_info['headers']`) and returns the first non-empty hit. Snapshot lives on `self.last_response_headers` (init `{}`, overwritten on every on_llm_end). Missing/unparseable вҶ’ `{}` + debug log, never raises. Downstream Z.1 checkboxes (provider name mapping + SharedKV write) read this attribute. Tests: `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_header_extract.py` 24 cases вҖ” per-path surface + priority order + empty/malformed degradation + Ollama-shape вҶ’ `{}` + on_llm_end overwrite + inner-try/except graceful-drift. Normalise mapping table, SharedKV("provider_ratelimit") write, TTL = 60s, and end-to-end 4-provider header normalisation tests belong to the remaining Z.1 checkboxes and are NOT delivered by this commit. -->
- [x] жҜҸ provider дёҖеҖӢ header name mapping tableпјҲ`_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS`пјүпјҢжҠҠдёҚеҗҢеҗҚеӯ—пјҲ`anthropic-ratelimit-requests-remaining` / `x-ratelimit-remaining-requests` / `x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens`пјүиҰҸж•ҙеҢ–жҲҗзөұдёҖ dictпјҡ`{remaining_requests, remaining_tokens, reset_at_ts, retry_after_s}`. <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.1-checkbox-2 shipped: module-const `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` in `backend/agents/llm.py` covers the seven rate-limited providers (Anthropic native + six OpenAI-compatible family members: OpenAI / xAI / Groq / DeepSeek / Together / OpenRouter). Ollama + Google Gemini deliberately absent вҖ” `_normalize_ratelimit_headers` returns `{}` for them so the downstream SharedKV write (next checkbox) short-circuits on truthiness. Three parser helpers handle the shape zoo: `_parse_reset_value` (ISO 8601 absolute / OpenAI duration `"12ms"|"1s"|"1m30s"` / bare numeric fallback), `_parse_retry_after_seconds` (integer + HTTP-date RFC 9110 В§10.2.3), `_parse_int_or_none` (preserves negative remaining as an upstream-drift signal, coerces `"42.0"` float-shape). Unified output dict: `{remaining_requests:int|None, remaining_tokens:int|None, reset_at_ts:float|None (unix epoch), retry_after_s:float|None}`; case-insensitive header lookup defends against `httpx.Headers` mixed-case drift; missing fields degrade to `None` (NULL-vs-genuine-zero); all-`None` collapses to `{}` so no-ratelimit-headers response is indistinguishable from unknown-provider downstream. `on_llm_end` writes `self.last_ratelimit_state` alongside the existing `self.last_response_headers` under an independent try/except вҖ” normalise bug can't unseat the raw capture and vice-versa. Tests: `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_header_normalize.py` 72 cases вҖ” mapping-table coverage guard (all 7 providers, 4 unified keys each) + per-parser unit matrix + Anthropic full/partial normalise + OpenAI-family parametrised normalise Г— 6 providers + 9 edge cases (unknown provider / None / empty / non-dict / case-drift / malformed field) + 4 on_llm_end wiring cases (initial state / full capture / Ollama skip / normalise-failure graceful). Also fixed pre-existing N4 firewall drift: re-exported `ChatGeneration` from `backend.llm_adapter` + routed both header-related test files through the adapter (the extract test from checkbox 1 had the same violation). Module-global audit (SOP Step 1): `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` is a module-const dict literal (answer #1 "дёҚе…ұдә«пјҢеӣ зӮәжҜҸ worker еҫһеҗҢжЁЈдҫҶжәҗжҺЁе°ҺеҮәеҗҢжЁЈзҡ„еҖј"). `TokenTrackingCallback.last_ratelimit_state` is per-instance вҖ” the callback wraps a single LangChain request and has no shared state. Zero new cross-worker coordination surface. SharedKV("provider_ratelimit") write + 60s TTL + four-provider end-to-end test belong to the remaining Z.1 checkboxes and are NOT delivered by this commit. -->
- [x] еӯҳйҖІ `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")`пјҢkey = provider nameпјҲдёҚеҠ  tenant / user з¶ӯеәҰвҖ”вҖ”йҖҷжҳҜж•ҙеҖӢеҜҰдҫӢзҡ„ rate-limit зӢҖж…ӢпјҢжүҖжңү tenant е…ұдә«еҗҢдёҖжҠҠ API keyпјҢиіҮиЁҠ scope зӣёеҗҢпјүгҖӮTTL = 60sпјҲrate-limit жҜҸеҲҶйҗҳ resetпјҢд№…дәҶиіҮиЁҠе°ұйҒҺжңҹпјүгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.1-checkbox-3 shipped: (A) `backend/shared_state.py::SharedKV` gains three public methods вҖ” `set_with_ttl(field, value, ttl_seconds, *, now=None)` / `get_with_ttl(field, *, now=None)` / `get_all_with_ttl(*, now=None)` вҖ” that wrap any JSON-serialisable payload in a `{_data, _expires_at}` envelope and lazy-prune expired/malformed entries on read. Chose embedded-expiry over `r.expire(hash_key)` (would cross-contaminate per-field TTLs вҖ” touching `openai` would refresh `anthropic`'s clock) and over Redis 7.4 `HEXPIRE` (not guaranteed in production). Injectable `now` kwarg keeps tests free of `time.time` monkey-patching. Zero-or-negative TTL raises `ValueError` вҖ” caller bug, not a runtime state. Store is self-healing: legacy plain-`set` values, non-JSON strings, and envelope-less dicts all get deleted on first `get_with_ttl` pass. (B) `backend/agents/llm.py` grows module constants `_RATELIMIT_TTL_SECONDS = 60.0`, `_RATELIMIT_KV_NAMESPACE = "provider_ratelimit"`, and lazy singleton `_get_ratelimit_kv()` (cache the `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` instance so `shared_state.reset_for_tests` can re-open the underlying Redis client without us holding a stale reference). `TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_end` gains a third try/except (siblings of the raw-headers-extract + normalise blocks): if `self.provider and self.last_ratelimit_state`, mirror the normalised dict into `_get_ratelimit_kv().set_with_ttl(self.provider, state, 60.0)`. Empty-state and provider=None turns are silent no-ops вҖ” writing `{}` under a provider key would overwrite a genuine prior snapshot from a sibling turn with stale "no data", strictly worse than letting the existing entry age out. KV-side exception (Redis drop, simulated blowup) degrades to `logger.debug` вҖ” LLM turn completes, dashboard just misses one update. (C) Module-global audit (SOP Step 1, 2026-04-21 rule): `_RATELIMIT_TTL_SECONDS` + `_RATELIMIT_KV_NAMESPACE` are module-const literals (answer #1 "дёҚе…ұдә«пјҢеӣ зӮәжҜҸ worker еҫһеҗҢжЁЈдҫҶжәҗжҺЁе°ҺеҮәеҗҢжЁЈзҡ„еҖј"). `_ratelimit_kv_singleton` is a per-worker lazy-init handle to `SharedKV`, itself Redis-backed when `OMNISIGHT_REDIS_URL` is set (answer #2 "йҖҸйҒҺ Redis еҚ”иӘҝ" вҖ” one hash shared across uvicorn workers + replicas) with in-memory fallback for single-worker dev (answer #3 "ж•…ж„ҸжҜҸ worker зҚЁз«Ӣ"). Zero new cross-worker coordination surface beyond what `SharedKV` already guarantees вҖ” matches the `session_presence = SessionPresence()` precedent at `shared_state.py:345`. Tests: `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_kv_write.py` 22 cases вҖ” SharedKV TTL unit matrix (round-trip / scalar payloads / absent field / expired lazy-prune / per-field independence regression / zero+negative TTL rejection / malformed legacy entries / overwrite refreshes own TTL) + `get_all_with_ttl` filter + malformed-pruning + on_llm_end wiring Г— 4 providers (anthropic native + 4 OpenAI-family вҖ” openai/xai/groq/deepseek coexisting) + 3 skip contracts (ollama unmapped / provider=None / empty state preserves prior snapshot) + 60s-TTL end-to-end (frozen clock shows live at t+30s, pruned at t+61s) + SharedKV-explodes debug-log-only degradation + module-const + singleton idempotence locks. All 22 pass; pre-existing 155 Z.1 tests + session_presence + token_usage_shared + turn_boundary/turn_metrics still green. Runtime smoke: `python3 -c 'import ...'` exercises full wiring, observes `{'remaining_requests': 47, 'remaining_tokens': 199876, 'reset_at_ts': 1777035600.0, 'retry_after_s': 0.0}` under `provider_ratelimit/anthropic`. Remaining Z.1 checkboxes (graceful-fallback boundaries test + four-provider mock end-to-end normalise test) belong to the next two rows; this commit delivers ONLY the SharedKV persistence + TTL. -->

Picked Anthropic (native `anthropic-ratelimit-*` + ISO 8601 absolute reset), OpenAI (OpenAI-family `x-ratelimit-*` + duration-string reset `"14ms"` / `"8.64s"`), DeepSeek (`x-ratelimit-*` + bare numeric-seconds reset вҖ” tests the `_parse_reset_value` fall-through past ISO hint + duration grammar), and OpenRouter (`x-ratelimit-*` + minute-duration `"1m"` reset вҖ” per-minute free-tier bucket shape). Header payloads mirror each vendor's public API вҖ” not synthetic minimal sets вҖ” so a real-world regression (e.g. vendor adds/renames a header) would fail the test. Classes: (A) `TestFourProviderNormalise` Г— 4 вҖ” parametrised realistic-headers вҶ’ unified dict assertion with per-field equality for `remaining_requests` / `remaining_tokens` / `retry_after_s` + `reset_at_ts is not None and isinstance(float)` (duration-string shapes anchor on wall-clock `time.time()` so equality would be flaky); (B) `TestFourProviderSharedKVWrite` Г— 4 вҖ” parametrised SharedKV byte-for-byte mirror check (`stored == cb.last_ratelimit_state`) + no-cross-provider-leakage; (C) `TestFourProviderCoexistence` Г— 2 вҖ” four sequential turns вҶ’ four independent keys (core Z.2/Z.4 dashboard invariant) + per-field TTL independence across providers (Anthropic@t=1000 / OpenAI@t=1050 read at t=1061 вҶ’ only OpenAI survives); (D) `TestFourProviderTTLExpiry` Г— 5 вҖ” parametrised 60s TTL expiry per provider (live at t+30s / pruned at t+61s / underlying hash row pruned-not-masked) + boundary precision lock (live at t+59.999 / pruned at t+60.001 вҖ” half-open interval contract of the envelope `_expires_at` field) + `_RATELIMIT_TTL_SECONDS == 60.0` module-const check; (E) `TestUnknownProviderSkipsEndToEnd` Г— 3 вҖ” Ollama turn between live Anthropic + OpenAI preserves both siblings / Gemini with spurious OpenAI-shape headers (proxy rewrite case) still skipped (allow-list is structural not content-based) / Ollama as very first turn leaves KV empty (deployed-inactive initial state). No production code modified вҖ” this is a test-only contract-lock on top of checkboxes 1-4. Module-global audit (SOP Step 1, 2026-04-21): zero new module-global state; fixture `fresh_kv` cycles the existing `_ratelimit_kv_singleton` the same way `test_ratelimit_kv_write.py` + `test_ratelimit_boundaries.py` do. ### Z.2 Balance fetch вҖ” DeepSeek + OpenRouter + fallback иЁӯиЁҲ (#291)

е…©е®¶жңүе…¬й–Ӣ balance APIпјҢзӣҙжҺҘжҺҘгҖӮе…¶д»–е®¶жІ’ API зҡ„дёҚ fake ж•ёеӯ—гҖҒйЎҜзӨә "unsupported"гҖӮ

- [x] ж–° `backend/llm_balance.py`пјҡжҜҸе®¶дёҖеҖӢ `async fetch_balance_<provider>(api_key) -> BalanceInfo | None`пјҢеӣһ `{currency, balance_remaining, granted_total, usage_total, last_refreshed_at, raw}`гҖӮ
  - DeepSeekпјҡ`GET https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance`пјҢheader `Authorization: Bearer {key}`пјҢеӣһ `{balance_infos: [{total_balance, granted_balance, topped_up_balance, currency}]}`.
  - OpenRouterпјҡ`GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/auth/key`пјҢheader еҗҢдёҠпјҢеӣһ `{data: {label, usage, limit, limit_remaining, is_free_tier, rate_limit}}`.
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-1 shipped: `backend/llm_balance.py` exports `fetch_balance_deepseek(api_key, *, timeout=10.0, client_factory=None, now=None)` + `fetch_balance_openrouter(...)` returning a `BalanceInfo` TypedDict (`currency`, `balance_remaining`, `granted_total`, `usage_total`, `last_refreshed_at`, `raw`) вҖ” `total=False` so per-provider field omissions stay typed. Shared `_http_get_json` helper opens a fresh `httpx.AsyncClient` per call (matches `backend/cms/sanity.py:110` + sentry mobile pattern вҖ” once-per-10-min cadence does not warrant a global client lifecycle), sends `Authorization: Bearer {key}` + `Accept: application/json`, maps responses three ways: `{401, 403}` вҶ’ `None` (cache layer must not store auth-fail envelopes вҖ” operator may rotate the key and we want the next refresh to pick it up cleanly), `>= 500` + `>= 400 (non-auth)` + transport errors + non-JSON вҶ’ `BalanceFetchError(provider, reason)` (cache layer keeps showing prior snapshot with `stale_since`), `2xx` вҶ’ parsed JSON. Empty `api_key` short-circuits to `None` without any HTTP call (defence against the resolver chain handing the fetcher an empty string for keyless providers like Ollama if someone wires the registry wrong later). DeepSeek normaliser picks `balance_infos[0]` (multi-currency rare, single-key cache cannot represent two simultaneously) but preserves the full vendor list under `raw`; sets `usage_total=None` (DeepSeek does not report cumulative usage вҖ” fabricating a 0 would be misleading). OpenRouter normaliser maps `data.{limit_remaining вҶ’ balance_remaining, limit вҶ’ granted_total, usage вҶ’ usage_total}` with a `limit - usage` fallback floored at 0.0 when `limit_remaining` is omitted (older API + future schema-drift insurance); currency hard-coded to `"USD"` per OpenRouter's pricing model. `_coerce_float` tolerates DeepSeek's stringified `"0.85"` shape + None-or-unparseable degrades to `None` rather than crashing. Module registry `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS = {"deepseek": fetch_balance_deepseek, "openrouter": fetch_balance_openrouter}` + `is_balance_supported(name)` predicate so the upcoming `/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` endpoint can short-circuit unsupported providers without threading a parallel allow-list. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero module-global mutable state вҖ” fetchers are pure functions of (api_key, vendor response); registry is a module-const dict literal (rubric #1 "жҜҸ worker еҫһеҗҢжЁЈдҫҶжәҗжҺЁе°Һ"). The cache + cross-worker coordination work belongs to the next Z.2 checkbox (background task + `SharedKV("provider_balance")` write). **Tests**: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_fetchers.py` 46 cases (4 classes) вҖ” registry shape + happy-path normalise per provider + bearer header + URL contract lock + auth-fail Г— {401, 403} вҶ’ None + 5xx Г— {500, 502, 503, 504} вҶ’ BalanceFetchError + transport error вҶ’ BalanceFetchError + 4xx-not-auth (429) вҶ’ raises + non-JSON вҶ’ raises + DeepSeek multi-currency picks-first-preserves-raw + DeepSeek string amount coercion + DeepSeek unparseable-amount-degrades-to-None + DeepSeek malformed body (missing balance_infos / empty list) + OpenRouter limit_remaining derivation + unlimited account stays None + overshoot floored at 0 + missing data envelope + empty api_key short-circuits without HTTP for both providers. All 46 pass; pre-existing `test_llm_credentials_crud.py` still green (8 pass + 23 PG-only skips). Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean. Remaining Z.2 checkboxes (background refresh task + SharedKV write + endpoints + boundary contract + integration tests) are NOT delivered by this commit. -->
