Next gate: deployed-inactive вҖ” waits on Z.2 checkbox 2 (background refresh + SharedKV write) and checkbox 3 (`/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` endpoint) to actually surface balances to the dashboard. -->
- [x] иғҢжҷҜ async taskпјҲlifespan е•ҹеӢ•пјүжҜҸ 10 min refresh дёҖж¬ЎпјҢзөҗжһңеЎһ `SharedKV("provider_balance")`, key = provider name. еӨұж•—йҮҚи©Ұ exponential backoff дёҠйҷҗ 1 е°ҸжҷӮгҖӮ
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-2 shipped: `backend/llm_balance_refresher.py` exports `run_refresh_loop(*, interval_s=None)` (singleton background coroutine matching the Phase-52 DLQ / Phase-63-E memory-decay convention вҖ” `_LOOP_RUNNING` flag + cancellation-aware `asyncio.sleep` + `finally`-reset) + `refresh_once(*, state, base_interval_s, now=None, fetchers=None, key_resolver=None, kv=None)` (pure one-tick logic, all side-effect entrypoints injectable for unit tests). Wired into `backend/main.py:246` lifespan right after `memory_decay.run_decay_loop()` as `balance_task = asyncio.create_task(_lbr.run_refresh_loop())` and added to the shutdown-cancel tuple at line 287. Constants: `DEFAULT_INTERVAL_S=600.0` (10 min), `MAX_BACKOFF_S=3600.0` (1 h cap per checkbox spec), `BALANCE_NAMESPACE="provider_balance"` (exported so the upcoming `/runtime/providers/*/balance` endpoint can `from backend.llm_balance_refresher import BALANCE_NAMESPACE` rather than re-declare the string). Per-tick flow: iterate `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` вҶ’ look up per-provider `_ProviderBackoff` state (dataclass with `consecutive_failures` + `next_attempt_at`) вҶ’ skip if within backoff window (emit `"backoff"` outcome) вҶ’ resolve api_key via Settings scalar read (`settings.{provider}_api_key` вҖ” refresher runs in system scope so we avoid the tenant-contextvar + pool round-trip, and bypass `get_llm_credential_sync`'s per-worker deprecation-warn) вҶ’ if no key, skip silently with `"no_key"` outcome (no HTTP, no backoff advance вҖ” next tick re-evaluates so operator setting the key mid-run is picked up immediately) вҶ’ call fetcher вҶ’ on `BalanceFetchError` bump `consecutive_failures` and set `next_attempt_at = now + min(base Г— 2^failures, 3600s)`, cache untouched (prior snapshot keeps serving, upcoming endpoint surfaces `stale_since`) вҶ’ on `None` (401/403) same backoff treatment, cache untouched (operator may rotate the key; don't stamp over with auth_failed envelope) вҶ’ on unexpected exception same fetch_error path (defensive вҖ” one bad vendor schema cannot kill the loop) вҶ’ on success `SharedKV("provider_balance").set(provider, json.dumps(BalanceInfo))` and reset backoff to `now + base`. Boot policy: one immediate `refresh_once` call before the first sleep so dashboards are not blank for the first 10 min post-startup; initial-tick exceptions logged+swallowed so a bad-key misconfig doesn't crash lifespan. Backoff formula locked: delays after N failures = [1200, 2400, 3600 (cap), 3600, ...] seconds вҖ” verified in `TestProviderBackoff::test_record_failure_doubles_then_caps`. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: `_LOOP_RUNNING` (rubric #1 "жҜҸ worker еҫһеҗҢжЁЈдҫҶжәҗжҺЁе°Һ" вҖ” flag presence identical per worker). Per-provider backoff state is **not** module-global on purpose вҖ” held in the `state` dict owned by `run_refresh_loop`'s stack frame (rubric #3 "ж•…ж„ҸжҜҸ worker зҚЁз«Ӣ"); in N-worker deployments each worker fetches independently and `SharedKV.set` is the cross-worker coordination point (last-writer-wins is fine because all writers produce the same logical datum from the same vendor). Vendor API traffic scales linearly with worker count (~NГ—12 req/hour/provider at 2-worker Г— 2-provider config вҖ” well under every documented rate limit). **Read-after-write audit**: only write is `SharedKV.set` which composes atomically (Redis HSET atomic; in-memory fallback uses `threading.Lock`). **Tests**: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_refresher.py` 29 cases (9 classes) вҖ” happy path writes SharedKV with correct envelope + backoff resets to base on success + JSON round-trippable for upcoming endpoint + missing key skips silently without fetch or backoff + empty string treated as missing + fetch error does NOT overwrite prior cache + fetch error bumps next_attempt to baseГ—2 + repeated failures double delay [1200, 2400, 3600, 3600] with cap + non-BalanceFetchError exception also caught + auth-fail does not overwrite cache + auth-fail applies same exponential backoff + within-backoff-window skips fetch (no resolver/fetcher call) + past-backoff-window retries + providers independent (one fails other succeeds) + each provider resolves own key + `_ProviderBackoff.reset` clears counters + `record_failure` cap math exact + serialiser preserves None numeric + serialiser degrades non-JSON via `default=str` + resolver reads settings scalar + empty/whitespace/unknown-provider вҶ’ None + `run_refresh_loop` exits cleanly on cancel with `_LOOP_RUNNING=False` + second-start singleton guard short-circuits + initial tick runs before first sleep + initial-tick exception does not crash loop + default fetcher set is SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS (regression guard against silently dropping a provider). All 29 pass; pre-existing 46 `test_llm_balance_fetchers.py` cases still green (75 combined). Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean. `backend.main` imports clean with the new lifespan wiring. Remaining Z.2 checkboxes (endpoints + boundary contract + integration tests) not delivered by this commit. -->

Next gate: deployed-inactive вҖ” waits on Z.2 checkbox 3 (`/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` endpoint) to surface the refresher's output to the dashboard + a live restart of the backend image with Settings scalars populated for at least one supported provider. -->

- [x] ж–° `GET /runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` endpointпјҢеӣһеҝ«еҸ–еҖјпјҲе„Әе…Ҳ SharedKVпјүпјӣSharedKV з©ә вҶ’ дё»еӢ•и§ёзҷјдёҖж¬Ў fetchгҖӮдёҚж”ҜжҸҙ provider еӣһ `{status: "unsupported", reason: "provider does not expose a public balance API with API-key authentication"}`.
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-3 shipped: `backend/routers/llm_balance.py` exports `resolve_balance(provider, *, kv=None, fetchers=None, key_resolver=None)` (pure async service-layer function вҖ” all side-effect entrypoints injectable for unit tests) + `get_provider_balance(provider)` (thin FastAPI route wrapper that rejects unknown provider names with HTTP 400 before dispatching). Router: `APIRouter(prefix="/runtime/providers", tags=["runtime-providers"], dependencies=[Depends(_auth.require_admin)])` вҖ” balance data reveals billing, so matches the `require_admin` gate used by `backend/routers/llm_credentials.py` rather than the dashboard's `current_user` baseline. Wired into `backend/main.py:916` under `settings.api_prefix` вҶ’ `/api/v1/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance`. Envelope contract: three top-level shapes keyed on `status` вҖ” `ok` carries `{status, provider, currency, balance_remaining, granted_total, usage_total, last_refreshed_at, source: "cache"|"live", raw}` (exact 9-key set locked by `TestEnvelopeShape::test_ok_envelope_keys`); `unsupported` carries `{status, provider, reason}` with the Z.2-spec verbatim reason string "provider does not expose a public balance API with API-key authentication"; `error` carries `{status, provider, message}`. Service flow: (1) `is_balance_supported` gate вҶ’ early-return unsupported envelope, **no** key resolution, **no** fetch (efficiency + "unsupported" doesn't need a Settings scalar read for providers like ollama/together/anthropic); (2) `SharedKV("provider_balance").get(provider)` вҶ’ JSON-decode with graceful-fallback-to-live on malformed/non-dict payloads (defence-in-depth so a single bad refresher write cannot permanently 500 the dashboard); (3) cache-hit вҶ’ `source="cache"` ok envelope, key-resolver/fetcher NOT invoked (hot-path optimisation verified by `TestCacheHit::test_cache_hit_key_resolver_not_invoked`); (4) cache-miss вҶ’ `_resolve_api_key(provider)` вҶ’ if None/empty, `{status: "error", message: "no API key configured for this provider"}` with **no** cache write (next refresher tick picks up when operator sets the key); (5) fetcher-call вҶ’ `BalanceFetchError` вҶ’ `{status: "error", message: "fetch failed: {reason}"}`, auth-fail `None` вҶ’ `{status: "error", message: "authentication failed вҖ” key may be missing or revoked"}`, unexpected exception (e.g. `RuntimeError`) caught + surfaces as `{status: "error", message: "unexpected error: {type}"}` вҖ” all three error paths keep the cache untouched per the "operator may rotate the key" rule documented in the fetcher / refresher layers; (6) fetcher success вҶ’ `SharedKV.set(provider, _serialise_balance(info))` (atomic HSET / in-memory `threading.Lock`) + `source="live"` ok envelope; SharedKV write failure is logged but does not fail the request (operator sees the live value, next refresher tick retries). **Router-level 400**: path-param outside `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES = {anthropic, google, openai, xai, groq, deepseek, together, openrouter, ollama}` raises HTTP 400 "Unknown provider {...}" rather than silently emitting the `unsupported` envelope вҖ” caller-error must be loud. Note: "unsupported" stays the envelope for keyed-but-no-balance-API providers (anthropic/openai/google/xai/groq/together/ollama); "400" is reserved for typo'd path params outside the service registry entirely. Re-uses `_resolve_api_key` + `_serialise_balance` + `BALANCE_NAMESPACE` from `backend.llm_balance_refresher` so cache-key format + Settings-scalar resolution stay in lock-step with the writer; avoids a parallel implementation that could drift. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-globals in the router вҖ” `resolve_balance` takes kv/fetchers/key_resolver as kwargs, production defaults resolve lazily to `SharedKV(BALANCE_NAMESPACE)` + `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` + `_resolve_api_key`. Cross-worker coordination: the cache lives in SharedKV (qualified answer #2 "via Redis"); the on-demand fetch path is qualified answer #3 ("intentionally per-worker") вҖ” in worst case N workers all get a cache miss at the same moment on cold-start and each issue one vendor request, which at 2-worker Г— 2-provider is ~2 req/worker/cold-start = 4 total req, well under every documented rate limit. **Read-after-write audit**: the only writer site is `kv.set(provider, _serialise_balance(info))`; SharedKV.set is atomic so concurrent readers see either the old or the new snapshot, never torn. **Tests**: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_endpoint.py` 27 cases (7 classes) вҖ” `TestUnsupportedProvider`: 7 unsupported providers parametrised (anthropic/openai/google/xai/groq/together/ollama) all return verbatim-reason envelope without touching fetcher, plus unsupported-no-cache-write; `TestCacheHit`: cached BalanceInfo deserialises + 9-key ok envelope + `source=cache` + fetcher-not-called, plus key_resolver-not-called efficiency lock; `TestCacheMissLiveFetch`: exactly-one-fetch with resolved key + SharedKV populated after + second request hits cache (fetcher call counter stays at 1); `TestCacheMissNoKey`: `None` + empty-string both map to "no API key configured" without fetch or cache write; `TestCacheMissFetcherFailure`: auth-fail/BalanceFetchError/unexpected `RuntimeError` all вҶ’ `error` envelope with no cache write (explicit `kv.get == ""` assertion per branch); `TestMalformedCachePayload`: non-JSON string + JSON-but-non-dict both fall through to live fetch (defence-in-depth); `TestRouterSurface`: unknown-provider вҶ’ HTTP 400, router prefix + route path shape locked, `require_admin` dependency present in `router.dependencies`, `/api/v1/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` registered on `backend.main.app.routes`; `TestEnvelopeShape`: exact key-set lock for each of ok/unsupported/error. All 27 pass; pre-existing 75 balance tests still green (102 combined). Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean. `backend.main` imports clean with the new router wiring. Remaining Z.2 checkboxes (batch endpoint + boundary `stale_since`/error-no-cache contract + integration tests) not delivered by this commit. -->

  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-4 shipped: `backend/routers/llm_balance.py` now exports `resolve_all_balances(*, kv=None, fetchers=None, key_resolver=None)` (pure async service-layer function that delegates per-provider to existing `resolve_balance`) + `get_providers_balance_batch()` (HTTP route handler). Registered on the existing `APIRouter(prefix="/runtime/providers", dependencies=[Depends(_auth.require_admin)])` as `GET /balance` вҶ’ wires to `/api/v1/runtime/providers/balance`. Registered in source order *before* `GET /{provider}/balance`; segment-count differs (0 vs 2 after prefix) so FastAPI disambiguates structurally regardless of order, but source-order reflects general-before-specific per style. No path-param conflict. Response envelope: `{"providers": [<envelope>, <envelope>, ...]}` вҖ” each inner envelope is exactly what `GET /{provider}/balance` would return for that provider (`ok` / `unsupported` / `error` вҖ” same 9/3/3 key-sets, NO extra wrapper keys added to per-provider envelopes so frontend can share a single `ProviderBalance` render component). Ordering deterministic: `sorted(_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES)` вҶ’ `[anthropic, deepseek, google, groq, ollama, openai, openrouter, together, xai]` so dashboard renders rows in stable order without client-side sort. All 9 providers surface regardless of support/key/cache state (both supported and unsupported listed per Z.2 spec "еүҚз«ҜдёҖж¬ЎжёІжҹ“"). Per-provider calls run concurrently via `asyncio.gather(*[resolve_balance(p, ...) for p in names], return_exceptions=True)` вҖ” independent vendors + independent `SharedKV` slots make this safe; warm-cache path issues zero HTTP calls (all 9 hit cache-hit short-circuit); cold-start worst case issues 2 parallel HTTP calls (deepseek + openrouter) because the other 7 exit at the `is_balance_supported` gate before any fetch attempt. **Single `SharedKV` handle** threaded through all 9 per-provider calls (avoids 9Г— redundant `SharedKV(BALANCE_NAMESPACE)` constructions per request). **Defence-in-depth exception isolation**: `return_exceptions=True` + a post-gather `isinstance(result, BaseException)` check converts any hypothetical abstraction-leak past `resolve_balance`'s inner except blocks to an `error` envelope for that single provider (`"unexpected error: <ClassName>"`) while letting the other 8 through вҖ” so one flaky vendor can never 500 the whole batch. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-globals. `resolve_all_balances` takes all side-effect entrypoints as kwargs for unit tests (matches the checkbox-3 service-layer pattern); production default resolves to `SharedKV(BALANCE_NAMESPACE)` + `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` + `_resolve_api_key`. `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` (frozenset, module-const) is the only read вҖ” qualified answer #1 (each worker derives the same value). Cross-worker coordination stays identical to checkbox-3: the cache lives in SharedKV (qualified answer #2 "via Redis"); on-demand fetch path is qualified answer #3 ("intentionally per-worker") вҖ” at N workers the worst-case cold-start issues NГ—2 = 4 requests at 2-worker Г— 2-supported config, well under every documented rate limit. **Read-after-write audit**: writer sites unchanged (still `_store.set(provider, _serialise_balance(info))` inside `resolve_balance`); `asyncio.gather` runs 2 potential writes concurrently for different SharedKV slots (no collision вҖ” different keys) and at most 1 write per key per request (no self-concurrency). **Auth gate**: batch route inherits `require_admin` from the router-level `dependencies` parameter (no per-route override needed) вҖ” balances reveal billing, so `require_admin` stays the correct gate, not `current_user`. **Tests**: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_endpoint.py` +14 new cases (3 new classes appended after existing 27) вҖ” `TestBatchEndpoint` (8 cases): all-9-providers-covered-exactly-once + alphabetical-ordering-lock + mixed-supported-unsupported-in-single-payload (deepseek cache-hit + openrouter live-fetch + 7 unsupported) + supported-no-key-returns-error-while-unsupported-stays-unsupported + partial-failure-isolation (one fetcher raises, other succeeds) + shared-single-KV-handle (both writes land on the same KV instance readable after batch) + second-call-is-all-cache (warm-up then verify call counters don't increment on second round) + unexpected-resolve_balance-exception-isolated (monkeypatches `resolve_balance` to raise for one provider, verifies `return_exceptions=True` converts to error envelope without collapsing batch); `TestBatchRouterSurface` (4 cases): batch route registered on router + on app at `/api/v1/runtime/providers/balance` + coexists with single route (no shadow) + inherits `require_admin`; `TestBatchEnvelopeShape` (2 cases): top-level `{providers: [...]}` shape + each inner envelope matches the ok/unsupported/error key-sets from the single endpoint. All 41 endpoint tests pass; 116 combined Z.2 balance tests still green (46 fetchers + 29 refresher + 41 endpoint). Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean on both router + test files. `backend.main` imports clean with existing router wiring (no changes to main.py вҖ” batch route registers via the already-included router). Remaining Z.2 checkboxes (boundary `stale_since` on 5xx + integration tests with mocked DeepSeek/OpenRouter HTTP) not delivered by this commit. -->

- [x] йӮҠз•ҢпјҡAPI key ж јејҸйҢҜжҲ–иў« provider дҪңе»ў вҶ’ еӣһ `{status: "error", message: "..."}` дёҚ cacheпјҲдёӢж¬ЎжӯЈеёё key иҰҒиғҪз«ӢеҲ» pick upпјүпјӣDeepSeek / OpenRouter API жң¬иә« 5xx вҶ’ еӣһеҝ«еҸ–еҖјдёҰжЁҷ `stale_since`гҖӮ
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-5 shipped: boundary contract implemented across `backend/llm_balance_refresher.py` + `backend/routers/llm_balance.py`. New SharedKV namespace `STALE_NAMESPACE = "provider_balance_stale"` (distinct from the cache namespace `"provider_balance"`) holds per-provider `"<epoch_float>"` timestamps вҖ” written on the fetcher's 5xx / transport / malformed / unexpected-exception paths (BalanceFetchError + bare `Exception` catch), cleared on successful fetch, **never** touched on the auth-fail (`None`) path or the `no_key` path. New helpers in `llm_balance_refresher.py`: `_stale_kv()` вҶ’ `SharedKV(STALE_NAMESPACE)`, `_write_stale_marker(stale_kv, provider, now)` вҶ’ `stale_kv.set(provider, f"{now:.6f}")`, `_clear_stale_marker(stale_kv, provider)` вҶ’ `stale_kv.delete(provider)`, `_read_stale_marker(stale_kv, provider)` вҶ’ `float | None` with self-healing delete on unparseable entries (so a corrupted slot cannot permanently mask a provider behind a "stale" render that never clears). `refresh_once` now accepts `stale_kv` kwarg (defaults to `_stale_kv()`) and wires the four paths per the contract above вҖ” defensive `try/except Exception` around every stale SharedKV call so a flaky Redis here cannot cascade into an uncaught exception that skips the backoff reset or the cache write. `resolve_balance` in the router mirrors the same contract on the cache-miss branch: 5xx writes marker, auth-fail leaves it untouched, success clears it; on cache-hit the marker is read and surfaced via `stale_since` in the envelope. Auth-fail path also preserves the existing cache per Z.2 spec "дёӢж¬ЎжӯЈеёё key иҰҒиғҪз«ӢеҲ» pick up" вҖ” operator rotating a key does not invalidate prior good snapshots, so the next successful refresh lands cleanly without a gap. `_ok_envelope` gained a `stale_since: float | None` field (always emitted for shape stability, `None` when fresh, epoch timestamp when marker is set). Cache-hit short-circuits the fetcher even when `stale_since` is present вҖ” the refresher owns the retry cadence, and endpoint-side re-fetch would both hammer the vendor and race the backoff state. `resolve_all_balances` threads a single `stale_kv` handle through all 9 per-provider calls (same pattern as the cache handle from checkbox 4) so a marker set by one provider's fetcher-raise path is visible to a concurrent cache-hit read in the same batch round; envelope ordering + shape unchanged (`ok` envelopes grow `stale_since`; `unsupported` and `error` shapes stay at 3 keys). **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: no new module-globals вҖ” the only additions are `STALE_NAMESPACE` (module-const string, rubric #1 "жҜҸ worker еҫһеҗҢжЁЈдҫҶжәҗжҺЁе°Һ") + the four pure helper functions that take `stale_kv` as an argument. Cross-worker coordination: the stale marker lives in SharedKV just like the cache (qualified answer #2 "via Redis") so marker writes from worker A are read by worker B on the next cache-hit; in-memory fallback is per-worker and matches the cache's own fallback semantics so single-worker dev behaves identically. **Read-after-write audit**: three write sites (`_stale.set` via `_write_stale_marker`, `_stale.delete` via `_clear_stale_marker`, cache `_store.set`). SharedKV.set / .delete are atomic (Redis HSET/HDEL atomic, in-memory fallback uses `threading.Lock`). The endpoint does NOT read-its-own-writes within one request вҖ” cache-hit path reads marker first then returns, cache-miss path writes marker on failure then returns; there is no sequence where our write has to be visible to our read in the same call. **Tests**: 25 new cases across the two suites. `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_endpoint.py` +13 cases (5 new classes appended): `TestBoundaryStaleSinceOnCacheHit` (4) вҖ” cache hit without marker вҶ’ `stale_since=None`; cache hit with marker вҶ’ `stale_since=<epoch>` AND cached balance still served (locks the "еӣһеҝ«еҸ–еҖјдёҰжЁҷ stale_since" half of the spec verbatim); unparseable marker self-heals; cache-hit short-circuits fetcher even with marker; `TestBoundaryStaleMarkerLifecycle` (3) вҖ” live fetch success clears prior marker; live fetch 5xx writes marker at `now`; live fetch unexpected exception also writes marker (defence-in-depth); `TestBoundaryAuthFailDoesNotMarkStale` (3) вҖ” auth-fail must NOT write marker; prior marker survives auth-fail; auth-fail on one provider does not touch another provider's cache slot; `TestBoundaryNoKeyPath` (1) вҖ” no-key branch does not write marker; `TestBoundaryBatchAggregation` (2) вҖ” batch endpoint propagates per-provider `stale_since` to cache envelopes with distinct/fresh per-provider markers; unsupported envelopes have no `stale_since` key (only `ok` shape carries it). Envelope shape tests updated: `test_ok_envelope_keys` now locks the 10-key set (+stale_since); `test_batch_each_envelope_matches_single_endpoint_shape` same. `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_refresher.py` +13 cases (2 new classes appended before the existing `TestRegistryIntegration`): `TestRefreshOnceStaleMarker` (8) вҖ” fetch_error writes marker at now; unexpected exception writes marker; success clears prior marker; auth-fail does not touch existing marker; auth-fail does not synthesise marker where none existed; no-key does not touch marker; backoff-gated skip does not touch marker; multi-provider markers independent (one 5xx's marker does not bleed to the other's slot); `TestStaleMarkerHelpers` (5) вҖ” round-trip write+read; read-empty returns None; clear removes entry; unparseable self-heals; `STALE_NAMESPACE != BALANCE_NAMESPACE` (regression guard against someone collapsing the two namespaces back to one). Totals: fetchers 46 + refresher 42 + endpoint 54 = 142 all green (pre-checkbox: 46+29+41=116 вҶ’ +26 new cases). `backend.main` imports clean, pre-commit fingerprint grep (`_conn()`/`await conn.commit()`/`datetime('now')`/`VALUES (?, ?, ...)`) clean on both router + refresher + both test files. Next Z.2 checkbox (integration tests with mocked DeepSeek/OpenRouter HTTP responses) remains `[ ]` and is not delivered by this commit. -->

- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡmock DeepSeek + OpenRouter API responseпјҲvalid balance / auth fail / 5xxпјүпјҢй©— SharedKV жӣҙж–°гҖҒbatch endpoint иҒҡеҗҲгҖҒunsupported provider иЎҢзӮәгҖӮ
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-6 shipped: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_integration.py` вҖ” 36 new integration cases across 6 classes driving the full stack (mocked vendor HTTP via `respx` вҶ’ **real** `fetch_balance_deepseek` / `fetch_balance_openrouter` вҶ’ `refresh_once` вҶ’ `SharedKV` вҶ’ `resolve_balance` / `resolve_all_balances`). Deliberate distinction from the three pre-existing unit files: `test_llm_balance_fetchers.py` / `test_llm_balance_refresher.py` / `test_llm_balance_endpoint.py` each lock one layer in isolation and the refresher/endpoint suites drive **injected fake fetchers**, so they never exercise the real fetcher вҶ” refresher вҶ” SharedKV вҶ” endpoint wiring end-to-end; this file does, with respx intercepting only at the transport layer so the BalanceInfo shape and serialise/deserialise format round-trip through real code on both sides. **Classes + cases**: `TestIntegrationValidBalance` (4) вҖ” 200 response вҶ’ refresher populates SharedKV for both providers with the full BalanceInfo envelope (currency/balance_remaining/granted_total/usage_total/None-for-deepseek), endpoint subsequent cache-hit renders `source="cache"` + `stale_since=None`, endpoint cache-miss вҶ’ live HTTP fetch hit + SharedKV populated, backoff reset after success (consecutive_failures вҶ’ 0, next_attempt_at pushed by base_interval). `TestIntegrationAuthFail` (6) вҖ” parametrised 401/403 for each of deepseek + openrouter вҶ’ refresher outcome `"auth_fail"`, SharedKV cache **empty** (no stale auth_failed envelope), stale marker **untouched** (auth-fail is operator-side not provider-down), backoff still advances; auth-fail preserves prior cached snapshot (дёӢж¬ЎжӯЈеёё key иҰҒиғҪз«ӢеҲ» pick up еҘ‘зҙ„); endpoint cache-miss + 403 surfaces the spec-verbatim "authentication failed вҖ” key may be missing or revoked" message with cache + stale marker both clean. `TestIntegration5xx` (10) вҖ” parametrised 500/502/503/504 for each of deepseek + openrouter вҶ’ refresher outcome `"fetch_error"`, cache untouched, stale marker written at `now`; canonical lifecycle test (tick-1 success at now=1000 вҶ’ tick-2 503 at now=2000 with clock advanced past backoff window) locks cache-preserved + stale_since surfaces on subsequent endpoint cache-hit at `now=2000.0`; endpoint cache-miss + 502 writes marker at `now` even though nothing to serve (concurrent-worker coherence). `TestIntegrationBatchAggregation` (5) вҖ” `resolve_all_balances` under real HTTP: mixed-success aggregates 2 ok + 7 unsupported (alphabetical order locked), mixed 200/500 aggregates 1 ok + 1 error + 7 unsupported (only failing provider gets stale marker + only succeeding provider gets cache write), both-supported-auth-fail surfaces 2 error + 7 unsupported with zero KV/stale writes, second-call-is-all-cache proves respx call_count stays at 1 after warm-up, cache-hit-with-stale-marker propagates `stale_since` through batch to the deepseek envelope while openrouter stays fresh. `TestIntegrationUnsupportedProvider` (9) вҖ” 7 unsupported providers parametrised (anthropic/google/groq/ollama/openai/together/xai) вҶ’ `unsupported` envelope with spec-verbatim reason, no HTTP attempted (respx would reject unregistered routes), no cache/stale touch, resolver never consulted (short-circuit before key resolution); refresher default iteration is supported-only (anthropic/etc. never appear in outcomes even when resolver returns `sk-{p}` for all); batch-with-unsupported-entry-in-fetchers-dict proves `is_balance_supported` gate fires before fetcher dispatch (anthropic's injected fetcher never called despite being present in fetchers kwarg). `TestIntegrationRefresherToEndpoint` (2) вҖ” cross-layer regression guard: refresher write is round-trip readable by endpoint (full 10-key envelope exact match including `raw` vendor body), concatenated lifecycle (refresher success at t=1000 вҶ’ refresher 503 at t=5000 вҶ’ endpoint reads cache with stale_since=5000) anchors the whole Z.2 story. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero shared state between tests вҖ” respx.mock context per-case, SharedKV namespaces via `uuid.uuid4` so in-memory fallback dicts cannot cross-pollute even without Redis; refresher `_LOOP_RUNNING` never touched (tests drive `refresh_once` directly, not `run_refresh_loop`); every test passes own `kv`/`stale_kv`/`key_resolver` kwargs and lets `fetchers=None` so the real `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` fetcher code path is exercised (this is the key distinction from unit siblings). **Read-after-write audit**: two write sites (`_store.set` on success, `_stale.set` on 5xx); both atomic (Redis HSET / in-memory `threading.Lock`); within-test writeвҶ’read happens in same coroutine so ordering is trivial, not timing-sensitive. **Test results**: 36/36 pass (0.96s). Combined with pre-existing balance tests: 46 fetchers + 42 refresher + 54 endpoint + 36 integration = **178/178 pass** (3.40s, 0 regression). Pre-commit fingerprint grep (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) clean on the new file. No source-code changes вҖ” this commit is test-only (+ TODO.md status flip + HANDOFF.md entry). Z.2 (#291) all 6 checkboxes now shipped. -->

Next gate: deployed-inactive вҖ” waits on a live restart of the backend image so the lifespan refresh loop writes to production Redis against real DeepSeek/OpenRouter keys in Settings; this Z.2 row no longer gates that (all code paths now covered), so deployment can proceed whenever the backend image is next rebuilt. -->

й җдј°пјҡ**1 day**

### Z.3 Price table еӨ–зҪ®еҢ–пјҲYAML + hot-reloadпјү (#292)

зҸҫеңЁзЎ¬еҜ« 8 еҖӢ model зҡ„ in/out rate еңЁ Python code иЈЎпјҢж”№ rate иҰҒж”№ code йҮҚйғЁзҪІгҖӮжӢҶжҲҗ YAML + `/reload` endpoint е°ұеҸҜд»ҘеҚіжҷӮи·ҹ provider иӘҝеғ№гҖӮ

- [x] ж–° `config/llm_pricing.yaml`пјҡиҰҶи“Ӣ 9 е®¶дё»иҰҒ modelпјҲeach `(input_per_mtok, output_per_mtok)` USDпјүпјҢзөҗж§ӢеҰӮпјҡ

  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-1 shipped: new `config/llm_pricing.yaml` (~95 lines). Covers all 9 providers surfaced by `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` from Z.2 (anthropic / openai / google / xai / groq / deepseek / together / openrouter / ollama) with 2вҖ“4 representative models each plus a per-provider `_default` fallback tier, a global `defaults: {input: 1, output: 3}` (deliberately higher than the cheapest real provider so unknown-model billing is visible in dashboards rather than silently charged near-free), and `metadata` (`updated_at: 2026-04-25` / `source: <issue #292>` / `schema_version: 1` / multi-line `notes`). **Scope discipline**: file-only delivery вҖ” no code changes to `backend/routers/system.py` (the `_PRICING` dict stays authoritative until checkbox 2 wires `backend/pricing.py::get_pricing`), no new Python module, no reload endpoint, no tests; those are later Z.3 checkboxes and would expand blast radius beyond a single row. **Price-neutral migration contract**: all 8 models currently hard-coded in `backend/routers/system.py:1094-1103` (`claude-opus-4-7` / `claude-opus-4-20250514` / `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` / `gpt-4o` / `gemini-1.5-pro` / `grok-3-mini` / `llama-3.3-70b-versatile` / `deepseek-chat`) carry values **bit-identical** to the hard-coded dict, verified by cross-reference in the commit; this honours Z.3 checkbox 7 up-front вҖ” "ж—ўжңү token usage зҙҖйҢ„дёҚйҮҚз®—жҲҗжң¬пјҢдҝқз•ҷз•¶жҷӮиЁҲиІ»пјҢеҸӘеҪұйҹҝжңӘдҫҶиЁҲеғ№" вҖ” and means the later cutover can't accidentally shift "future" billing either at the exact moment of migration. New models added beyond those 8 (claude-haiku-4-5 / gpt-5 / gpt-5-mini / gpt-4o-mini / gemini-2.5-pro / gemini-2.5-flash / grok-4 / llama-3.1-8b-instant / deepseek-reasoner / 2 together-ai models / 4 openrouter aggregator entries / 3 ollama local models) draw from each vendor's public pricing page as of 2026-04-22; they are not currently billed by `track_tokens` (which only looks up exact-match in `_PRICING`) so adding them here has zero effect until checkbox 2 wires the lookup. **Relationship to `configs/provider_pricing.yaml`**: the pre-existing file is blended per-tier (premium/default/budget) used by `backend/forecast.py` for dollar-estimate forecasting; this file is exact per-model (input, output) for accurate post-hoc billing. The two files are intentionally **not** merged вҖ” merging would force either forecasts to lose tier granularity or billing to lose input/output split; header comment in `config/llm_pricing.yaml` documents the distinction so future contributors don't collapse them. **YAML contract tested by `python3 -c "import yaml; вҖҰ"`**: parses cleanly, 9 providers, every model has numeric `input` + `output`, no missing keys. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` вҶ’ 0 hits (YAML file, no SQL surface). **Module-global audit**: no Python code touched, no new module-globals, no cross-worker coherence concerns this commit; when checkbox 2 lands `backend/pricing.py` the load+cache path will need its own rubric #1 / rubric #2 answer (each worker loads same file at boot = rubric #1; reload fan-out = rubric #2 via SharedKV version counter). Directory `config/` is new (singular) and sits next to existing `configs/` (plural, unrelated) вҖ” this intentional per the Z.3 spec text `ж–° config/llm_pricing.yaml`. Commit co-author includes env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->

Next gate: deployed-inactive вҖ” waits on Z.3 checkbox 2 (`backend/pricing.py::get_pricing` + startup load) + checkbox 4 (`POST /runtime/pricing/reload`) to actually route `track_tokens` lookups through this file. Until then, `backend/routers/system.py::_PRICING` remains the authoritative billing source and this file is unreferenced config. -->
- [x] ж”№ `backend/routers/system.py:1091-1100` зЎ¬еҜ« dictпјҢж”№жҲҗ `backend/pricing.py::get_pricing(provider, model) -> tuple[float, float]`пјҢе•ҹеӢ•жҷӮ load YAMLгҖҒhot-reload via `/runtime/pricing/reload`. <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-2 shipped: new module `backend/pricing.py` (~210 LOC) wires the YAML at `config/llm_pricing.yaml` (the singular `config/` dir from checkbox 1) into `backend/routers/system.py::track_tokens`. **Public API**: `get_pricing(provider: str | None, model: str) -> tuple[float, float]` вҖ” never raises, always returns floats. Lookup chain: (1) `providers[provider][model]` exact, (2) provider known + model unknown вҶ’ `providers[provider]._default`, (3) provider unknown / `provider=None` with no scan hit вҶ’ global `defaults` from YAML, (4) YAML missing/corrupt вҶ’ `_HARD_CODED_FALLBACK` per-model dict (bit-identical to the pre-Z.3 dict that lived at system.py:1094-1103) вҶ’ `_HARD_CODED_GLOBAL_DEFAULT = (1.0, 3.0)`. **Provider auto-detect**: `track_tokens(model, ...)` callers (LLM callback at backend/agents/llm.py:675, tests in backend/tests/test_token_budget.py) only know the model id, so passing `provider=None` triggers a first-hit-wins scan across all provider tables вҖ” preserves the legacy single-arg call shape. **Wiring at system.py**: replaced the 9-line `_PRICING = {...}` dict with `from backend.pricing import get_pricing as _get_pricing`; the `_PRICING.get(model, (1.0, 3.0))` call inside `track_tokens` becomes `_get_pricing(None, model)`. No call-site signature change вҶ’ backend/agents/llm.py + tests untouched. **Price-neutral verified end-to-end**: smoke script exercised `track_tokens('claude-opus-4-7', 1_000_000, 1_000_000, 100)` вҶ’ cost = `$30.00` (5 + 25), bit-identical to pre-Z.3; `track_tokens('totally-unknown-model', 1M, 1M, 50)` вҶ’ `$4.00` matching the (1.0, 3.0) historical fallback. All 8 pre-Z.3 hard-coded models cross-checked through `get_pricing(None, ...)` вҶ’ tuples bit-identical to the EXPECT dict harvested from the original lines 1094-1103. **Hot-reload primitive**: `reload()` clears the local-process cache and returns `{loaded_from_yaml, providers, metadata}` for the future `POST /runtime/pricing/reload` endpoint (Z.3 checkbox 4) to call before broadcasting via SharedKV. **Side-API**: `get_metadata()` returns the YAML's metadata block (updated_at / source / schema_version / notes) so checkbox 5's `GET /runtime/pricing` can render it; `reset_cache_for_tests()` mirrors `backend/context_limits.py`'s test hook. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: `_PRICING_CACHE` is module-level dict but each uvicorn worker derives identical state from the same on-disk YAML at first call вҶ’ matches answer 1 of the three valid cross-worker-coherence answers ("derived from a shared static source"); the YAML is bundled in the production image, read-only at runtime; `reload()` only clears local-process cache (cross-worker fan-out is checkbox 4's domain вҖ” explicit scope split documented in pricing.py docstring). **Boot resilience**: tested missing-file path (`_PRICING_PATH.exists() is False` вҶ’ cache populated with hard-coded fallback + global default + `_loaded_from_yaml=False`); a corrupt YAML (`yaml.safe_load` raises) is caught broadly, logged at WARNING, falls through to the same path. Z.3 checkbox 6 (the test row) will assert this contract; not exercised here to keep blast radius single-row. **Pre-existing test sweep**: `backend/tests/test_token_budget.py` 6/6 green after the wiring change вҖ” confirms `track_tokens` arithmetic, `track_tokens` budget-flip, and the cost-rounding all unchanged. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` on the new file: 0 hits. The single hit on `backend/routers/system.py:1444` is pre-existing prose in a comment about `alembic_pg_compat`, not introduced or touched by this commit. **Scope discipline вҖ” what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to its own row): no per-day-throttled fallback warning logging (checkbox 3); no `POST /runtime/pricing/reload` endpoint (checkbox 4); no SharedKV broadcast for cross-worker reload sync (checkbox 4); no `GET /runtime/pricing` (checkbox 5); no test row dedicated to YAML-corrupt boot resilience (checkbox 6); no migration row about historical token_usage cost preservation (checkbox 7 вҖ” already honoured implicitly by the price-neutral cutover). Each is a separate `- [ ]` row in Z.3 вҖ” going further would expand blast radius beyond a single checkbox. Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->

Until then, picking up a price change still requires a worker restart (which on uvicorn `--workers N` means a Caddy-level rolling restart). Next gate: deployed-active вҖ” needs Z.3 checkbox 4 (`POST /runtime/pricing/reload` + SharedKV broadcast) to flip; ETA single-checkbox commit, no maintenance window required since the underlying loader is already live. -->
- [x] жӣҙж–° fallback зӯ–з•Ҙпјҡprovider зҹҘйҒ“дҪҶ model дёҚзҹҘйҒ“ вҶ’ з”Ёи©І provider зҡ„ `_default`пјӣйғҪдёҚзҹҘйҒ“ вҶ’ з”Ёе…Ёеҹҹ `defaults`гҖӮжҜҸж¬Ў fallback йғҪ log warningпјҲдёҖж¬Ў/еӨ©/providerпјҢдёҚиҰҒжҙ— logпјүгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-3 shipped: `backend/pricing.py` gains throttled fallback warnings on the two fallback arms the Z.3 spec names вҖ” `provider known + model unknown вҶ’ providers[<provider>]._default` and `both unknown вҶ’ defaults`. The lookup chain itself was already wired by checkbox 2; this commit adds the observability layer. **Throttle mechanics**: new module-globals `_FALLBACK_WARN_TS: dict[str, float]` + `_FALLBACK_WARN_LOCK: threading.Lock()` + constant `_WARN_THROTTLE_SECONDS = 86400.0`; new private helper `_maybe_warn_fallback(throttle_key, fmt, *args)` does check-then-update under the lock and only calls `logger.warning` when вүҘ24h since the last emit for that key. **Key convention**: `{arm}:{provider_key}` вҖ” four distinct arms (`provider_default:<provider>` / `global_default:<provider_or_"<unknown>">` / `hardcoded_global:<provider_or_"<unknown>">`, plus implicit "no log" arms for exact + scan hits) so two different arms on the same provider each get their own clock, and two different providers on the same arm each get their own clock (both behaviours locked by `test_different_providers_log_independently` + `test_different_arms_same_provider_log_independently`). **What does NOT log**: exact `providers[<provider>][<model>]` hit and `provider=None` scan hit (scan-hit is successful disambiguation, not fallback) вҖ” locked by `test_exact_hit_does_not_warn` + `test_provider_none_scan_hit_does_not_warn`. **Warning payload** (examples caught by caplog): `llm_pricing fallback: provider='anthropic' known but model='claude-unknown-model' unknown; billing at providers[anthropic]._default=(3.0, 15.0) (throttled 1/day/provider per worker)` / `llm_pricing fallback: no provider/model match for provider=None model='totally-unknown-model'; billing at global defaults=(1.0, 3.0) (throttled 1/day/provider per worker)`. Operator sees exactly which provider + which model first tripped the arm + which rate table served the request, so dashboards can correlate "expensive unknown" cost spikes back to the YAML gap that needs filling. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1, answer 3 "ж•…ж„ҸжҜҸ worker зҚЁз«Ӣ")**: `_FALLBACK_WARN_TS` is module-level and deliberately not coordinated across uvicorn workers. Rationale explicitly documented in the module docstring: at 2 workers Г— 2 replicas = 4 aggregate workers, the ceiling is 4 WARNING lines per day per (arm, provider) key вҖ” far below any reasonable "log spam" threshold, and a SharedKV/Redis round-trip on every price lookup would be wildly disproportionate to the observability gain. Rolling restarts reset per-worker cleanly, which is the desired behaviour (fresh deploy should re-announce which providers are tripping fallbacks). A `threading.Lock` guards the dict so concurrent async callers in the same worker can't race on check-then-update. **reset_cache_for_tests contract extended**: now also clears `_FALLBACK_WARN_TS` under the lock so every test gets a pristine "first emit" window вҖ” this keeps `test_pricing.py`'s autouse fixture honest regardless of what earlier tests in the same process already did. **Tests (new `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`, 25 cases, 4 TestCase classes)**: `TestFallbackStrategy` (6) locks the lookup chain itself вҖ” exact-hit / provider-default arm / global-default arm / `provider=None` scan-hit / `provider=None` miss / provider case-insensitive; `TestFallbackWarningEmission` (5) confirms each arm emits exactly one WARNING with the expected content and that exact-hit / scan-hit emit nothing; `TestFallbackWarningThrottle` (6) locks the 24h throttle semantics including cross-provider independence, cross-arm independence, and the monkeypatched `pricing.time.time` test that advances beyond `_WARN_THROTTLE_SECONDS` to prove re-emission works; `TestPriceNeutralityAcrossFallbacks` (8 parametrised, one per pre-Z.3 model) re-asserts the checkbox-2 bit-identical contract under the new warning machinery to catch regressions where the log path accidentally short-circuits the lookup. **Future checkbox 6 + Z.5 will extend this same `test_pricing.py` file** with YAML-corrupt boot / reload endpoint / cross-worker sync cases. 25/25 green on the new file in ~0.24s. **Regression sweep**: `test_token_budget.py` 6/6 + `test_forecast.py` 6/6 + `test_context_limits.py` 25/25 = 37/37 neighbour tests green after the pricing.py edit, confirming (a) the `track_tokens` arithmetic is untouched (same `get_pricing(None, model)` return values), (b) `forecast.py`'s `configs/provider_pricing.yaml` loader is completely separate and unaffected, (c) `context_limits.py`'s sibling `configs/context_window_limits.yaml` loader has no shared state with `backend/pricing.py`. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/pricing.py` + `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`: 0 matches on both. **Scope discipline вҖ” what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred): no `POST /runtime/pricing/reload` endpoint (checkbox 4); no SharedKV cross-worker fan-out for reload (checkbox 4); no `GET /runtime/pricing` (checkbox 5); no YAML-corrupt-boot dedicated test row (checkbox 6); no historical token_usage migration row (checkbox 7 вҖ” already honoured by checkbox 2's price-neutral cutover). Commit co-author includes env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: deployed-inactive вҖ” code shipped to main, throttle is active inside every running worker as soon as the image is rolled, but no operator is yet looking at the WARNING lines because the ops runbook entry about "when you see llm_pricing fallback warnings in backend logs, edit config/llm_pricing.yaml and POST /runtime/pricing/reload" lives alongside the reload endpoint (Z.3 checkbox 4, next). The fallback arms themselves have been **live since checkbox 2** (the code was already returning provider._default and global defaults); this checkbox just makes those silent fallbacks visible. Next gate: deployed-active вҖ” needs Z.3 checkbox 4 (reload endpoint + SharedKV broadcast) so operators can respond to the warning without a rolling restart; ETA single-checkbox commit, no maintenance window. -->

- [x] ж–° `POST /runtime/pricing/reload`пјҲrequire_adminпјүпјҡйҮҚж–°и®Җ YAMLпјҢSharedKV е»Јж’ӯ reload дәӢд»¶и®“жүҖжңү worker еҗҢжӯҘгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-4 shipped: new endpoint `POST /api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` in `backend/routers/system.py` (~36 LOC), gated by `_REQUIRE_ADMIN = [Depends(_auth.require_role("admin"))]` (the same shared dep list at system.py:63 used by `/runtime/deploy`, `/runtime/release`, `/runtime/forecast/recompute`, `/runtime/token-budget/reset` вҖ” uniform admin-write convention across the router). **Local + cross-worker reload contract**: the endpoint (a) calls `pricing.reload()` synchronously to clear THIS worker's `_PRICING_CACHE` and re-read `config/llm_pricing.yaml`, then (b) calls `publish_cross_worker(pricing.PRICING_RELOAD_EVENT, {"origin_worker": <pid>})` so every peer worker's `_on_pricing_reload_event` callback (registered at module-import time in `backend/pricing.py`, mirroring the `events.py:265-269` precedent against `shared_state._pubsub_callbacks`) clears its own cache. Each peer's next `get_pricing()` call lazily re-reads the same YAML вҖ” no synchronous fan-out RPC required, the loader is the broadcast amplifier. **Response body**: `{"status": "reloaded", "loaded_from_yaml": bool, "providers": [...sorted...], "metadata": {updated_at, source, schema_version, notes}, "broadcast": "redis_pubsub" | "local_only"}` вҖ” operator can inspect at a glance whether the YAML actually loaded (catches typos that produce empty providers + `loaded_from_yaml=False`), which providers landed, and whether the cross-worker fan-out succeeded (degraded "local_only" surfaces missing Redis without erroring). **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: `_PRICING_CACHE` mutation now has a coordinated cross-worker invalidation path вҶ’ matches answer 2 of the three valid audit answers ("Redis-coordinated"). Documented in `pricing.py` module docstring including the small reload-propagation window: worker A is reloaded, worker B has not yet processed the pubsub message (bounded by `pubsub.get_message(timeout=1.0)` in `shared_state.start_pubsub_listener`); a token-tracking call landing on B during that window bills at the OLD rates, which is acceptable per Z.3 checkbox 7 вҖ” "ж—ўжңү token usage зҙҖйҢ„дёҚйҮҚз®—жҲҗжң¬пјҢдҝқз•ҷз•¶жҷӮиЁҲиІ»пјҢеҸӘеҪұйҹҝжңӘдҫҶиЁҲеғ№" вҖ” both rates are valid "future" prices in those sub-second windows. **Read-after-write timing audit**: not relevant вҖ” the reload doesn't change a serialised operation to a parallel one, only invalidates a cache; existing `track_tokens()` callers don't read-after-write across workers in a single transaction. **Constant `pricing.PRICING_RELOAD_EVENT = "pricing_reload"`** is the wire-format string published over Redis pub/sub. Locked by a dedicated `test_event_constant_is_stable` because changing it during a rolling deploy would silently break already-deployed workers listening for the old name. **Origin filtering deliberately absent** in `_on_pricing_reload_event`: invalidating a stale cache costs ~one disk read on the next `get_pricing()` call, much cheaper than the bookkeeping required to suppress the originator's self-message. `events.py::_on_cross_worker_event` does filter, but it has different semantics (the SSE bus would deliver duplicates to local subscribers without filtering); pricing's reload is idempotent. **Tests (9 new in `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`, brings file from 25 вҶ’ 34 cases)**: `TestCrossWorkerCallbackRegistration` (5) вҖ” callback is in `shared_state._pubsub_callbacks` at import time / event constant is the wire-format `"pricing_reload"` / callback clears `_PRICING_CACHE` when invoked / callback ignores unrelated events like `"sse"` (regression guard against accidentally widening the trigger) / callback tolerates malformed non-dict payloads. `TestReloadPricingEndpoint` (4) вҖ” endpoint returns 200 with the documented status shape + 9 providers from the live YAML; endpoint picks up YAML edits via `monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_PRICING_PATH", fake)` (reading 99/999 from the temp file post-reload locks the actual reload-from-disk semantic, not just cache invalidation); endpoint survives YAML missing at reload time (returns 200, `loaded_from_yaml: False`, falls back to hard-coded `claude-opus-4-7 = (5.0, 25.0)`); `test_endpoint_route_requires_admin` inspects the route's `dependencies` list and asserts both `current_user` (router-level baseline) and `_dep` (the closure produced by `require_role("admin")`) are wired вҖ” a route-definition lock that survives even if the test conftest's "open" auth-mode default would otherwise mask a missing gate. 34/34 green on `test_pricing.py` in ~3.0s. **Regression sweep**: 96/96 neighbour tests green across `test_token_budget.py` (6) + `test_forecast.py` (6) + `test_context_limits.py` (25) + `test_shared_state.py` (59 вҖ” confirms the new callback registration doesn't break the existing `register_cross_worker_callback` contract or the SSE callback in events.py). Pre-existing master failures in `test_event_system.py::TestReplayEndpoint` (3) and `test_release.py` (4) verified via `git stash && pytest && git stash pop` вҖ” both fail identically on the master baseline (db_pool init ordering + release fixture issues), unrelated to this checkbox. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/pricing.py` + `backend/routers/system.py` + `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`: 0 hits introduced; the single grep hit at `backend/routers/system.py:1444` is pre-existing prose in the alembic_pg_compat comment, untouched by this commit. **Live smoke** (asyncio.run on `reload_pricing_table()` outside the ASGI stack): returns the documented dict with broadcast=`local_only` (no Redis in dev shell вҖ” exactly the documented degradation path), 9 providers, full metadata block. **Scope discipline вҖ” what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred): no `GET /runtime/pricing` (checkbox 5 вҖ” the next row); no dedicated YAML-corrupt-boot test row (checkbox 6 вҖ” covered transitively by `test_endpoint_survives_yaml_missing_at_reload` here, but checkbox 6 will add a startup-path version + a live-Redis cross-worker integration test); no historical token_usage migration row (checkbox 7 вҖ” already honoured by checkbox 2's price-neutral cutover). Commit co-author includes env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: deployed-inactive вҖ” endpoint code is on main, route registers at boot (`POST /api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` with admin gate), the cross-worker callback is registered against shared_state at every worker's import time. Operator runbook entry "when you see llm_pricing fallback warnings in backend logs, edit config/llm_pricing.yaml and POST /runtime/pricing/reload" still needs to be added to docs/ops/ alongside the next live POST in production (the dev shell exercise above used asyncio.run, not an HTTPS round-trip through Caddy + Cloudflare). Z.3 checkbox 5 (`GET /runtime/pricing`) will land alongside the runbook entry so operators can verify the new table state after their POST. Next gate: deployed-active вҖ” operator does one live POST against `https://ai.sora-dev.app/api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` after editing config/llm_pricing.yaml in production and confirms (a) HTTP 200 with the expected providers list, (b) backend logs show the cross-worker reload INFO line on the peer replica (`llm_pricing cross-worker reload received (origin=...)`), (c) a fresh `track_tokens` call after the reload bills at the new rate. ETA single operator POST + 5 min log-grep, no maintenance window required since the underlying loader is already live. -->

- [x] `GET /runtime/pricing` еӣһз•¶еүҚиЎЁ + `updated_at` + source URLгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-5 shipped: new endpoint `GET /api/v1/runtime/pricing` in `backend/routers/system.py` (~17 LOC) backed by new helper `backend/pricing.py::get_pricing_table()` (~40 LOC). **Auth tier**: NO admin gate вҖ” pricing is non-sensitive informational data, matches peer GETs like `/runtime/info` and `/runtime/status`; the router-level `Depends(_auth.current_user)` baseline still applies so unauthenticated callers get 401. Locked by dedicated `test_endpoint_route_does_not_require_admin` that inspects the route's `dependencies` list and asserts `current_user` IS present and `_dep` (the closure produced by `require_role("admin")` in `_REQUIRE_ADMIN`) is NOT present вҖ” a route-definition lock that survives even if a future contributor accidentally adds `dependencies=_REQUIRE_ADMIN`. **Response shape** (4 top-level keys): `providers` map (per-provider, per-model `{input, output}` USD per 1M tokens, including `_default` rows so dashboards can render the provider-level fallback tier alongside exact rates) + `defaults` `{input, output}` global fallback + `metadata` block (`updated_at` + `source` URL + `schema_version` + `notes` round-tripped from the YAML, satisfying the spec's "еӣһз•¶еүҚиЎЁ + `updated_at` + source URL" requirement) + `loaded_from_yaml: bool` so a dashboard can render a degraded-state banner instead of silently showing the boot-safety fallback. **Tuple вҶ’ dict serialization**: rate pairs in the in-memory cache are tuples `(input, output)`; `get_pricing_table()` converts them to `{"input": X, "output": Y}` dicts to mirror the YAML on-disk shape and keep the JSON wire format self-documenting (a bare 2-element list would lose the in/out distinction вҖ” locked by `test_rate_pairs_serialize_as_input_output_dicts`). **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: read-only endpoint hitting the existing `_PRICING_CACHE`. No new module-globals introduced вҖ” the cache + cross-worker reload coherence already audited and documented by checkboxes 2/4. Each worker derives identical state from the same on-disk YAML at first call (audit answer 1) with `POST /runtime/pricing/reload` providing Redis-coordinated invalidation (audit answer 2). The GET endpoint is observation-only so it cannot drift this. **Read-after-write timing audit**: not applicable вҖ” read-only endpoint, no parallel write being introduced. The small reload-propagation window already documented in checkbox 4 (worker B reads stale cache for вүӨ1s after worker A reloaded) means a GET landing on B during that window returns the OLD table вҖ” acceptable per the same Z.3 checkbox 7 rationale ("ж—ўжңү token usage зҙҖйҢ„дёҚйҮҚз®—жҲҗжң¬пјҢдҝқз•ҷз•¶жҷӮиЁҲиІ»пјҢеҸӘеҪұйҹҝжңӘдҫҶиЁҲеғ№"; both old and new are valid prices in those sub-second windows). The operator-facing implication: a GET-after-POST round-trip could see the old table if it lands on a peer worker that hasn't processed the pubsub message yet вҖ” this is documented in the get_pricing_table() docstring so future runbook authors know to expect a brief inconsistency window. **Tests (11 new in `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`, brings file from 34 вҶ’ 45 cases)**: `TestPricingTableHelper` (7) вҖ” full provider map with 9 live providers / rate pairs serialise as `{input, output}` dicts not bare lists / `_default` provider rows surface in the snapshot / global `defaults` present / `metadata` carries `updated_at` + `source` URL substring lock / `loaded_from_yaml=True` under normal boot / `loaded_from_yaml=False` + empty providers when YAML missing (degraded state surfaces, doesn't silently render boot-safety fallback). `TestPricingSnapshotEndpoint` (4) вҖ” endpoint returns 200 with all 4 top-level keys + live YAML loaded + spot-check on anthropic Opus 4.7 rate; **GET-after-POST round-trip** monkeypatches `_PRICING_PATH` to a fake YAML, POSTs `/pricing/reload`, then GETs `/pricing` and asserts the new providers + new defaults + new `updated_at` + new `source` URL appear (locks the operator workflow "edit YAML вҶ’ POST reload вҶ’ GET to verify" end-to-end); endpoint surfaces degraded state with 200 + `loaded_from_yaml: False` + empty providers when YAML missing (so dashboard can render banner); `test_endpoint_route_does_not_require_admin` inspects the route's `dependencies` list and locks the negative assertion (no `_dep` admin gate). 45/45 green on `test_pricing.py` in ~3.7s. **Regression sweep**: 37/37 neighbour tests green across `test_token_budget.py` (6) + `test_forecast.py` (6) + `test_context_limits.py` (25), confirming (a) the new helper does not perturb `_PRICING_CACHE` lookup arithmetic so `track_tokens` still bills bit-identically, (b) `forecast.py`'s `configs/provider_pricing.yaml` loader is completely separate, (c) `context_limits.py`'s sibling YAML loader has no shared state. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/pricing.py` + `backend/routers/system.py` + `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`: 0 hits introduced (pre-existing hit at `backend/routers/system.py:1444` is the alembic_pg_compat comment prose, untouched). **Live smoke** (asyncio.run on `get_pricing_snapshot()` outside the ASGI stack): returns the documented 4-key dict with all 9 providers, `anthropic.claude-opus-4-7 = {input: 5.0, output: 25.0}`, `defaults = {input: 1.0, output: 3.0}`, `metadata.updated_at = '2026-04-25'`, `metadata.source = 'https://github.com/OmniSight/OmniSight-Productizer/issues/292'`, `metadata.schema_version = 1`. **Scope discipline вҖ” what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to its own row): no dedicated YAML-corrupt-boot test row beyond what's covered transitively here (checkbox 6 вҖ” the dedicated test row will add a startup-path version + a live-Redis cross-worker integration test that this row does not exercise); no historical token_usage migration row (checkbox 7 вҖ” already honoured by checkbox 2's price-neutral cutover); no operator runbook entry in `docs/ops/` documenting the "edit YAML вҶ’ POST /pricing/reload вҶ’ GET /pricing to verify" workflow (deferred until checkbox 6 lands so the runbook can document both the reload and the GET in one place вҖ” flagged in HANDOFF). Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: deployed-inactive вҖ” endpoint code is on main, route registers at boot (`GET /api/v1/runtime/pricing` with router-level `current_user` dep, no admin gate). The dev-shell smoke above used asyncio.run on the route function, not an HTTPS round-trip through Caddy + Cloudflare. Operator runbook entry "after POST /pricing/reload, GET /pricing to verify the new updated_at + source + rates landed" still needs to be added to docs/ops/ alongside the next live exercise (deferred to checkbox 6 so reload + GET are documented together).
