The comprehensive matrices already live in the surrounding Z.1 classes; this slim net exists so a future contributor running `pytest -k TestZ5RatelimitRegressionNet` gets a sub-second smoke subset that trips on any of the three load-bearing invariants. **Mirrors the Z.5 `test_pricing.py` + `test_llm_balance.py` pattern**: slim canonical regression class that references the comprehensive suites exist elsewhere but locks the exact regression matrix from the Z.5 checkbox wording. **The three dimensions**: (1) **Header parse (4 providers)** вҖ” `test_four_provider_header_parse`, parametrised over the 4 providers Z.5 calls out (anthropic / openai / deepseek / openrouter) feeding realistic vendor-shaped headers (Anthropic native `anthropic-ratelimit-*` + ISO 8601 absolute reset, OpenAI duration-string `"14ms"` / `"8.64s"`, DeepSeek bare numeric-seconds, OpenRouter minute-duration `"1m"`); asserts (a) the unified `{remaining_requests, remaining_tokens, reset_at_ts, retry_after_s}` contract on `cb.last_ratelimit_state` AND (b) byte-identical mirror under `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")[provider]` via `fresh_kv.get_with_ttl(provider) == cb.last_ratelimit_state` вҖ” catches mapping schema drift (`_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` vendor rename, accidental drop of one of the 4 providers, in-memoryвҶ”KV divergence). (2) **TTL expiry** вҖ” `test_ttl_expiry_60s_end_to_end`, `monkeypatch.setattr(time, "time", lambda: 1000.0)` write via real `on_llm_end` round-trip with Anthropic headers; live at t+30s (`get_with_ttl(now=1030.0) is not None`); pruned at t+61s (`get_with_ttl(now=1061.0) is None`); destructive-prune assert (`get(default="__missing__") == "__missing__"`); locks the constant `_RATELIMIT_TTL_SECONDS == 60.0` to catch a silent retune (someone bumps it to 5 min for "convenience" and breaks the dashboard's freshness guarantee). (3) **Unknown provider skip** вҖ” `test_unknown_provider_skip_preserves_siblings`, Ollama turn (provider absent from `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS`) interleaved between live anthropic + openai turns; (a) `set(snap.keys()) == {"anthropic", "openai"}` вҖ” no key under "ollama"; (b) sibling payloads intact with their correct `remaining_requests` (987 for anthropic / 9998 for openai). Catches both "wrote {} under unknown key" and "wiped sibling on every turn" regressions. **Module-global state audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-global mutable state; tests reuse the existing `fresh_kv` fixture (per-test `monkeypatch.setattr(_llm_mod, "_ratelimit_kv_singleton", None)` + purge in-memory residue) + `silent_track_tokens` fixture (per-test `monkeypatch.setattr(_sys, "track_tokens", ...)` stub). `time.time` monkeypatch is scoped to the individual test via pytest's `monkeypatch` fixture (auto-rolled-back at teardown) вҖ” no cross-test clock pollution. Per the SOP rubric: answer #1 ("derived from shared static source") for the static `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` mapping every worker re-derives, and answer #3 ("deliberately per-worker") for the in-memory SharedKV fallback the test exercises (Redis-coordinated in production per the SharedKV contract). **Read-after-write audit**: N/A вҖ” single-process test; the SharedKV writes are read by the same test thread via `get_with_ttl` / `get_all_with_ttl` on the same KV instance; no asyncio gather, no cross-worker timing. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (SOP Step 3): clean вҖ” 0 hits on `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`. **Test result**: 24/24 green in 0.50s on the full `test_ratelimit_capture.py` (18 pre-existing Z.1 + 6 new Z.5); 165/165 green across the five rate-limit sibling suites (`test_ratelimit_capture` + `test_ratelimit_header_extract` + `test_ratelimit_header_normalize` + `test_ratelimit_kv_write` + `test_ratelimit_boundaries`) in 0.79s вҖ” 0 regressions. **Scope discipline вҖ” what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to sibling Z.5 rows): (a) no `docs/integrations/llm-observability.md` support-matrix docs (Z.5 row 4, separate checkbox); (b) no operator UI / reload / budget-alarm docs (Z.5 row 5, separate checkbox); (c) no HANDOFF before/after table for Z completion (Z.5 row 6, separate checkbox); (d) no production code changes вҖ” the spec calls for tests only, no new module-level API in `backend/agents/llm.py`. The comprehensive Z.1 suite kept in the same file (18 cases spanning the 5 classes above) remains the authoritative contract surface; the Z.5 regression net is the canonical smoke subset. Commit co-authors include env-user (Agent-software-beta) + global-user (nanakusa sora) per CLAUDE.md. -->

  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.5-checkbox-4 shipped: created `docs/integrations/llm-observability.md` (~165 LOC, 5 sections) вҖ” a single reference page that answers "I configured an API key for provider X, what will the dashboard show me?" **Scope of this checkbox**: the Z.5 spec wording is "еҲ—еҮәж”ҜжҸҙеәҰзҹ©йҷЈ" (list the support matrix) вҖ” the matrix itself is the load-bearing deliverable; everything else is framing to make the matrix interpretable to a future operator who has not read the Z.1 / Z.2 / Z.3 / Z.4 commit threads. **Explicit scope discipline вҖ” what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to sibling Z.5 rows): (a) operator UI walkthrough / manual price-table reload instructions / per-provider budget alarm wiring (Z.5 row 5, separate checkbox вҖ” will extend this same file); (b) HANDOFF before/after table (Z.5 row 6, separate checkbox). The doc's intro explicitly flags "Downstream checkboxes in Z.5 layer an operator how-to on top вҖ” see Z.5 row in TODO.md for sibling scope boundaries" so a future reader lands on the right row without re-discovering the decomposition. **Matrix fidelity вҖ” cross-reference against live runtime registries**: (1) `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` in `backend/agents/llm.py:117-165` carries exactly 7 providers вҖ” anthropic / openai / xai / groq / deepseek / together / openrouter вҖ” matching the вң… + вҡ пёҸ rows in the matrix. Google is intentionally absent (inline comment at `backend/agents/llm.py:160-164` explains langchain-google-genai adapter does not surface x-goog-quota-* headers) вҖ” this is what the вҡ пёҸ in the matrix's Google row refers to. Ollama is absent (local deployment, no remote rate) вҖ” matches the вқҢ in the matrix's Ollama row. (2) `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` in `backend/llm_balance.py:342-348` carries exactly 2 providers вҖ” deepseek / openrouter вҖ” matching the вң… rows in the matrix's Balance API column; the `is_balance_supported()` predicate at `backend/llm_balance.py:351-358` is the runtime check that drives the `{status: "unsupported"}` envelope the router returns for the other 7. (3) `config/llm_pricing.yaml:30-93` carries exactly 9 providers вҖ” the 7 ratelimit + google (no ratelimit) + ollama (no remote anything) вҖ” matching the 9 rows of the matrix. **Section 2 ("What the two columns actually mean")** is essential because the raw matrix is ambiguous without definitions: вң… / вҡ пёҸ / вқҢ / N/A each need a runtime criterion a future maintainer can apply when adding a provider. вң… = "API-key auth to a public HTTP endpoint" (the registry gate); вҡ пёҸ = "signal appears for some models / SDK versions / request shapes"; вқҢ = "provider exposes the data but only behind auth path OmniSight does not implement" (session cookie, GCP OAuth, dashboard scrape вҖ” cross-references the TODO Non-goals); N/A = "signal is definitionally meaningless" (local provider). **Section 3 ("How the matrix maps to the dashboard")** bridges the developer-visible matrix to the operator-visible UI: Balance column вҶ’ Providers panel Balance column; Rate-Limit column вҶ’ Providers panel Rate-limit badge; non-grey rows count toward the Z.4 roll-up tile denominator (Ollama excluded so local-only deployments do not see permanent "1/9 healthy" red). **Section 4 ("Adding a new provider")** gives the 4-step recipe a future contributor needs: pricing YAML вҶ’ rate-limit headers mapping вҶ’ balance fetcher вҶ’ matrix row. Ordered the same as the Z.1 / Z.2 / Z.3 checkbox sequence so "add a provider" is a recognisable mini-Z. **Section 5 ("Related files")** lists the 7 files a reader might want to cross-reference вҖ” the two registries (authoritative truth), the pricing YAML, the two lifespan-scoped components (refresher + endpoint router), the two test files. **Module-global state audit (SOP Step 1)**: N/A вҖ” pure docs, no production code, no tests. **Read-after-write audit**: N/A. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (SOP Step 3): clean вҖ” 0 hits on `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` (expected вҖ” it's a markdown file). **Consistency with existing docs**: matches the style of `docs/integrations/jira-automation.md` (the only other file under `docs/integrations/`) вҖ” H1 title, "> Y-prep.3 (#289) вҖ” operator-facing reference" lead-in pattern, numbered H2 sections, tables with leading/trailing pipes, back-ticks for code identifiers. Used the same front-matter-style `> Z.5 (#294) checkbox 4 вҖ” canonical reference` so operators can locate the provenance without grepping. **Doc discipline вҖ” what the 165 LOC deliberately does NOT become**: (a) no line-by-line walkthrough of `_extract_response_headers` parse logic (that's the test file's job); (b) no example `curl` calls against each provider's balance endpoint (vendor-specific, drift-prone вҖ” the fetcher source code is canonical); (c) no per-model latency / cost benchmarks (future Z-follow-up if ever); (d) no vendor dashboard URL clickthrough list (deferred to Z.5 row 5 "еёёиҰӢ provider dashboard йҖЈзөҗжё…е–®" вҖ” a deliberate rough edge so the next checkbox has a concrete deliverable). Last-verified timestamp + instruction to re-verify on provider add/rename/remove is the ONE operator burden the doc creates вҖ” everything else is cross-referable from the runtime registries. Commit co-authors include env-user (Agent-software-beta) + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->

## рҹҶүрҹҶү Priority ZZ вҖ” Claude-Code-Style Agent ObservabilityпјҲжҠҠ agent й»‘зӣ’й–ӢжҲҗзҺ»з’ғзӣ’пјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҲ2026-04-22 operator auditпјүпјҡoperator и©•дј°дәҶ [lis186/ccxray](https://github.com/lis186/ccxray)пјҲClaude Code transparent proxy + е„ҖиЎЁжқҝпјҢжҠҠ Claude Code зҡ„ LLM йҖҡиЁҠ intercept дёӢдҫҶз•¶гҖҢзҺ»з’ғзӣ’гҖҚеұ•зӨәпјҡper-turn еҚЎзүҮгҖҒcontext window barгҖҒcache hit %гҖҒinter-turn gapгҖҒtool failureгҖҒsystem prompt diffгҖҒsession heatmapгҖҒburn rateпјүвҖ”вҖ”и«ӢжұӮжҠҠжүҖжңүйҒ©з”Ёзҡ„еҠҹиғҪжҗ¬йҖІ OmniSightгҖӮOmniSight зӣ®еүҚеҸӘжңү `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` зҡ„ per-model еҚЎпјҲinput / output / total / cost / req count / avg latency / last usedпјүпјҢзңӢдёҚеҲ°е–®дёҖ turn зҡ„ж•…дәӢгҖҒзңӢдёҚеҲ° cache ж•ҲзҺҮгҖҒзңӢдёҚеҲ° context жҺҘиҝ‘дёҠйҷҗгҖӮ
>
> ZZ е’Ң ZпјҲLLM Provider ObservabilityпјҢйӨҳйЎҚ / rate-limitпјүжҳҜе§ҠеҰ№пјҡ**Z и§ЈгҖҢprovider йӮЈйӮҠйӮ„еү©д»ҖйәјгҖҚпјҢZZ и§ЈгҖҢжҲ‘йҖҷз«ҜжҖҺйәјзҮ’гҖҚ**гҖӮе…©иҖ…е…ұз”Ёж—ўжңү `TokenTrackingCallback` + `SharedTokenUsage` infraпјҢдҪҶијёеҮәз¶ӯеәҰдёҚеҗҢвҖ”вҖ”Z жҳҜ per-provider summaryгҖҒZZ жҳҜ per-turn timeline + session heatmap + prompt diffгҖӮ
>
> **иҗҪең°дҪҚзҪ®**пјҡж“ҙе……ж—ўжңү `components/omnisight/orchestrator-ai.tsx`пјҲORCHESTRATOR AI йқўжқҝпјҢе·І mount `<TokenUsageStats>`пјҢжҳҜ dashboard дёҠ "зңӢ LLM жҖқиҖғ + token" зҡ„дё»иҰҒиҰ–зӘ—пјүпјҢиҖҢйқһж–°й–ӢзҚЁз«Ӣ panelвҖ”вҖ”йҒҝе…Қе’Ң TokenUsageStats йҮҚиӨҮгҖӮ
>
> **зӣёдҫқ**пјҡ`TokenTrackingCallback`пјҲbackend/agents/llm.pyпјүвң… / `SharedTokenUsage`пјҲshared_state.pyпјүвң… / `prompt_versions` tableпјҲSP-5.2 е·ІиҗҪең°пјүвң… / `GraphState.tool_results`пјҲagents/graph.pyпјүвң… / I2 RLS ContextVar вң…гҖӮзЎ¬зӣёдҫқйӣ¶гҖӮ
>
> **жҳҺзўәжҺ’йҷӨзҡ„ ccxray еҠҹиғҪ**пјҲNon-goalsпјүпјҡ
> - вқҢ **Plan detection (Pro / Max 5x / Max 20x)**вҖ”вҖ”OmniSight з”Ё API key зӣҙйҖЈ providerпјҢжІ’жңүгҖҢClaude Code иЁӮй–ұ planгҖҚжҰӮеҝөпјӣйЎһдјјгҖҢйӮ„жңүеӨҡе°‘ creditгҖҚзҡ„йңҖжұӮз”ұ Z.2 (DeepSeek / OpenRouter balance API) ж¶өи“ӢгҖӮ
> - вқҢ **зҖҸиҰҪеҷЁй ҒзұӨ pulse + desktop notif**вҖ”вҖ”UX иЈқйЈҫпјҢйқһ core observabilityпјӣиӢҘеҫҢзәҢжғіеҠ е°ұиө° Priority Q жҲ–зҚЁз«Ӣе°Ҹй …гҖӮ
> - вқҢ **йҚөзӣӨе°ҺиҰҪ cheatsheet**вҖ”вҖ”йҖҡз”ЁеҘҪй»һеӯҗдҪҶдёҚеұ¬ ZZ зҜ„еңҚпјӣе·Іжңү `components/omnisight/command-palette.tsx` иҷ•зҗҶйғЁеҲҶйҖҷйЎһйңҖжұӮгҖӮ
> - вқҢ **Transparent proxy / еӨ–йғЁ HTTP intercept**вҖ”вҖ”ccxray зҡ„жӢӣзүҢжһ¶ж§ӢеҸӘеңЁгҖҢжҲ‘дёҚжҺ§еҲ¶ agentпјҢиҰҒеҫһеӨ–йғЁи§ҖеҜҹгҖҚжҷӮжңүж„Ҹзҫ©пјӣOmniSight жң¬дҫҶе°ұж“Ғжңү agent еҹ·иЎҢжөҒпјҢзӣҙжҺҘеҫһ `TokenTrackingCallback` еҹӢй»һжҜ” proxy жӣҙжә–зўәгҖҒз„ЎжөҒйҮҸй–ӢйҠ·гҖӮ

еҫҢз«Ҝ response иЈЎе·Із¶“жңүгҖҒеҸӘжҳҜеүҚз«ҜжІ’жёІжҹ“зҡ„еӣӣеҖӢз¶ӯеәҰгҖӮеҒҡе®ҢеҫҢ `<TokenUsageStats>` еҚЎзүҮеӨҡеӣӣжўқиіҮиЁҠдё”дёҖзңјзңӢеҫ—жҮӮгҖӮ

- [x] UI еҚЎзүҮ Row 2 ж–°еўһе…©жўқйҖІеәҰжўқпјҡгҖҢCACHE HIT 72%гҖҚпјҲз¶  > 50% / й»ғ 20-50% / зҙ… < 20%пјү+ tooltip йЎҜзӨә `read: 12.5k / write: 1.2k`пјӣraw ж•ёеӯ—ж”ҫ hoverгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A1 checkbox 4: `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` gains a new "Row 3b" cache block per model card rendered after the existing usage bar (Row 3) and before Input/Output Tokens (Row 4) вҖ” keeps cache info in the visual hierarchy "percent-of-total вҶ’ cache effectiveness вҶ’ raw counts". **Bar 1 (CACHE HIT)**: width = `cacheHitRatio * 100`, color banded per spec вҖ” `> 50` `var(--validation-emerald)`, `>= 20` `#eab308` (yellow-500), `< 20` `var(--critical-red)` (boundary semantics: strict `>` at 50 puts the 50.0 edge on yellow; `>= 20` keeps 20.0 on yellow, matches "з¶  > 50 / й»ғ 20-50 / зҙ… < 20" literal reading). Label reads "CACHE HIT" + right-aligned `N%` percent in the band color. **Bar 2 (CACHE WRITE)**: width = `cache_create / (input + cache_read + cache_create) * 100` capped at 100 вҖ” surfaces "cache-creation overhead paid this window" in `var(--hardware-orange)/70` (neutral-warm, non-alarm). Label reads "CACHE WRITE" + right-aligned K-formatted raw count. **NULL degradation** (pre-ZZ legacy rows + synthesised `configuredProviders` placeholder cards): all three cache fields come through as `null` вҶ’ percent renders as literal em-dash `"вҖ”"` instead of `0%`, color falls back to `var(--muted-foreground)`, both bars render at `0%` width on an empty rail. This makes "no data" visually distinct from a real zero hit-rate per ZZ.A1 contract (the whole reason the backend preserves NULL on pre-ZZ rows). **Tooltips**: container-level `title` carries the verbose raw form (`cache_read_tokens=12512 cache_create_tokens=1203 cache_hit_ratio=0.7200`) satisfying "raw ж•ёеӯ—ж”ҫ hover"; per-bar `title` carries the K-formatted summary (`read: 12.5k / write: 1.2k`) matching the spec example вҖ” operators hovering anywhere on the section get the exact counts, hovering on a bar gets the quick glance. `data-testid` attributes (`cache-hit-section` / `cache-hit-pct` / `cache-hit-bar` / `cache-hit-bar-rail` / `cache-write-bar` / `cache-write-bar-rail`) seed the not-yet-written frontend vitest (next checkbox row) without introducing a test dep now. No API schema change вҖ” reads `item.cacheHitRatio` / `item.cacheReadTokens` / `item.cacheCreateTokens` already present on `ModelTokenUsage` from checkbox-3. `tsc --noEmit` baseline 3 pre-existing errors in `integration-settings.tsx` (TS2322 `GitForgeTokenTestResult` vs `TestResult` index-signature mismatch, unrelated); post-change: still 3 вҖ” 0 new type errors in `token-usage-stats.tsx` or any consumer. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->

- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡ`test_token_usage_shared.py` ж–° 3 йЎҶпјҲcache shape normalise Anthropic vs OpenAIгҖҒNULL зӣёе®№гҖҒhit ratio иЁҲз®—пјүпјӣеүҚз«Ҝ vitest ж–° 2 йЎҶпјҲbadge йЎҸиүІ Г—3 еҚҖй–“гҖҒNULL йҷҚзҙҡйЎҜзӨәгҖҢвҖ”гҖҚпјүгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A1 checkbox 5 (tests row): new file `backend/tests/test_token_usage_shared.py` scopes the three ZZ.A1 regression guards the spec calls out вҖ” (1) `TestCacheShapeNormalise::test_normalise_anthropic_vs_openai_shape` feeds both provider response shapes (Anthropic flat `cache_read_input_tokens`/`cache_creation_input_tokens` and OpenAI nested `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`) through `TokenTrackingCallback._extract_cache_tokens` and asserts both land as int-typed `(read, create)` tuples (OpenAI's missing creation side normalises to 0 so downstream callers don't branch); (2) `TestNullCompat::test_get_all_surfaces_null_for_pre_zz_row` seeds a legacy Redis-shaped dict with no `cache_*` keys and confirms `SharedTokenUsage.get_all()` returns `None` for all three cache fields (not 0 вҖ” that's the whole point of the "distinguish legacy from genuine-zero" contract, so the UI can render "вҖ”"); (3) `TestHitRatioCalculation::test_hit_ratio_anthropic_and_openai_land_same_formula` locks `hit_ratio = cache_read / (input + cache_read)` end-to-end for both providers вҖ” Anthropic 800/(200+800)=0.8, OpenAI 300/(1000+300)вүҲ0.230769 вҖ” so a future formula refactor can't silently drift. New frontend file `test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` scopes the two UI regression guards вҖ” (1) `CACHE HIT badge colour bands` renders three `ModelTokenUsage` rows (0.72/0.35/0.10 вҶ’ 72% green > 50 / 35% yellow 20-50 / 10% red < 20) and asserts both the `[data-testid='cache-hit-pct']` text + `style.color` and the matching `[data-testid='cache-hit-bar']` fill colour land on `var(--validation-emerald)` / `rgb(234,179,8)` (jsdom-normalised #eab308) / `var(--critical-red)` per band вҖ” boundary semantics `>50` + `>=20` matches the spec reading; (2) `NULL degradation renders em-dash` feeds a pre-ZZ row with `cacheReadTokens/cacheCreateTokens/cacheHitRatio = null`, asserts the pct label renders literal "вҖ”" in `var(--muted-foreground)` (explicitly NOT any of the three band colours вҖ” "no data" must not masquerade as a bad hit rate), the fill bar width degrades to `0%`, and the section-level `title` attribute carries the "no cache data" tooltip. **Test results**: 3/3 backend tests green, 2/2 frontend tests green. Full `test_token_usage_shared + test_shared_state + test_token_budget + test_db_token_usage` suite: 68 passed, 10 skipped (PG-only `test_db_token_usage` вҖ” unchanged baseline), 0 regression. Frontend `metric-sparkline + token-usage-stats-cache` co-run: 6 passed. SOP Step 4 (unit tests + fingerprint grep) clean вҖ” no `_conn()` / `conn.commit()` / SQLite-isms in new files. No prod code touched in this row вҖ” all lifting is in the two new test files alone. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->

#### ZZ.A2 Context window usage bar (#303-2)
- [x] ж–° `config/context_window_limits.yaml`пјҲе’Ң Z.3 зҡ„ pricing YAML еҗҢдҪҚзҪ®пјүпјҢиҰҶи“Ӣ 9 е®¶дё»иҰҒ model зҡ„ context дёҠйҷҗпјҲe.g. Claude 4 = 1M, GPT-4o = 128k, Gemini Pro = 2M, DeepSeek = 64k, Groq = 32k, Ollama = жЁЎеһӢзӣёдҫқпјүгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 1: new file `configs/context_window_limits.yaml` (placed alongside `configs/provider_pricing.yaml` per "е’Ң Z.3 зҡ„ pricing YAML еҗҢдҪҚзҪ®"; spec text says `config/` but the actual project dir is `configs/` вҖ” chose the real path so the forthcoming `backend/context_limits.py` can mirror `backend/forecast.py::_PRICING_PATH = _PROJECT_ROOT / "configs" / "provider_pricing.yaml"` pattern exactly, else we get asymmetric YAML locations nobody remembers). Structure is `provider вҶ’ model_id вҶ’ int | null` with a per-provider `default` row so the next-row `get_context_limit(provider, model) -> int | None` can do a two-step lookup (exact вҶ’ provider-default вҶ’ None). **All 9 providers from provider_pricing.yaml covered** (anthropic / openai / google / xai / groq / deepseek / together / ollama / openrouter) keeping the two YAMLs in 1:1 sync so nobody has to reconcile "this provider exists in one file but not the other" later. **Values authoritative as of 2026-04 per vendor docs**: Claude 4.x family (opus-4-7 / sonnet-4-6 / haiku-4-5) = 1M; Claude 3.x = 200k; GPT-5 family = 256k; GPT-4o family = 128k; o1/o3 reasoning = 200k; Gemini 2.5 Pro / 1.5 Pro = 2M; Gemini 2.5 Flash / 2.0 Flash / 1.5 Flash = 1M; Grok 4 = 256k, Grok 3/2 = 131072; Groq-hosted Llama-3.3/3.1-versatile = 131072, Mixtral-32768 = 32768, Gemma = 8192; DeepSeek V3/R1 = 65536; Together hosts multi-vendor OSS, common routes listed (Llama 3.x = 131072, Qwen 2.5 = 32768, DeepSeek V3 = 65536, Mixtral = 32768); Ollama local models listed with upstream max (llama3.3/3.1 = 131072, llama3 = 8192, mistral = 32768, qwen2.5-coder = 32768, etc.) **with `default: null`** because local `num_ctx` is operator-configurable вҖ” matches spec "Ollama = жЁЎеһӢзӣёдҫқ" and lets UI render "вҖ”" instead of a fake %; OpenRouter same pattern (`default: null`) because it's a pass-through proxy and the real limit depends on the routed-to model, listed common routes (anthropic/openai/google upstream) so the happy path still gets a real %. **NULL semantics**: `default: null` вҶ’ callee (ZZ.A2 checkbox 2's `backend/context_limits.py::get_context_limit`) returns `None` вҶ’ UI (ZZ.A2 checkbox 4's Row 3 progress bar) must degrade to "вҖ”" in muted-foreground per the same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 already established for cache fields. **Env-var escape hatch**: Ollama block comment flags `OMNISIGHT_OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LIMIT` as the per-deployment override knob so operators running tighter `num_ctx` than upstream max don't get a misleading bar вҖ” exact env-var plumbing lands in ZZ.A2 checkbox 2 (where the Python loader has the code surface to honour it). **Validation**: 9/9 expected providers present, every entry `int > 0` or (for `default`) `int > 0` or literal `None`, per-vendor sanity-checked against spec (`claude-opus-4-7 == 1_000_000`, `gpt-4o == 128_000`, `gemini-2.5-pro == 2_000_000`, `deepseek.default == 65_536`, `groq.default == 32_768`, `ollama.default is None`). Total entries: 92 across 9 providers (anthropic 12, openai 13, google 8, xai 7, groq 9, deepseek 6, together 9, ollama 14, openrouter 14). No Python loader or UI consumer wired yet вҖ” intentionally scope-limited to "data file only", per SOP "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] `backend/pricing.py`пјҲZ.3 е»әз«Ӣзҡ„пјүеҠ  sibling `backend/context_limits.py::get_context_limit(provider, model) -> int | None`гҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 2: new file `backend/context_limits.py` (~130 LOC) вҖ” spec text says "sibling to backend/pricing.py" but the actual pricing loader lives in `backend/forecast.py::_load_pricing()` (Z.3 never factored out a standalone `pricing.py`), so "sibling" is read as "same pattern, same YAML directory convention, same cache discipline" вҖ” module sits next to forecast.py under `backend/`, mirrors the `_PROJECT_ROOT / "configs" / "*.yaml"` path anchor, and reuses the lazy-global cache idiom (`_LIMITS_CACHE` filled on first call, reset_cache_for_tests hook for test isolation). **Public API**: single function `get_context_limit(provider: str | None, model: str | None) -> int | None` with the two-step lookup the YAML header documents вҖ” exact model-id under provider вҶ’ provider-level `default` вҶ’ None. Unknown provider, empty/None provider, `default: null`, and blank inputs all route to `None` so downstream callers can unambiguously render "вҖ”" per the ZZ.A1-established NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract. Provider key is lowercased so `Anthropic` / `ANTHROPIC` / `anthropic` all resolve (real-world callers hand us env-var casing). Model key is case-sensitive вҖ” `together` / `openrouter` use slash-path keys like `meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo` whose case matters to the upstream API, matching the YAML as authored. **Ollama env override**: `OMNISIGHT_OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LIMIT` (flagged in `configs/context_window_limits.yaml` comment as the per-deployment escape hatch) is honoured in `get_context_limit` вҖ” when set on `provider=='ollama'`, the env value beats both the YAML's per-model entry and the `default: null`. Invalid values (empty / non-numeric / вүӨ 0) silently fall through to YAML so a typoed knob does not zero-out the UI. Override is strictly scoped to `provider=='ollama'` вҖ” asserts no leakage to anthropic/openai in the test suite. **Loader robustness**: missing YAML, corrupt YAML, and null entries all coerce to `None` rather than raising вҖ” the forecast loader's same defensive posture ("pricing.yaml missing should not kill the forecast endpoint" вҶ’ "limit lookup missing should not kill the turn_metrics SSE emitter"). Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1: `_LIMITS_CACHE` is module-level but derived purely from a bundled, read-only YAML вҖ” each uvicorn worker computes the same value from the same file, matches the "derived from shared static source" escape hatch (answer 1 of the three valid answers), docstring records the decision. **Tests** (`backend/tests/test_context_limits.py`, 25 cases in 6 TestCase classes): exact-match for the four headline models (claude-opus-4-7=1M, gpt-4o=128k, gemini-2.5-pro=2M, quoted-slash keys for together + openrouter) + provider-default fallback (unknown model, empty model, None model) + NULL semantics (ollama unknown, openrouter unknown, unknown-provider, empty/None provider) + case-insensitive provider (Anthropic/OPENAI) + Ollama env override (beats known model, beats NULL default, scoped to ollama, garbage ignored, empty ignored, zero ignored) + YAML loader robustness (missing-file degrades to None, corrupt YAML degrades to None, null entries coerce to None via a custom tmp_path YAML). All 25/25 green. Neighbour sanity: `test_forecast.py` 6/6 co-green, 31/31 total. Pre-commit fingerprint grep (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in both new files. **Scope discipline**: file is strictly the lookup helper вҖ” next-row ZZ.A2 checkbox 3 will wire it into `TokenTrackingCallback` + SSE `turn_metrics`; checkbox 4 into the UI progress bar. No wiring in this commit. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] per-turn иЁҳйҢ„ `tokens_used / context_limit` вҶ’ з•¶еүҚ turn зҡ„ context usage % жҺЁеӣһ SSE event `turn_metrics`гҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 3: new SSE event `turn_metrics` wires the just-landed ZZ.A2 checkbox-1 YAML (`configs/context_window_limits.yaml`) + checkbox-2 loader (`backend/context_limits.py::get_context_limit`) into the ZZ.A1 token-callback pipeline. **Schema** (`backend/sse_schemas.py`): new `SSETurnMetrics` Pydantic model with 10 payload fields (`provider` / `model` / `input_tokens` / `output_tokens` / `tokens_used` / `context_limit` / `context_usage_pct` / `latency_ms` / `cache_read_tokens` / `cache_create_tokens`) + `timestamp` auto-stamped by the bus. `tokens_used` is the pre-computed `input + output` sum so the UI progress bar numerator is one field-read away; `context_usage_pct` is the pre-computed `round(tokens_used / context_limit * 100, 2)` percentage. Registered under `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["turn_metrics"]` so the runtime schema export (`GET /api/v1/runtime/sse-schema`) advertises it for the frontend codegen. **Emit helper** (`backend/events.py::emit_turn_metrics`): matches the existing `emit_*` family signature вҖ” explicit `broadcast_scope=None` default + `_resolve_scope("emit_turn_metrics", ..., "global")` legacy fallback so post-Q.4 strict mode (`OMNISIGHT_SSE_SCOPE_STRICT=1`) stays green when the callback passes `broadcast_scope="global"` explicitly. **NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract**: `context_limit=None` (unknown provider/model / Ollama without env override / OpenRouter pass-through) вҶ’ `context_usage_pct=None` (NOT 0.0 вҖ” the whole point of the NULL contract ZZ.A1 established for prompt-cache fields is that the UI can render "вҖ”" distinguishable from a real 0% turn). `context_limit <= 0` also degrades to `None` to defend against ZeroDivisionError / nonsense percentages. **Callback wire-up** (`backend/agents/llm.py`): `TokenTrackingCallback.__init__` gains optional `provider: str | None = None` kwarg (back-compat вҖ” legacy `TokenTrackingCallback(model_name)` fixtures keep working, limit lookup returns None); `get_llm` callsite passes the resolved provider through so post-failover (`chain[fallback_provider]`) the provider string is authoritative. `on_llm_end` after the existing `track_tokens(...)` call: imports `get_context_limit` + `emit_turn_metrics` late (avoids import-cycle with `routers.system`), computes the limit, emits with `broadcast_scope="global"`. Emit is wrapped in its own try/except with `logger.debug` on failure вҖ” an emit hiccup must never break the LLM turn. **Tests** (`backend/tests/test_turn_metrics_sse.py`, 6 cases): canonical payload shape (happy path, 10 fields + timestamp) / NULL context_limit degrades pct to None / zero-limit defensive branch / end-to-end callback via stubbed `track_tokens` + fake LLMResult (claude-opus-4-7 вҶ’ 1M limit вҶ’ 25.00% pct) / back-compat callback without provider (limit=None, pct=None, tokens_used still populates) / schema registry drift guard (`SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["turn_metrics"] is SSETurnMetrics` + 10 required field names present). **Schema drift guard** (`backend/tests/test_schema.py::TestSSESchemaExport::test_all_events_have_schemas`): expected-set updated to include `turn_metrics` (and the pre-existing Q.3-SUB family `workflow_updated` / `notification.read` / `preferences.updated` / `integration.settings.updated` / `chat.message` which were pre-existing drift вҖ” without them the test was already red before this change, listing them here is the minimum needed to keep the drift guard green after the new entry lands). **Test results**: 6/6 new tests green; neighbour sanity 55/55 across `test_turn_metrics_sse + test_token_usage_shared + test_context_limits + test_events_bus + test_token_budget + test_schema.py::TestSSESchemaExport`; 0 regression. Smoke: `get_sse_schema_export()` round-trip includes `turn_metrics` with `SSETurnMetrics` title + 11 properties keyed; `emit_turn_metrics("claude-opus-4-7", input_tokens=10000, output_tokens=2000, latency_ms=1234, provider="anthropic", context_limit=1_000_000, broadcast_scope="global")` lands `context_usage_pct=1.20` on the bus. `scripts/dump_openapi.py --check` clean вҖ” SSE schema export is runtime-only (not part of OpenAPI), so no snapshot regen needed. Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1: no new module-global state introduced вҖ” `emit_turn_metrics` is stateless, `SSETurnMetrics` is a Pydantic class definition, `TokenTrackingCallback.provider` is an instance field (per-request scoped), `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS` is a read-only registry dict initialised at import. SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in `backend/agents/llm.py`, `backend/events.py`, `backend/sse_schemas.py`, `backend/tests/test_turn_metrics_sse.py`. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] UI еҚЎзүҮ Row 3 еҠ ж°ҙе№і progress bar + зҷҫеҲҶжҜ”пјҡ<50% з¶  / 50-75% й»ғ / 75-90% ж©ҷ / >90% зҙ…и„ҲеӢ•еӢ•з•«пјӣhover йЎҜзӨәгҖҢ123k / 200k tokensгҖҚгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 4: `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` gains a new "Row 3a (context window)" block per model card, rendered between the existing Row 3 (`% of total usage` cross-model proportion bar) and Row 3b (cache hit/write bars from ZZ.A1 checkbox 4). Self-contained data flow вҖ” no use-engine wiring needed: the component subscribes to the shared `EventSource` via `subscribeEvents(...)` in a `useEffect` (cleaned up on unmount via `handle.close()`) and maintains a local `Record<modelLowercase, { tokensUsed, contextLimit, contextUsagePct }>` keyed by the same lowercased-model convention `usedModels` already uses. **Snapshot semantics**: backend emits `turn_metrics` SSE per-turn (ZZ.A2 checkbox 3), and the component keeps only the *latest* per model вҖ” lifetime totals would balloon as the agent keeps talking and lose the "is THIS turn near the cap" signal the bar exists to surface. **Color bands per spec**: `pct < 50` вҶ’ `var(--validation-emerald)` (green) / `< 75` вҶ’ `#eab308` (yellow-500) / `< 90` вҶ’ `var(--hardware-orange)` (orange) / `>= 90` вҶ’ `var(--critical-red)` (red) with Tailwind `animate-pulse` on the fill div for the >= 90 band вҖ” boundary semantics use strict `<` upper / inclusive `>=` lower so 50.0 вҶ’ yellow, 75.0 вҶ’ orange, 90.0 вҶ’ red, mirroring the cache-bar band reading. **Hover tooltip**: rail-level `title` carries the spec-mandated `"123k / 200k tokens"` form via `formatTokens(tokensUsed) / formatTokens(contextLimit) tokens` (e.g. `"45.0K / 1.0M tokens"` for a Claude turn at 45k/1M); when `context_limit` is `null` (Ollama local without env override / OpenRouter pass-through / unknown provider) the tooltip degrades to `"N tokens used (context limit unknown for this provider/model)"` so operators see the limit-unknown reason rather than an em-dash; when no `turn_metrics` SSE has landed yet (fresh card / configured-provider placeholder) the tooltip says `"no context data вҖ” no turn_metrics seen yet"`. **NULL degradation**: percent label renders literal `"вҖ”"` in `var(--muted-foreground)` and the bar fill renders `0%` width on an empty rail вҖ” same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 established for cache fields, so a "no data" card is visually distinct from a real 0% turn (e.g. an immediately-after-reset card where the agent literally sent zero tokens). **SSE wiring** (`lib/api.ts`): new `turn_metrics` entry added to both the `SSEEvent` discriminated-union (carries `provider/model/input_tokens/output_tokens/tokens_used/context_limit/context_usage_pct/latency_ms/cache_read_tokens/cache_create_tokens/timestamp` matching `backend/sse_schemas.py::SSETurnMetrics` field-for-field, with the two `*_limit/_pct` fields nullable) and the `SSE_EVENT_TYPES` whitelist (`addEventListener` only registers the listed types; without this entry the shared `EventSource` would silently drop `turn_metrics` frames before any subscriber sees them). **`data-testid` attributes** seeded for the next-checkbox-row vitest (`context-usage-section` / `context-usage-pct` / `context-usage-bar` / `context-usage-bar-rail`) following the same convention the cache section uses for `token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx`. **Type baseline**: `tsc --noEmit` shows the same 3 pre-existing `integration-settings.tsx` errors as the ZZ.A1 baseline (TS2322 `GitForgeTokenTestResult` vs `TestResult` index-signature mismatch, unrelated); 0 new type errors in `token-usage-stats.tsx` or `lib/api.ts`. Cache test (`test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx`, 2/2) co-runs green вҖ” no regression. Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1: `_sharedES` / `_sseListeners` in `lib/api.ts` are existing module-globals (not new) вҖ” they're per-frontend-process only (browser tab), not per-uvicorn-worker, so the SOP escape hatch doesn't apply (the worker-fan-out concern is backend-only). Component-local `contextByModel` is per-mount React state (per-tab, not global). Scope discipline: warning icon on >90% (next checkbox) and frontend tests for color bands + NULL degradation (checkbox after) intentionally NOT touched вҖ” strict "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] еҚЎзүҮй ӮйғЁеҰӮжһңд»»дёҖжңҖиҝ‘ turn > 90% context з”ЁйҮҸпјҢеҠ иӯҰе‘Ҡ icon + tooltipгҖҢContext жҺҘиҝ‘дёҠйҷҗпјҢagent еҸҜиғҪ truncateгҖҚгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 5: `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` gains a card-top warning icon next to the "TOKEN USAGE" header text. **Logic**: scans the component-local `contextByModel` state (populated by the ZZ.A2 checkbox-4 `turn_metrics` SSE subscriber) and collects models whose latest-turn `contextUsagePct >= 90`; when at least one matches, renders an `AlertTriangle` (lucide-react) in `var(--critical-red)` with Tailwind `animate-pulse` so the signal is visible at a glance and even when the panel is collapsed (the existing Row 3a bar also pulses red on >=90 per checkbox 4, but a collapsed panel hides it вҖ” the header icon is the always-visible analogue). **Tooltip** (`title` attr, ``Context жҺҘиҝ‘дёҠйҷҗпјҢagent еҸҜиғҪ truncate вҖ” <offending models>``) matches spec string literally and appends the offending model list so operators know which card to expand; `aria-label` carries the English translation for a11y. **NULL degradation**: `contextUsagePct === null` (Ollama without env override / OpenRouter pass-through / unknown provider / no SSE seen yet) is explicitly excluded from the trigger вҖ” "no data" MUST NOT fire the alarm, same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 / ZZ.A2 established for cache + context fields. **Placement**: inside the header `<button>` so it tracks the expand-toggle click target (clicking the icon still toggles the panel, matches operator intuition "click anywhere on the header row"); wrapped in a `<span>` carrying the `title` + `data-testid` because `title` on SVG elements has inconsistent browser tooltip behaviour. **`data-testid="context-critical-warning"`** seeded for next-checkbox-row test (ZZ.A2 checkbox 6 `иӯҰе‘Ҡ icon жўқд»¶`). `AlertTriangle` added to the lucide-react import list. `tsc --noEmit` baseline 3 pre-existing errors in `integration-settings.tsx`; post-change: still 3 вҖ” 0 new type errors. Neighbour test `test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` 2/2 co-green (no DOM shuffle in header cache selectors). Module-global audit per SOP Step 1: no new module-global state вҖ” `criticalContextModels` / `hasCriticalContext` are per-render derived values; `contextByModel` is existing component-local `useState` from checkbox 4 (per-mount React state). Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡlimit lookup fallbackгҖҒNULL model иҷ•зҗҶгҖҒUI йЎҸиүІеҚҖй–“гҖҒиӯҰе‘Ҡ icon жўқд»¶гҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 6: backend + frontend test coverage matrix for ZZ.A2 locked. **Backend (limit lookup fallback + NULL modelиҷ•зҗҶ)**: `backend/tests/test_context_limits.py` (25 cases, landed w/ ZZ.A2 checkbox 2) already covers `TestExactMatch` (4 headline models + slash-path keys) + `TestProviderDefaultFallback` (unknown-model/empty/None-model falls to provider-level `default`) + `TestNullSemantics` (`default: null` вҶ’ None, unknown provider вҶ’ None, None/empty provider вҶ’ None) + `TestProviderCaseInsensitive` (Anthropic/ANTHROPIC) + `TestOllamaEnvOverride` (6 cases: beats known, beats null, scoped to ollama, garbage/empty/zero fall-through) + `TestYamlLoaderRobustness` (missing/corrupt/null-entry YAML). `backend/tests/test_turn_metrics_sse.py` (6 cases, landed w/ ZZ.A2 checkbox 3) covers the SSE emit contract under NULL limit. Scope discipline: this row does NOT duplicate those вҖ” they exist and pass. **Frontend (UI йЎҸиүІеҚҖй–“ + иӯҰе‘Ҡ icon жўқд»¶)**: new file `test/components/token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` (8 cases in 3 describe blocks). `TokenUsageStats вҖ” context-window bar colour bands` (2): 4-band sweep `25/60/80/95 вҶ’ green/yellow/orange/red` with pulse check (only the 95% row carries `animate-pulse`; green/yellow/orange must NOT pulse else the alarm signal loses meaning) + boundary-sentinel sweep `50/75/90 вҶ’ yellow/orange/red` locking strict `<` upper-edge semantics (50 is NOT green, 75 is NOT yellow, 90 is NOT orange вҖ” the same "right edge moves up a band" reading ZZ.A1 used for cache). `TokenUsageStats вҖ” context-window NULL degradation` (2): Ollama-shape `context_limit=null, context_usage_pct=null` renders literal `"вҖ”"` in `var(--muted-foreground)` (explicitly NOT any of the four band colours вҖ” NULL must never masquerade as a real band), bar width degrades to `0%`, bar does NOT pulse, rail tooltip surfaces `"context limit unknown"` + fresh-card (no SSE seen yet) renders `"вҖ”"` with tooltip `"no turn_metrics seen yet"`. `TokenUsageStats вҖ” card-top warning icon trigger condition` (4): 89.99% + 45% sibling вҶ’ NO icon (inclusive `>=90` means 89.99 is still safe + near-miss sentinel guards a future off-by-0.01 regression) / 92% + 45% mixed вҶ’ icon fires + tooltip names only the offending model (ZZ.A2-5 contract: operator must know WHICH card to expand, tooltip must NOT cry wolf over a comfortable sibling) / NULL `pct=null` with 10M raw tokens вҶ’ NO icon (NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract: "no data" MUST NOT fire the alarm, the whole point of preserving None in the backend) / drop-back semantics: 95% вҶ’ icon fires вҶ’ next snapshot 30% вҶ’ icon clears (latest-turn snapshot semantics, not lifetime вҖ” verifies the `setContextByModel` state updates drive the derived `hasCriticalContext`). **Infra pattern**: uses `test/helpers/sse.ts::primeSSE` (same pattern as `mode-selector.test.tsx` / `integration-settings-sse.test.tsx`) to mock `@/lib/api::subscribeEvents`, push synthesised `turn_metrics` events via the listener capture, wrapped in `act(...)` so React 18 batches the state updates before assertion. Model rows are seeded via `externalUsage={rows}` + `totalTokens` ordering tricks so the DOM order matches `pctNodes[0..3]` assertions (component sorts by totalTokens DESC). **Test results**: 8/8 new tests green. Co-run with ZZ.A1 cache test (`token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx`): 10/10 passed, 0 regression. Backend neighbour sanity: `test_context_limits + test_turn_metrics_sse` 31/31 green (25 context_limits + 6 turn_metrics). **Module-global audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state introduced by the test file вҖ” `vi.mock("@/lib/api", ...)` hoists per-file, `primeSSE` captures listeners in a closure per `primeSSE(api)` call, `beforeEach` resets the mock between cases. Component-local `contextByModel` is existing per-mount React state from ZZ.A2 checkbox 4 (not module-global). **Fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in the new test file. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

#### ZZ.A3 Inter-turn gap + tool call stats (#303-3)
- [x] `TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_start` иЁҳ turn иө·й»һ timestampпјӣ`on_llm_end` иЁҳзөӮй»һпјӣе…©иҖ…е·®еҚігҖҢLLM йҒӢз®—жҷӮй–“гҖҚгҖӮе…©еҖӢ turn д№Ӣй–“зҡ„е·®жҳҜгҖҢtool еҹ·иЎҢ + event bus жҺ’зЁӢ + зӯү context gatherгҖҚжҷӮй–“вҖ”вҖ”дҪө `SharedTokenUsage.track()` зҡ„ `turn_started_at` / `turn_ended_at`гҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 1: threaded per-turn LLM boundary stamps (`turn_started_at` / `turn_ended_at`, ISO-8601 UTC microsecond precision) from `TokenTrackingCallback` through `track_tokens` вҶ’ `SharedTokenUsage.track` вҶ’ in-memory / Redis / PG row end-to-end. **Callback** (`backend/agents/llm.py`): `__init__` gains `self._start_ts_utc = ""`; `on_llm_start` sets it via `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()` alongside the existing `self._start` float (keeps back-compat for `latency_ms` computation); `on_llm_end` captures `turn_ended_at` as close to the `track_tokens()` call as possible (after `_extract_cache_tokens`, before the ZZ.A2 SSE emit) so the stored stamp reflects "LLM call completed", not "bookkeeping done". Both stamps land as kwargs on `track_tokens(..., turn_started_at=..., turn_ended_at=...)` вҖ” `self._start_ts_utc or None` ensures a callback that never saw `on_llm_start` degrades to None (partial-knowledge-caller contract) rather than fabricating `""`. **Router** (`backend/routers/system.py::track_tokens`): two new optional kwargs with `None` default; the in-memory `_token_usage[model]` fresh dict adds `turn_started_at: ""` / `turn_ended_at: ""` (fresh ZZ-era row, no turn yet); the apply-delta block OVERWRITES (not accumulates) when a kwarg is provided вҖ” last-turn-snapshot semantics, essential for the downstream gap computation (`this_turn.started - last_turn.ended`). `None` kwargs leave the stored value alone so legacy callers (rule-based fallback, test fixtures) don't clobber a previously populated stamp. Plumbs through to `_token_usage_shared.track(...)`. **SharedTokenUsage** (`backend/shared_state.py`): (1) `_fresh_token_entry` adds `turn_started_at: ""` / `turn_ended_at: ""` вҖ” fresh ZZ rows start `""` (authoritative from turn 1). (2) `_normalize_token_entry` applies the same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 established for `cache_*` fields: absent on legacy payloads вҶ’ `None` (dashboard renders "вҖ”"), present вҶ’ preserved. (3) `_apply_token_delta` gains two kwargs; overwrites iff not None вҖ” same partial-knowledge semantics as router. (4) `SharedTokenUsage.track` signature extended with both kwargs, plumbed identically through the Redis and in-memory branches. **Persistence** (`backend/db.py`): `token_usage` schema CREATE TABLE adds `turn_started_at TEXT` / `turn_ended_at TEXT` (NULLable, no DEFAULT вҖ” same discipline as ZZ.A1 cache columns); `upsert_token_usage` bumped to 13 positional placeholders with `_str_or_none` helper that coerces empty-string вҶ’ SQL NULL so pre-first-turn fresh rows persist cleanly rather than polluting the column with sentinel `""`. **Migration** (`backend/alembic/versions/0025_token_usage_turn_timestamps.py`): dialect-safe ADD COLUMN (PG `IF NOT EXISTS`, SQLite PRAGMA-probe) mirroring ZZ.A1's `0024_token_usage_cache.py` exactly; downgrade is a defensive no-op (dropping loses accumulated data + breaks hot workers). **Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state. `_token_usage` (routers/system.py) pre-existing; per-worker PG-reload-on-startup via `load_token_usage_from_db` already established (escape hatch #1 вҖ” derived from shared PG source). `SharedTokenUsage._local` is instance-scoped (not module-global). `TokenTrackingCallback._start_ts_utc` is instance-scoped per request. **Tests** (`backend/tests/test_turn_boundary_stamps.py`, 6 cases in 4 TestCase classes): `TestCallbackCapturesBothBoundaries` (2) вҖ” fresh instance starts empty-string + `on_llm_start` sets parseable tz-aware ISO-8601 UTC; end-to-end callback plumbs both stamps to track_tokens via monkeypatched `track_tokens` + stubbed `LLMResult` (Anthropic shape), asserts `end >= start` + both tz-aware. `TestSharedTokenUsageOverwriteSnapshot` (1) вҖ” two consecutive track() calls leave latest pair in the entry; canonical counters (input_tokens/request_count) still ACCUMULATE (proves split contract holds). `TestNullCompatPreZzRow` (2) вҖ” legacy payload (no turn_* keys) surfaces None through get_all() while cache_* ZZ.A1 fields stay intact; fresh ZZ row starts `""` (not None), upgrades to real ISO stamp on first stamped track(). `TestPartialKnowledgeCallerPreservesPriorStamp` (1) вҖ” stamped call followed by no-stamp call preserves prior stamps (not clobbered to None) while counters still increment. **Test results**: 6/6 new tests green. Neighbour sanity: `test_shared_state + test_token_usage_shared + test_token_budget + test_turn_metrics_sse + test_turn_boundary_stamps + test_schema + test_context_limits + test_db_token_usage` вҶ’ 121/121 green, 13 PG-only skipped (unchanged baseline). `test_llm_adapter + test_llm_errors + test_hmi_llm + test_migrator_schema_coverage` вҶ’ all green (one pre-existing unrelated bootstrap test failure confirmed via `git stash` bisect вҖ” `db_pool.get_pool called before init_pool` in `test_bootstrap_llm_provision.py`, unrelated to this change). **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in `shared_state.py` / `routers/system.py` / `agents/llm.py` / new migration / new test file. The pre-existing `db.py` hits are all other tables' `(datetime('now'))` DEFAULTs + pre-existing `_conn()` compat wrapper вҖ” none touched by this change (new `turn_*` columns are NULLable with no DEFAULT, consistent with ZZ.A1 discipline). **Scope discipline**: `TokenUsageEntry` pydantic model + `/runtime/tokens` response schema + frontend consumer NOT touched вҖ” that's ZZ.A3 checkbox 3's UI "avg gap 320ms" column; this checkbox scopes strictly to "record in SharedTokenUsage.track()" per spec wording. ZZ.A3 checkboxes 2-5 (GraphState tool stats, UI row 3 mini-columns, p95 outlier, matrix test) intentionally untouched. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] `GraphState.tool_results`пјҲ`agents/graph.py`пјүзөұиЁҲ `tool_call_count` + `tool_failure_count`пјҲ`result.error is not None`пјүпјҢжҜҸ turn зөҗжқҹжҷӮжҺЁ SSE event йҷ„её¶гҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 2: new SSE event `turn_tool_stats` wires turn-end tool-execution aggregates out of `GraphState.tool_results` to the frontend. **Schema** (`backend/sse_schemas.py::SSETurnToolStats`): 5-field payload (`agent_type` / `task_id` / `tool_call_count` / `tool_failure_count` / `failed_tools`) + bus-auto `timestamp`. Registered under `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["turn_tool_stats"]` so `/runtime/sse-schema` export advertises it for frontend codegen. **Emit helper** (`backend/events.py::emit_turn_tool_stats`): matches the existing `emit_*` family signature вҖ” explicit `broadcast_scope=None` default + `_resolve_scope("emit_turn_tool_stats", ..., "global")` legacy fallback so `OMNISIGHT_SSE_SCOPE_STRICT=1` stays green when the summarizer passes `broadcast_scope="global"` explicitly. `failed_tools` preserved in insertion order + with duplicates (a 3-retry loop on the same tool surfaces as 3 entries, matches the red "failed N" badge to actual retry attempt count, not a de-duped set). Logs at `warn` level when `tool_failure_count > 0`, `info` otherwise, so REPORTER VORTEX surfaces the failing-turn badge. **Wire-up** (`backend/agents/nodes.py::summarizer_node`): computes `tool_call_count = len(state.tool_results)` + `failed_tools = [r.tool_name for r in state.tool_results if not r.success]` at the top of the node (before the pass-through early-return), then emits. **Semantic mapping**: spec text says `result.error is not None` but the actual `ToolResult` class (`backend/agents/state.py`) models failure as `success: bool` (the tool executor sets `success=False` on any `[ERROR]` / `[BLOCKED]` / `[TIMEOUT]` prefix or raised exception) вҖ” the LangGraph shape's equivalent of `error is not None`, documented in the schema docstring + emit helper + test file so future readers understand the mapping. **Zeroed snapshot on pass-through**: the emit runs BEFORE the `if state.answer and not state.tool_results: return {}` early-return so a conversational turn after a failing tool turn still pushes `tool_call_count=0 / tool_failure_count=0` вҖ” clears any stale "failed N" badge the UI card might be carrying from a prior turn (without this, the red badge would stick across turn boundaries on the same agent card). **Turn semantics**: "turn end" = `summarizer_node` execution (last node before `END` in `backend/agents/graph.py::build_graph`), runs exactly once per `run_graph` call via the `context_gate вҶ’ summarizer вҶ’ END` edge. **Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state introduced вҖ” `emit_turn_tool_stats` is stateless, `SSETurnToolStats` is a Pydantic class at module import, `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS` is a read-only registry dict. Summarizer-side computation is per-call (derived purely from the passed-in `GraphState` instance). **Emit hardened**: wrapped in try/except with `logger.debug` on failure вҖ” an emit hiccup must never break the turn's answer synthesis. **Tests** (`backend/tests/test_turn_tool_stats_sse.py`, 5 cases): emit helper canonical payload shape / summarizer end-to-end with mixed success+failure batch (4 tools, 2 failures, in-order `failed_tools`) / zeroed snapshot on pass-through (no tools, 0/0 payload) / duplicate-preserving `failed_tools` (3Г— `run_bash` failure surfaces as 3 entries) / schema registry drift guard (`SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["turn_tool_stats"] is SSETurnToolStats` + 5 required field names present). Plus `backend/tests/test_schema.py::TestSSESchemaExport::test_all_events_have_schemas` expected-set updated to include `turn_tool_stats`. **Test results**: 5/5 new tests green. Neighbour sanity: `test_turn_tool_stats_sse + test_turn_metrics_sse + test_schema::TestSSESchemaExport + test_nodes + test_events_bus + test_verification_loop + test_permission_errors + test_platform_tags_for_rag` вҶ’ 96/96 green, 0 regression. **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches across `backend/sse_schemas.py`, `backend/events.py`, `backend/agents/nodes.py`, new test file, updated test file. **Scope discipline**: UI Row 3 mini-columns ("tools 5 / failed 1" badge, ZZ.A3 checkbox 3) + p95 outlier highlight (ZZ.A3 checkbox 4) + matrix test (ZZ.A3 checkbox 5) intentionally untouched вҖ” strict "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] UI еҚЎзүҮ Row 3 еҸіеҒҙеҠ иҝ·дҪ е…©ж¬„пјҡгҖҢavg gap 320msгҖҚ+гҖҢtools 5 / failed 1гҖҚпјҲзҙ…еӯ— badge иӢҘ failed > 0пјүгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 3: landed the Row 3 companion mini-stats block on the per-model TokenUsageStats card. **Frontend SSE plumbing** (`lib/api.ts`): new `turn_tool_stats` entry in both the `SSEEvent` discriminated-union (carries `agent_type` / `task_id` / `tool_call_count` / `tool_failure_count` / `failed_tools` / `timestamp`, mirroring `backend/sse_schemas.py::SSETurnToolStats` field-for-field, `task_id` nullable) and the `SSE_EVENT_TYPES` whitelist вҖ” without the whitelist entry the shared `EventSource` `addEventListener` loop would silently drop the frame before any component saw it (same gotcha that bit `turn_metrics` in ZZ.A2 checkbox 4). **Component** (`components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx`): `useRef` added to imports; extended the existing `useEffect` subscription (landed in ZZ.A2 checkbox 4 for `turn_metrics`) to also track per-model turn history + attribute `turn_tool_stats` to the most-recently-seen model. Two new state maps + one ref: `turnHistoryByModel: Record<modelLowercase, {ts, latency}[]>` вҖ” rolling buffer of the last `HISTORY_DEPTH=10` turns' parsed `timestamp` epoch-ms + `latency_ms`, used for avg gap; `toolStatsByModel: Record<modelLowercase, {callCount, failureCount, failedTools}>` вҖ” latest `turn_tool_stats` per model; `lastMetricsModelRef` вҖ” the model from the most-recent `turn_metrics`, used to attribute the immediately-following `turn_tool_stats` (summarizer doesn't carry a model field; since summarizer_node fires after on_llm_end in the same graph run the ordering holds вҖ” best-effort heuristic, documented in the source as "would need backend SSETurnToolStats.model to tighten up with concurrent agents"). **Avg gap formula** per ZZ.A3 checkbox 1 spec: `gap_i = (t[i].ts - t[i-1].ts) - t[i].latency_ms` вҶ’ "tool exec + event bus scheduling + context gather" time; averaged over the buffer; needs вүҘ2 turns to produce a value, else renders "вҖ”" (NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 established). Negative-gap guard (`g >= 0`) filters clock-skew / out-of-order deliveries. **Render** (after Row 3 usage bar, before Row 3a context): new right-aligned flex row `[avg gap Xms | tools N / failed M]` in `text-[9px] font-mono text-[var(--muted-foreground)]` matching the existing Row 2 micro-metadata typography. **Red badge**: the "failed M" digit is wrapped in a `<span>` that gains `text-[var(--critical-red)] font-semibold` iff `hasToolStats && failureCount > 0` вҖ” matches spec "зҙ…еӯ— badge иӢҘ failed > 0". A zero-failure turn (like the pass-through zeroed snapshot emitted by summarizer after a conversational turn) renders "failed 0" in muted-foreground, NOT red вҖ” so the badge doesn't cry wolf on green turns, and a stale red badge from a prior failing turn gets cleared on the next pass-through (the zeroed-snapshot behaviour ZZ.A3 checkbox 2 specifically designed for). **NULL degradation**: pre-ZZ cards with no SSE data yet (synthesised `configuredProviders` placeholders / fresh cards / legacy payloads) render `avg gap вҖ”` + `tools вҖ” / failed вҖ”` muted. **`data-testid` attributes** seeded for the ZZ.A3 checkbox 5 vitest (`turn-mini-stats` / `turn-avg-gap` / `turn-tool-count` / `turn-failed-count`) вҖ” tests for gap formula + failure-count red badge + p95 outlier land in the next checkbox row. **Tooltips**: `avg gap` carries "Average gap between LLM turns (tool exec + event bus + context gather)" вҖ” matches the spec's verbatim framing so ops know what the number represents. `tools / failed` carries the failed-tool-name list inline when `failureCount > 0` (e.g. "tools 5 / failed 1 вҖ” run_bash") so a hover on the card tells the operator WHICH tool retried, not just a count. When no `turn_tool_stats` has landed yet, tooltip reads "no turn_tool_stats seen for this model yet" вҖ” distinct from "data arrived but counts are zero". **Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state. `turnHistoryByModel` / `toolStatsByModel` are component-local `useState` (per-mount); `lastMetricsModelRef` is component-local `useRef` (per-mount). Existing `_sharedES` / `_sseListeners` in `lib/api.ts` are pre-existing module-globals scoped to the browser tab (not per-uvicorn-worker) вҖ” the SOP escape hatch doesn't apply (worker-fan-out is a backend concern). **`tsc --noEmit`**: baseline 3 pre-existing errors in `integration-settings.tsx` (TS2322 `GitForgeTokenTestResult` vs `TestResult` index-signature mismatch, documented in prior ZZ.A1 / ZZ.A2 checkbox notes); post-change: still 3 вҖ” 0 new type errors in `token-usage-stats.tsx` or `lib/api.ts`. **Neighbour test regression**: co-ran `test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` (2 cases) + `test/components/token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` (8 cases) = 10/10 green, 0 regression. The new Row 3-mini block sits between Row 3 (usage bar) and Row 3a (context) so existing selector paths in those tests are unaffected. **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` or `lib/api.ts`. **Scope discipline**: p95 outlier highlight (ZZ.A3 checkbox 4) + tests (ZZ.A3 checkbox 5) intentionally untouched вҖ” strict "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". No backend / prod-artefact changes вҖ” all lifting in two frontend files. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] и¶…йҒҺ p95 зҡ„ turn еҒҡ outlier highlightпјҲиҰ–иҰәжҸҗйҶ’гҖҢйҖҷ turn з•°еёёж…ўгҖҚпјүгҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 4: landed per-model "SLOW" outlier badge on the Row 3 mini-stats block in `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx`. **Signal**: `turn_metrics.latency_ms` (per-turn LLM call duration) from the rolling `turnHistoryByModel` buffer (HISTORY_DEPTH=10, landed w/ ZZ.A3 checkbox 3). Chose latency over "gap" because the spec says "йҖҷ turn з•°еёёж…ў" вҖ” the attribute-of-this-turn signal maps directly to `latency_ms` (which IS this turn's duration); gap is an inter-turn derived metric that isn't "of the turn". **p95 on prior turns only**: compute sorted-list nearest-rank p95 (`idx = min(n-1, max(0, ceil(n*0.95)-1))`) from the history EXCLUDING the latest entry; then check `latest > p95(prior)`. Including the latest in p95 is tautological вҖ” latest is always вүӨ max of the set it belongs to, so `latest > p95` would degenerate into `latest > max - epsilon` which never fires at small n. Excluding the latest makes the test read as "latest turn exceeds the 95th percentile of the preceding turns" вҖ” a clean outlier definition. **Minimum samples**: `MIN_PRIOR=4` вҶ’ need 5 total turns before the badge can light up. With HISTORY_DEPTH=10 the effective p95 with 4-9 prior samples approximates `max(prior)` (ceil(4*0.95)-1=3 last-of-4 / ceil(9*0.95)-1=8 last-of-9), which at this buffer depth is the right "strictly faster than any recent turn" signal. Document in a source comment so a future reader switching to a larger buffer understands the interaction. **Positive-latency filter**: `filter(l => Number.isFinite(l) && l > 0)` defends against degenerate samples where the backend emitted `latency_ms=0` (callback timing race / end-before-start on a stub вҖ” shouldn't happen in prod but seen in fixtures), so zero-latency samples don't drag the p95 down and cry wolf. **Visual**: inline `<span>` prepended before the "avg gap" text when outlier вҖ” carries an `AlertTriangle` (size=10, already imported) + literal text "SLOW" in `text-[var(--hardware-orange)] font-semibold animate-pulse`. Intentionally NOT red вҖ” context-critical warning is already `critical-red` (ZZ.A2 checkbox 5) and uses `AlertTriangle`; differentiating colour keeps the two alarms visually distinct ("context near cap" vs "this turn is slow"). **Tooltip**: `йҖҷ turn з•°еёёж…ў вҖ” latency Xms и¶…йҒҺ p95 (Yms) of жңҖиҝ‘ N turns` in the same Mandarin-first framing as the checkbox spec text, so operators see the exact numbers driving the alarm without needing to expand the card. `aria-label` carries the English translation for a11y. **Rendered iff `isOutlier`**: when latest is not an outlier, the badge simply doesn't render вҖ” no visual slot kept (keeps Row 3 mini-stats compact on happy-path turns). **`data-testid="turn-p95-outlier"`** seeded for the next-checkbox-row vitest (ZZ.A3 checkbox 5 p95 outlier еҲӨж–·). **Module-global audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state вҖ” `p95Latency` / `isOutlier` / `priorLatencies` / `latestLatency` are per-render derived values inside the existing Row-3-mini IIFE; `turnHistoryByModel` is pre-existing component-local `useState` from ZZ.A3 checkbox 3 (per-mount React state). **`tsc --noEmit`**: baseline 3 pre-existing `integration-settings.tsx` errors (TS2322 `GitForgeTokenTestResult` vs `TestResult` index-signature mismatch, documented in prior checkbox notes); post-change: still 3 вҖ” 0 new type errors. **Neighbour test regression**: `test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` (2) + `test/components/token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` (8) = 10/10 green, 0 regression. The new outlier `<span>` is conditionally rendered and prepended within the existing `turn-mini-stats` container so the existing `turn-avg-gap` / `turn-tool-count` / `turn-failed-count` selectors in any future test remain stable. **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in `token-usage-stats.tsx`. **Scope discipline**: the matrix test (ZZ.A3 checkbox 5: gap иЁҲз®—жӯЈзўәжҖ§ / tool failure count / p95 outlier еҲӨж–·) intentionally untouched вҖ” strict "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". No backend changes, no SSE wiring changes вҖ” all lifting in the single IIFE inside `token-usage-stats.tsx`. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] жё¬и©Ұпјҡgap иЁҲз®—жӯЈзўәжҖ§пјҲеҗ« timezone дёҖиҮҙпјүгҖҒtool failure countгҖҒp95 outlier еҲӨж–·гҖӮ <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 5: landed the matrix regression guard locking the three ZZ.A3 UI contracts the earlier checkboxes landed but never tested. New file `test/components/token-usage-stats-turn-stats.test.tsx` (10 cases in 3 describe blocks). **Block 1 вҖ” inter-turn gap formula** (3 cases): `avg gap = mean((delta_ts - latency))` rounded across multiple turns вҖ” emits three `turn_metrics` at +0 / +1s (lat=200) / +2.5s (lat=300) вҶ’ gaps 800 / 1200 вҶ’ avg "1000ms" verifying `Math.round((800 + 1200) / 2)`; **timezone consistency** вҖ” same three absolute moments serialised in Taipei `+08:00` (20:00:00 / 20:00:01 / 20:00:02.500) must yield the identical "1000ms" because `Date.parse()` normalises any offset to epoch ms (if the component computed in local-time-aware Date objects the +08:00 gaps would balloon into 28.8-million-ms); **<2 turns** вҖ” single turn has no consecutive pair вҶ’ renders "avg gap вҖ”". **Block 2 вҖ” tool failure count red badge** (3 cases): `failureCount > 0` renders the failed digit in `text-[var(--critical-red)] font-semibold`, tooltip contains the failing tool name (`run_bash`) so operators see WHICH tool retried without expanding the card; **zeroed-snapshot clear** вҖ” first turn with 2 failures gets red, next turn's pass-through zeroed snapshot (`tool_call_count=0, tool_failure_count=0`) clears the red вҶ’ locks the ZZ.A3 checkbox 2 "emit before early-return" invariant on the frontend side (without this, stale red would stick across turn boundaries); **NULL degradation** вҖ” no `turn_tool_stats` seen yet renders `tools вҖ” / failed вҖ”` in muted with tooltip `"no turn_tool_stats seen for this model yet"`, and crucially the em-dash failed cell does NOT carry the red class (else "no data" would masquerade as a critical failing turn вҖ” the same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 / ZZ.A2 established elsewhere on this card). **Block 3 вҖ” p95 outlier SLOW badge** (4 cases): **fires** вҖ” 4 priors [100,200,300,400] + latest 1000 (p95=400 via nearest-rank `ceil(4 * 0.95) - 1 = 3`), tooltip contains `1000ms` + `(400ms)`, badge uses `text-[var(--hardware-orange)]` + `animate-pulse` (NOT critical-red вҖ” reserved for ZZ.A2 context-critical); **does-not-fire under threshold** вҖ” same priors + latest 350 вҶ’ 350 > 400 false вҶ’ no badge (locks strict `>` boundary вҖ” flipping to `>=` would silently light up equal-to-p95 latencies); **<5 turns** вҖ” 4 total turns (3 priors + 1 latest) вҶ’ `priorLatencies.length = 3 < MIN_PRIOR=4` вҶ’ no badge even on a clearly-outlying 10_000ms latest (documents "don't cry wolf on a cold card"); **filter-then-count ordering** вҖ” 5 total turns but the first prior is a degenerate 0ms sample вҶ’ after filter `l > 0` we have 3 real priors (< MIN_PRIOR) вҶ’ badge refuses to fire (locks filter-before-count invariant; flipping to count-before-filter would silently re-enable the regression the positive-latency filter was added to prevent). **Test infrastructure**: uses `test/helpers/sse.ts::primeSSE` (same pattern as ZZ.A2 `token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` and mode-selector tests) to mock `@/lib/api::subscribeEvents`, push synthesised `turn_metrics` + `turn_tool_stats` events via the captured listener, wrapped in `act(...)` so React 18 batches the `setTurnHistoryByModel` / `setToolStatsByModel` / `setContextByModel` updates before assertion. `turn_metrics` emitted BEFORE `turn_tool_stats` in every tool-stats test so `lastMetricsModelRef.current` is set вҖ” otherwise summarizer-attributed events never reach the card (documented in the component as the best-effort-attribution heuristic). **Test results**: 10/10 new tests green. Co-run with ZZ.A1 `token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` (2 cases) + ZZ.A2 `token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` (8 cases): 20/20 green, 0 regression. **Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state introduced вҖ” `vi.mock("@/lib/api", ...)` hoists per-file, `primeSSE` captures listeners in a per-call closure, `beforeEach` resets the mock between cases. Component-local `turnHistoryByModel` / `toolStatsByModel` are existing per-mount React state from ZZ.A3 checkboxes 3 / 4 (not module-global). **Fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`): 0 matches in the new test file. **Scope discipline**: test-only row вҖ” no prod code touched. Backend scope-5 coverage (gap иЁҲз®— / tool failure / p95) is lifted in the earlier backend test files (`test_turn_boundary_stamps.py` Г— 6 cases / `test_turn_tool_stats_sse.py` Г— 5 cases / `test_turn_metrics_sse.py` Г— 6 cases вҖ” already green from the prior 4 checkboxes); the frontend matrix guard is what this row uniquely adds. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->

`to_char(now(), ...)` зҡ„зөҗжһң)гҖӮйҒёе®ғгҖҒдёҚйҒё `token_usage`гҖҒдёҚйҒё ZZ `_hourly_ledger_shared` (еғ… costгҖҒз„Ў tokensгҖҒиЁҳжҶ¶й«”йқһжҢҒд№…)гҖӮ**Backend** вҖ” (a) `backend/models.py` ж–° `TokenBurnRatePoint` (`timestamp` / `tokens_per_hour: int` / `cost_per_hour: float`) + `TokenBurnRateResponse` (`window` / `bucket_seconds=60` / `points`) Pydantic modelsгҖӮ (b) `backend/routers/system.py::system` ж–° `@router.get("/tokens/burn-rate", response_model=TokenBurnRateResponse)` endpoint жҺҘ `window: str = "1h"` query param (зҷҪеҗҚе–® `15m=900` / `1h=3600` / `24h=86400`)пјӣwhitelist miss вҶ’ `HTTPException(400)` и©іеҲ—еҗҲжі•еҖјдҫӣ client self-correctгҖӮauth иө° router-level `Depends(_auth.current_user)` з№јжүҝ `/runtime/*` baseline (иҲҮ `GET /tokens` / `GET /turns` еҗҢзҙҡ)гҖӮ (c) SQL жҺЎ CTE: е…§еұӨ `SELECT date_trunc('minute', to_timestamp(created_at, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')) AS bucket_ts, COALESCE((data_json::jsonb->>'tokens_used')::bigint, 0) AS tokens, COALESCE((data_json::jsonb->>'cost_usd')::numeric, 0) AS cost FROM event_log WHERE event_type='turn.complete' AND to_timestamp(...) >= NOW() - make_interval(secs => $1) AND tenant_where_pg`пјӣеӨ–еұӨ `SUM(tokens)` / `SUM(cost)` `GROUP BY bucket_ts ORDER BY bucket_ts ASC`пјӣжҜҸ bucket tokens *60 = tokens_per_hour (60s bucket width жҠ•еҪұ 1 е°ҸжҷӮ)пјӣcost еҗҢзҗҶгҖӮ`tenant_where_pg` иө°ж—ўжңү contextvar gatesгҖҒplaceholders $1 еӣәе®ҡзӮә window_seconds еҶҚ append tenant вҶ’ index stableгҖӮ (d) `to_timestamp(text, fmt)` еӣһ `timestamptz`гҖҒ`NOW()` еӣһ жё¬и©ҰйҺ–йҖҷжўқиЎҢзӮәгҖӮNaN/negative/zero/maxTokensвүӨ0 еӣӣеҖӢ edge case е…Ёеӣһ 0 (йҳІ grid жёІжҹ“жҷӮ crash)гҖӮ (c) **Day axis synthesis**пјҡbackend иө° sparse payload (checkbox 1 иЁӯиЁҲ)пјҢfrontend `buildDayAxis(window, anchor=new Date())` еҫһгҖҢжң¬ең°д»ҠеӨ©гҖҚеҖ’жҺЁ 7/30 еӨ©еЎ«ж»ҝ y-axis вҖ” з©әй–’ж—Ҙд»Қз•«иЎҢгҖҒзјә bucket зҡ„ cell иө° empty styling (muted `var(--secondary)` иғҢжҷҜ) - **Day 1-1.5**пјҡWave AпјҲcache / context window / gap / tool statsпјүвҖ” з•¶еӨ©е°ұзңӢеҲ° 80% зҡ„ UX ж”№е–„

Wave A еҸҜиҲҮ Priority ZпјҲProvider Observabilityпјү**еҗҢдәә sprint и·‘**вҖ”вҖ”е…©иҖ…йғҪеӢ• `TokenTrackingCallback` е’Ң `<TokenUsageStats>`пјҢдёҖж¬ЎйҮҚж–° deploy е°ұиҰҶи“Ӣе…©зө„ж”№еӢ•гҖӮ

**е’Ңе…¶д»– Priority зҡ„й—ңдҝӮ**пјҡ
- **Z**пјҲLLM Provider Observabilityпјүпјҡе§ҠеҰ№вҖ”вҖ”Z зңӢеӨ–йғЁ balanceпјҢZZ зңӢе…§йғЁ burnгҖӮе…ұз”Ё `TokenTrackingCallback` ж”№еӢ•дҪҚзҪ®гҖӮ
- **Y / I**пјҲtenant / projectпјүпјҡZZ.C2 heatmap еңЁ Y4 project еұӨиҗҪең°еҫҢеҸҜж“ҙејөжҲҗ per-project heatmap еҲҮжҸӣпјӣзҸҫеңЁе…ҲеҒҡ globalгҖӮ
- **Q**пјҲmulti-deviceпјүпјҡZZ.B2 иҮӘеӢ• session title SSE event иҰҒйҒөе®Ҳ Q.4 зҡ„ scope=user иҰҸеүҮпјҲжүҖжңү user зҡ„иЈқзҪ®еҗҢжӯҘзңӢеҲ°жЁҷйЎҢпјүгҖӮ
- **V**пјҲVisual Design WorkspaceпјүпјҡZZ зҡ„ per-turn timeline жңӘдҫҶеҸҜж“ҙжҲҗгҖҢзңӢйҖҷеҖӢ UI design iteration и·‘дәҶе“Әе№ҫ turnгҖҚзҡ„е·ҘдҪңеҚҖеӯҗйқўжқҝгҖӮ

**Non-goals**пјҲеүҚйқўе·ІеҲ—пјҢйҮҚз”іпјүпјҡ
- вқҢ Transparent HTTP proxyпјҲccxray жһ¶ж§ӢпјҢOmniSight дёҚйңҖиҰҒпјү
- вқҢ Plan detectionпјҲжІ’жңү Claude Code иЁӮй–ұжҰӮеҝөпјү
- вқҢ Desktop notif / й ҒзұӨ pulseпјҲUX иЈқйЈҫпјү
- вқҢ йҚөзӣӨ cheatsheetпјҲйҖҡз”ЁеҘҪй»һеӯҗдҪҶйқһ ZZ зҜ„еңҚпјү
- вқҢ ROI calculatorпјҲдё»и§Җ metricпјҢзӯү T billing зі»еҲ—жҸҗдҫӣжӣҙеҸҜйқ зҡ„ value metric еҶҚеҒҡпјү

## рҹ…Ё Priority Y вҖ” Tenant Ops & Multi-Project HierarchyпјҲеӨҡз”ЁжҲ¶ / еӨҡе°ҲжЎҲйҒӢзҮҹйқўжқҝпјү

> иғҢжҷҜпјҲ2026-04-22 operator auditпјүпјҡPriority I е·ІжҠҠ tenant_id жү“йҖІ users / workflow_runs / audit / artifacts / RLS / SSE / sandbox / frontend / rate-limit / multi-worker stateпјҢ**зҙ” Schema + Runtime еұӨ**жҳҜйҖҡзҡ„гҖӮдҪҶ**з®ЎзҗҶйқўжқҝ / API / жҘӯеӢҷжЁЎеһӢ**зјәеҸЈйӮ„еҫҲеӨ§вҖ”вҖ”operator жғіж–°еўһдёҖеҖӢ tenant зӣ®еүҚе”ҜдёҖж–№жі•жҳҜзӣҙжҺҘ `INSERT INTO tenants`пјҢжІ’жңү UIгҖҒжІ’жңү REST endpointгҖҒжІ’жңү invite flowгҖҒжІ’жңү RBACпјӣ`users` иЎЁжҳҜ 1:1 mapping еҲ° tenantпјҢдёҖдәәи·Ёз§ҹжҲ¶жғ…еўғпјҲеӨ–еҢ…йЎ§е•Ҹ / MSP / и·Ёе…¬еҸёеҚ”дҪңпјүе®Ңе…ЁжІ’ж”ҜжҸҙпјӣе°ҲжЎҲйҖҷеұӨж №жң¬дёҚеӯҳеңЁвҖ”вҖ”жүҖжңү workload йғҪзӣҙжҺҘжҺӣ tenantпјҢжІ’иҫҰжі•еҒҡгҖҢеҗҢдёҖ tenant дёӢеӨҡз”ўе“Ғз·ҡ / еӨҡе°ҲжЎҲ / еӨҡеҲҶж”ҜгҖҚзҡ„иіҮжәҗеҲҶйӣўиҲҮиЁҲиІ»жӢҶеҲҶгҖӮеҸҰеӨ– `backend/workspace.py` зҡ„ `.agent_workspaces/{agent_id}/` жҳҜжүҒе№іи·Ҝеҫ‘пјҢжІ’ tenant еҲҶзө„гҖҒжІ’ project еҲҶзө„гҖҒжІ’иҮӘеӢ• GCгҖҒжІ’ disk quotaвҖ”вҖ”1 дәәжё¬и©Ұ OK дҪҶ 10 еҖӢз”ўе“Ғз·ҡ Г— 20 е°ҲжЎҲ Г— еӨҡ user зҡ„ scale жңғзҲӣгҖӮ
>
> **ж ёеҝғиЁӯиЁҲжҠүж“Ү**пјҡжүҝиӘҚ `user вҶ” tenant` жҳҜеӨҡе°ҚеӨҡпјҲmembership жЁЎеһӢпјүпјҢжүҝиӘҚ `tenant вҶ’ product_line вҶ’ project вҶ’ branch` еӣӣеұӨйҡҺеұӨпјҢжүҝиӘҚ workspace иҰҒзҙҚе…Ҙ `tenant_quota` й җз®—гҖӮйҖҷиЈЎжҠҠ I зі»еҲ—зҡ„гҖҢж©ҹжҲҝзҙҡгҖҚеӨҡз§ҹжҲ¶йҡ”йӣўеҚҮзҙҡжҲҗгҖҢйҒӢзҮҹзҙҡгҖҚеӨҡз§ҹжҲ¶е№іеҸ°гҖӮ
>
> **зӣёдҫқ**пјҡI1-I10 е…ЁйғЁ вң… doneгҖҒG4 Postgres вң… doneгҖҒK MFA/session вң… doneгҖҒS0 shared foundation вң… doneгҖҒH4a sandbox DRF вң… doneгҖӮз„ЎзЎ¬йҳ»еЎһвҖ”вҖ”еҸӘиҰҒжұәе®ҡй–Ӣе·Ҙе°ұиғҪеҒҡгҖӮ
>
> **й—ңйҚөдёҚи®ҠйҮҸ**пјҡ
> - *з§ҹжҲ¶йҳІзҒ«зүҶ*пјҡtenant A зҡ„д»»дҪ• user / project / artifact / workspace / audit-chain / event / secret йғҪеҝ…й ҲиӯүжҳҺ tenant B жӢҝдёҚеҲ°пјҲз№јжүҝиҮӘ I зҡ„зҙ…з·ҡпјҢY дёҚиғҪејұеҢ–пјүгҖӮ
> - *жңҖе°Ҹй©ҡиЁқеҺҹеүҮ*пјҡж—ўжңү `t-default` tenant дёӢзҡ„жүҖжңүиіҮж–ҷдёҚеҸҜеӣ зӮә Y зҡ„жһ¶ж§Ӣи®ҠжӣҙиҖҢйҒәеӨұжҲ–йҮҚж–°и·Ҝз”ұвҖ”вҖ”migration еҝ…й ҲжҳҜеҠ жҖ§зҡ„пјҢshard жҲҗеӨҡ tenant жҳҜ operator дё»еӢ•еӢ•дҪңгҖӮ
> - *иЁҲиІ»еҸҜиҝҪжәҜ*пјҡжҜҸеҖӢ project / user зҡ„иіҮжәҗж¶ҲиҖ—пјҲdisk / LLM tokens / sandbox / bandwidthпјүйғҪиҰҒиғҪеңЁ T зі»еҲ— billing иЁҲйҮҸжҷӮзІҫзўәжӯёеӣ пјҢдёҚиғҪеҮәзҸҫгҖҢйҖҷзӯҶз”ЁйҮҸжҲ‘з®—дёҚжё…жҘҡжҳҜе“ӘеҖӢ projectгҖҚгҖӮ
> - *и§’иүІж¬ҠйҷҗеҲҶйӣў*пјҡsuper-adminпјҲе№іеҸ°ж–№пјү/ tenant-owner / tenant-admin / project-owner / project-member / project-viewer е…ӯеұӨпјҢдёҚжҳҜ role flag иҖҢжҳҜ membership Г— resource зҡ„ RBAC зҹ©йҷЈгҖӮ

### Y0. Multi-user Г— Multi-project жғ…еўғзӣӨй»һ + жһ¶ж§Ӣж–Үд»¶ (#276)

з¬¬дёҖеҖӢз ҙеЈһ S-1 / S-2 гҖҢper-tenant audit chain е®Ңе…Ёйҡ”йӣўгҖҚinvariant зҡ„жғ…еўғпјӣеҰӮжһңеҸӘеҜ« host йҸҲгҖҒguest tenant з«Ҝзҡ„ owner е°ұз„Ўжі•еҜ©иЁҲиҮӘе·ұ user зҡ„е°ҚеӨ–иЎҢзӮәпјҲеҗҲиҰҸ + transaction еҜ«е…ҘгҖҚжҳҜй…ҚеҘ—дҝқиӯ·пјҲйҳІжӯўд»»дёҖйҸҲиў«з”ЁдҪңеҸҰдёҖйҸҲзҡ„зӘәжҺўеҫҢй–ҖпјүгҖӮ(3) зӮәдҪ• guest role ceiling иЁӯеңЁ commenter иҖҢйқһ contributorпјҡи·Ё tenant role ceiling жҳҜе®үе…ЁйӮҠз•ҢгҖҒдёҚжҳҜ UX еҰҘеҚ”пјӣcontributor иғҪ trigger workflow_runпјҲзҮ’ host LLM quotaпјү + ж”№ SOPпјҲеҪұйҹҝ host е…§йғЁе…¶д»– userпјүвҖ” е…©еҖӢйғҪдёҚи©Із”ұи·Ё tenant еёіиҷҹжҺ§еҲ¶пјӣжғізңҹжӯЈ contribute е°ұиө° S-2 invite жөҒйӮҖе°Қж–№зӮә host tenant memberгҖҒдёҚи©І abuse share и·Ҝеҫ‘еҒҡ capability еҚҮзҙҡгҖӮ(4) зӮәдҪ• share еҝ…й Ҳйӣҷз°Ҫпјҡproject owner зҹҘйҒ“е…§е®№и©ІдёҚи©ІзөҰеӨ–йғЁзңӢпјҲжҘӯеӢҷеҲӨж–·пјү+ tenant owner зҹҘйҒ“е°Қж–№ tenant жҳҜеҗҰеҸҜдҝЎпјҲеҗҲиҰҸ / жі•еӢҷеҲӨж–·пјүпјӣе–®ж–№йғҪдёҚеӨ  вҖ” security incident е ҙжҷҜдёӢе–®ж–№жҺҲж¬ҠиғҪеңЁеҲҶйҗҳзҙҡйҖ жҲҗ leakгҖҒйӣҷз°Ҫеј·еҲ¶иҮіе°‘ 2 дәәеҚ”дҪң + audit з•ҷ 2 еҖӢ actor з—•и·ЎгҖӮ(5) зӮәдҪ• expires_at еј·еҲ¶ 1 е№ҙ hard capпјҡж°ёд№… share жҳҜеҗҲиҰҸеӨ§еҝҢпјҲйӣўиҒ·е“Ўе·ҘгҖҒйҖҖдј‘еҗҲдҪңеӨҘдјҙдёҖзӣҙдҝқжңү accessпјүпјӣ365d жҳҜгҖҢеҗҲзҗҶй•·жңҹеҗҲдҪңй—ңдҝӮгҖҚиҲҮгҖҢжі•иҰҸдёҖиҲ¬иҰҒжұӮе№ҙеәҰеҜ©жҹҘгҖҚзҡ„жҠҳиЎ·гҖҒи¶…йҒҺеҝ…й ҲзәҢжңҹйҮҚж–°йӣҷз°ҪгҖӮ(6) зӮәдҪ•гҖҢcaller paysгҖҚе°Қи·Ё tenant LLM иЁҲиІ»пјҡдҝқиӯ· host дёҚиў«жғЎж„Ҹ иҖҢйқһе№іиЎҢиЎЁгҖҒis_system partial unique дҝқиӯүжҜҸ tenant жҒ°дёҖеҖӢ pl-defaultгҖҒprojects е…©йҡҺж®ө NOT NULLгҖҒtenant_secrets еҠ ж¬„дҪҚз№јжүҝ S-1.3 RBACгҖҒaudit_log partial index еҠ йҖҹ line-scoped first-class иЁҲиІ»ж¬„дҪҚдёҚжҳҜ metadata.tagsгҖҚгҖҢlifecycle stage жҳҜеј·еһӢеҲҘ enum дёҚжҳҜ status stringгҖҚгҖҢдёү project е…ұз”Ё Doorbell SOP/skill_pack жҳҜ invariant дёҚжҳҜ default вҖ” Quinn дёҚиғҪеңЁ V1 е…§ fork SOP з§Ғж”№гҖҚпјү/ S-5.2 дёүеұӨ LLM й җз®—йҡҺеұӨеҒҪзўјпјҲеңЁ S-4.2 йӣҷеұӨд№ӢдёҠеҠ  project cap з¬¬дёүеұӨгҖҒAcme Doorbell 35M жӢҶ V1 20M+V2 10M+V3 5M=35MгҖҒ`check_llm_budget` дёүеұӨеҒҪзўј + `atomic_decrement_n` PG SELECT FOR UPDATE йҺ– N row дёҖж¬Ўпјӣ7 жўқиЁӯиЁҲж–·иЁҖпјҡОЈ(project cap) вүӨ line budget жҳҜ backend invariantгҖҒproject и¶…з”Ёе„Әе…Ҳ throttle и©І cap й җиЁӯеҖјпјү/ S-5.3 customer attribution жЁЎеһӢпјҲcustomer_accounts иЎЁ 10 ж¬„ per-tenant е…§йғЁиҰ–и§’гҖҒprojects.customer_account_id nullable + is_internal not null + CHECK дә’ж–Ҙ вҖ” еј·иҝ«жҜҸ project йЎҜејҸе®Је‘Ҡе°ҚеӨ–иЁҲиІ» vs е…§йғЁиҮӘзҮ’пјӣ6 жўқиЁӯиЁҲж–·иЁҖпјҡcustomer_accounts жҳҜ acme е…§йғЁ view дёҚжҳҜе№іеҸ° entityгҖҒдә’ж–Ҙ CHECK йҳІ silent bugгҖҒcustomer и®Ҡжӣҙиө° admin йӣҷз°Ҫ step-up MFAгҖҒcustomer churn дёҚзҙҡиҒҜ archive project gracefulгҖҒcustomer-level йӣҷйҸҲ audit иҲҮ S-3 и·Ё tenant йӣҷйҸҲиЁӯиЁҲеҗҢжЁЎеһӢеҶҚдёӢдёҖеұӨгҖҒcustomer и·Ё line project еҲ—иЎЁ first-class viewпјү/ S-5.4 SOP/skill_pack дёүеұӨз№јжүҝи§ЈжһҗпјҲеңЁ S-4.5 е…©еұӨд№ӢдёҠеҠ  project еұӨгҖҒspecific-first и§Јжһҗж–№еҗ‘пјӣinheritance modeпјҲsop_overrides и–„иЎЁгҖҒparent_sop_id + parameters jsonb specific-firstгҖҒinheritance й җиЁӯ clone йЎҜејҸгҖҒskill_pack и·Ё project caller paysгҖҒcross-project clone еј·еҲ¶ж–·д»ЈгҖҒtenant-wide SOP ж”№еӢ•йңҖ adminпјү/ S-5.5 lifecycle зӢҖж…Ӣж©ҹпјҲеҡҙж јзҷҪеҗҚе–® 6 stageпјҡrnd / poc / graduated / production / rejected / archivedпјӣзӢҖж…ӢиҪүз§»ең– + йҖІе…Ҙ / йҖҖеҮәжўқд»¶ + й җиЁӯ LLM cap иЎЁпјӣ7 жўқиЁӯиЁҲж–·иЁҖпјҡеҡҙж јзҷҪеҗҚе–®дёҚе…ҒиЁұи·ійҡҺгҖҒrndвҶ’poc еҝ…з¶Ғ customer_account_id + admin з°ҪгҖҒgraduated еј·еҲ¶дёӯд»ӢзӢҖж…Ӣ 30d и§ҖеҜҹжңҹгҖҒarchived 90d е…§еҸҜйҖҶ un-archiveгҖҒlifecycle и®Ҡжӣҙеҝ…йӣҷйҸҲ auditгҖҒrejected иҲҮ archived йЎҜејҸеҲҶе…© enum еҚҖеҲҶеӨұж•— vs жӯЈеёёйҖҖе ҙгҖҒзӢҖж…ӢиҪүз§»еј•зҷј cap иҮӘеӢ•иӘҝж•ҙ + SSE йҖҡзҹҘпјү/ S-5.6 schema еўһйҮҸпјҲеңЁ S-1.6 + S-2.6 + S-3.5 + S-4.6 ж—ўжңү 11 иЎЁ 13 ж¬„еҹәзӨҺдёҠеҠ  `customer_accounts` 10 ж¬„ + `project_lifecycle_history` 8 ж¬„ + `sop_overrides` 5 ж¬„ + `projects.customer_account_id` + `projects.is_internal` + иө° admin override зү№дҫӢеҚҮйҡҺ + еҠ еҖҚ auditгҖҒ3 project з·ҠйӮҠз•Ң line budgetгҖҒgraduated 30d иҮӘеӢ•з®—и§ҖеҜҹзӘ—гҖҒrename slug 308 redirect 30dпјү/ S-5.8 11 жўқйӮҠз•Ң / йҖҖеҢ–жғ…еўғпјҲowner ж”№ vs еҗ„иҮӘ fork з•ҷ S-6гҖҒproductionвҶ’archived cap з«ӢеҲ» 0 vs 30d з·©иЎқгҖҒskill_pack и·Ё project caller pays зҙ°зІ’еәҰгҖҒcustomer churn 90d иҮӘеӢ• archive vs еј·иҝ«дәәйЎһжұәзӯ–пјүжЁҷиЁҳзөҰеҫҢзәҢеӢҫйҒё / S-5.10 17 иЎҢж—ўжңүеҜҰдҪңе°Қз…§иЎЁпјҲзӣӨй»һ backend/alembic/versions/0006_project_runs.py:21-27 ж—ўжңү project_runs иЎЁеҸӘжҠҠ project_id з•¶ string label
