### L6. Step 5 вҖ” Smoke Test + е®ҢжҲҗ
- [x] и·‘ `scripts/prod_smoke_test.py` еӯҗйӣҶпјҲйҒё compile-flash host_native DAGпјҢ~60sпјү *(done: `--subset dag1` CLI flag on the smoke script + `POST /api/v1/bootstrap/smoke-subset` endpoint runs DAG_1 in-process, verifies audit hash chain, and flips `smoke_passed` + records `STEP_SMOKE` on green; wizard Step 5 UI wired via `SmokeSubsetStep`)*
- [x] йЎҜзӨә audit_log hash chain й©—иӯүзөҗжһңгҖҒе…©еҖӢ DAG зҡ„ run summary *(done: wizard now POSTs `subset=both` so `/bootstrap/smoke-subset` runs DAG_1 + DAG_2 and returns per-DAG `SmokeRunSummary`s; Step 5 pane renders each DAG as its own pass/fail card (label, run_id, plan_id, plan_status, target, t3 runner, task count, validation errors) and a dedicated audit-chain panel shows PASS/FAIL + tenant_count + detail + first_bad_id + bad_tenants)*
- [x] е…Ёз¶  вҶ’ `POST /api/v1/bootstrap/finalize` вҶ’ еҜ« `bootstrap_finalized=true` вҶ’ е°Һеҗ‘ dashboard *(done: backend `POST /bootstrap/finalize` already writes `bootstrap_finalized=true` in the marker + records `STEP_FINALIZED` via `mark_bootstrap_finalized`; wizard now exposes an inline "Finalize & go to dashboard" CTA inside the Step 5 Smoke pane (`bootstrap-smoke-finalize-button`) that is enabled only when `status.all_green` and `missing_steps` is empty вҖ” click posts `/bootstrap/finalize`, waits for `reloadStatus`, then `router.replace("/")`; Step 6 "Finalize" pane still carries the canonical button for operators who auto-advance past Smoke; two new vitest cases cover the inline-CTA green-path redirect and the disabled/missing-steps red-path)*
- [x] еӨұж•— вҶ’ йЎҜзӨәйҢҜиӘӨ + е…ҒиЁұеӣһеҲ°еүҚйқў step дҝ®жӯЈ *(done: `SmokeSubsetStep` now derives `hasFailure = !smokeGreen && (error !== null || (result !== null && !passed))` and вҖ” when the wizard parent supplies the new `onJumpToStep(id)` callback вҖ” renders a `bootstrap-smoke-jump-back` panel listing all four preceding gates as quick-jump buttons (`bootstrap-smoke-jump-back-{admin_password,llm_provider,cf_tunnel,services_ready}`); a `_diagnoseSmokeFailure(result, error)` heuristic picks the most likely culprit (audit-chain break вҶ’ admin_password; validation_errors mentioning llm/provider вҶ’ llm_provider; tunnel/cloudflare вҶ’ cf_tunnel; platform/compile/target/runner/ready or any network error вҶ’ services_ready) and tags the chosen button with `data-culprit="true"` plus a "likely" pill; the parent wires the callback to `setUserPinned(true) + setActiveId(id)` so a single click pins the wizard to the chosen prior gate (existing `goPrev/Next` chevrons still work for sequential navigation). Three new vitest cases cover (a) the audit-chain culprit path with click-through to a non-culprit step, (b) the network-error path with services_ready as the inferred culprit, and (c) the green-path that suppresses the panel entirely)*
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

### L7. йғЁзҪІжЁЎејҸеҒөжё¬ + docker-compose и·Ҝеҫ‘
- [x] `detect_deploy_mode()`пјҡеҒөжё¬жҳҜеҗҰеңЁ docker е…§ / жҳҜеҗҰжңү systemd / жҳҜеҗҰжңү docker socket *(done: new `backend/deploy_mode.py` exposes `detect_deploy_mode() -> DeployModeDetection` with per-probe signals вҖ” `_is_in_docker()` checks `/.dockerenv` + `/proc/1/cgroup` for docker/containerd/kubepods tokens, `_has_systemd()` checks `/run/systemd/system` + systemctl on PATH, `_has_docker_socket()` checks `/var/run/docker.sock` is a real unix socket. Decision table: env `OMNISIGHT_DEPLOY_MODE` wins вҶ’ in-docker+socket вҶ’ docker-compose (compose-in-docker) вҶ’ in-docker w/o socket вҶ’ dev (no-op) вҶ’ systemd вҶ’ docker-compose (socket or binary) вҶ’ dev fallback. Returns dataclass carrying `mode`, `in_docker`, `has_systemd`, `has_docker_socket`, `has_docker_binary`, `has_systemctl_binary`, `override_source`, `reason`, and per-probe `signals` dict for audit/UI. `backend/routers/bootstrap.py::_detect_deploy_mode()` now delegates to this so Step 4 launcher shares the richer probe without breaking callers. 22 new tests in `test_deploy_mode.py` cover every probe + decision row incl. regression guard that systemd wins over docker when both present.)*
- [x] дҫқжЁЎејҸжҸҗдҫӣдёҚеҗҢ start-services жҢҮд»Өпјҡ *(done: `backend/routers/bootstrap.py::_start_command()` now branches on the L7 detection: `systemd` вҶ’ `sudo -n systemctl start omnisight-backend.service omnisight-frontend.service` (the `-n` keeps sudo non-interactive so a missing K1 sudoers rule fails fast instead of blocking on a TTY prompt the wizard has no console for); `docker-compose` вҶ’ `docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d` unchanged (compose_file body field still overrides); `dev` вҶ’ `[]` no-op so the endpoint short-circuits with `status="already_running"`. Added module-level `SUDOERS_LINE` + `generate_sudoers_snippet()` helper that renders the scoped K1 grant `omnisight ALL=(root) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl start omnisight-backend.service, /usr/bin/systemctl start omnisight-frontend.service` вҖ” mirrors the `omnisight-cloudflared` sudoers shape operators already ship so ops learn one convention. Scope is start-only (K1 least-privilege): the wizard never stops or restarts services, and the generator proves it by omitting stop/restart grants. 6 new tests in `test_bootstrap_start_services.py`: `_start_command` argv shape for all three modes (sudo-wrap for systemd, default + override compose file for docker-compose, empty for dev w/ and w/o compose_file), and `generate_sudoers_snippet` covers every unit with absolute `/usr/bin/systemctl` path, never grants stop/restart, ends with a trailing newline for visudo, embeds `SUDOERS_LINE` verbatim. Existing `test_start_services_systemd_success` updated to expect the new `sudo -n` prefix in both the response.command and fake-exec captured argv. Full regression: 17 in `test_bootstrap_start_services.py` + 22 in `test_deploy_mode.py` + 10 in `test_bootstrap_service_tick.py` = **49 passed zero regressions**.)*
  - `systemd` жЁЎејҸпјҡ`sudo systemctl start omnisight-*`пјҲйңҖ K1 зҡ„ scoped sudoersпјү
  - `docker-compose` жЁЎејҸпјҡ`docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d`
  - `dev` жЁЎејҸпјҡи·ійҒҺ start-services stepпјҲе·ІеңЁ uvicorn + next devпјү
- [x] ж–Үд»¶ `docs/ops/bootstrap_modes.md` *(done: new operations doc covers (a) at-a-glance per-mode table mapping detection signal вҶ’ start argv вҶ’ tail argv вҶ’ privilege gate, (b) decision table from `detect_deploy_mode()` (env override вҶ’ in-docker+socket вҶ’ in-docker-only вҶ’ systemd вҶ’ docker вҶ’ dev fallback) with rationale for why systemd beats docker on bare metal + why compose-in-docker beats systemd-in-container, (c) per-mode deep dives: systemd section lists unit files + the K1 sudoers install one-liner that pipes `generate_sudoers_snippet()` output through `visudo -c -f` + `chmod 0440` and a 4-row troubleshooting matrix; docker-compose section covers compose plugin v2 / socket access / `compose_file` override + 4-row troubleshooting; dev section explains when it wins and why `/readyz` still validates even in dev so `dev вҶ’ systemd` migration stays honest, (d) "Source of truth" section linking every file the doc describes (`deploy_mode.py`, `bootstrap.py`'s `_start_command` / `_tick_command` / `generate_sudoers_snippet`, three test modules, cross-refs to `docs/operations/deployment.md` and `cloudflared_service.py`).)*

### L8. йҮҚзҪ® + жё¬и©Ұ
- [x] `POST /api/v1/bootstrap/reset`пјҲadmin йҷҗе®ҡгҖҒdev жЁЎејҸйҷҗе®ҡпјүвҖ” жё… bootstrap_stateгҖҒйҮҚиЁӯ must_change_passwordпјӣз”Ёж–ј QA *(done: new endpoint in `backend/routers/bootstrap.py::bootstrap_reset` gated by `Depends(_au.require_admin)` AND `detect_deploy_mode().mode == "dev"` (refuses with HTTP 403 + body carrying detected `deploy_mode` + `deploy_mode_reason` so QA sees why on non-dev hosts). On success it (1) `DELETE FROM bootstrap_state` via new `bootstrap.reset_bootstrap_state_table()` returning the row count, (2) wipes `data/.bootstrap_state.json` via new `bootstrap.clear_marker()` (clears `smoke_passed`, `cf_tunnel_configured`, `cf_tunnel_skipped`, `bootstrap_finalized` in one shot), (3) re-flags every enabled admin row via new `auth.flag_all_admins_must_change_password()` so the L2 wizard Step 1 gate fires again вҖ” disabled admins skipped because re-flagging an account no one can log into would be a footgun, (4) resets the in-process gate cache so the very next request hits the redirect middleware, (5) emits `bootstrap.reset` audit row with `actor=admin.email`, `severity=warning`, `reason`, the per-admin `admins_reflagged` list, deploy_mode + reason, and counts. Response is `{status, deploy_mode, bootstrap_state_rows_deleted, admins_reflagged, marker_cleared, actor_user_id}`. 8 new tests in `test_bootstrap_reset.py`: helper-level (`reset_bootstrap_state_table` returns row count + leaves table intact + idempotent on empty; `clear_marker` wipes marker + no-op on missing; `flag_all_admins_must_change_password` re-flags enabled admins, skips disabled + viewer rows) plus endpoint (happy path with `OMNISIGHT_DEPLOY_MODE=dev` env override + seeded admin row вҶ’ 200 with all counts; idempotent replay on clean install вҶ’ 200 with zero counts; audit row written with actor + reason + severity + emails; non-dev mode вҶ’ 403 with no DB/marker mutation; non-admin вҶ’ 401/403). Full bootstrap regression: 32 prior tests + 8 new = **40 passed, zero regressions**)*
- [x] E2E Playwrightпјҡе®Ңж•ҙ 5-step wizard иө°е®ҢпјҲmock CF APIгҖҒmock LLM provider pingпјү *(done: new `e2e/l8-bootstrap-wizard.spec.ts` drives every wizard gate end-to-end against a hermetic mock surface installed via `page.route("**/api/v1/bootstrap/**")`. A `BootstrapFlowState` object doubles as the test oracle: each mocked POST mutates it so the next `GET /bootstrap/status` reflects reality, and call-count assertions at the end of the run (`admin_password_calls >= 1`, `llm_provision_calls >= 1`, etc.) catch the regression where a pill paints green without its POST firing. Step-by-step: (1) **admin password** вҖ” fills `omnisight-admin` + a 20-char/4-class strong passphrase that passes `estimatePasswordStrength` with score 4, submits, waits for `bootstrap-step-admin_password` pill `data-state=green`; (2) **LLM provider** вҖ” picks Anthropic, fills a dummy `sk-ant-вҖҰ` key, submits, mocked `provider.ping()` returns a fake fingerprint + latency so the success banner has real data to render, waits for green pill; (3) **Cloudflare Tunnel** вҖ” uses the documented LAN-only skip escape hatch (`cf-tunnel-skip-reveal` вҶ’ fill reason вҶ’ `cf-tunnel-skip-confirm`) so the embedded B12 wizard never opens and the real CF API never fires (a defensive `/api/v1/cloudflare/**` stub catches any future auto-probe); (4) **service health** вҖ” asserts the sidebar pill flips green driven by the all-green mocked parallel-health-check (backend/frontend/db_migration green + cf_tunnel skipped, which the UI treats as green), deliberately skipping the panel body assertion because the auto-advance effect may move past it before Playwright can observe it вҖ” the pill is the durable contract; (5) **smoke subset** вҖ” clicks `bootstrap-smoke-run-button`, mock returns smoke_passed=true with both DAGs green (compile-flash host_native + cross-compile aarch64) and audit_chain.ok=true, waits for the smoke pill green; (6) **finalize** вҖ” auto-advance lands on the Finalize pane, clicks `bootstrap-finalize-button`, asserts `page.waitForURL((url) => !url.pathname.startsWith("/bootstrap"))` confirms the client-side redirect to `/` fired. Also stubs `/api/v1/operation-mode` + `/api/v1/budget-strategy` with benign defaults so the home page after redirect doesn't flake on real backend calls. `playwright.config.ts` extended with pass-through of `OMNISIGHT_DATABASE_PATH` so the spec can point the backend at a temp DB without clobbering the caller's dev data вҖ” run with `OMNISIGHT_PW_LIB_DIR=~/.local/lib/playwright-deps OMNISIGHT_DATABASE_PATH=/tmp/omnisight-e2e-l8/omnisight.db OMNISIGHT_E2E_BACKEND_PORT=18831 OMNISIGHT_E2E_FRONTEND_PORT=3101 npx playwright test e2e/l8-bootstrap-wizard.spec.ts --project=chromium` вҖ” **1 passed in 1.5s** once services are up. Exercise runtime includes the Next.js prod build + uvicorn boot (~30s) but the test itself is fast and hermetic.)*
- [x] йҢҜиӘӨи·Ҝеҫ‘пјҡеҜҶзўјеӨӘејұ / LLM key з„Ўж•Ҳ / systemctl еӨұж•—еҗ„иҮӘ UX *(done: three distinct error-path UXs, each keyed by a machine-readable `kind` so the banner + remediation is chosen without string-parsing `detail`. **Backend** вҖ” `POST /bootstrap/admin-password` now emits `kind` on every failure (409вҶ’`already_rotated`, 401вҶ’`current_password_wrong`, 422вҶ’`password_too_short` when `len < PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH`, 422вҶ’`password_too_weak` otherwise) via a new branch in `backend/routers/bootstrap.py::bootstrap_admin_password`; `POST /bootstrap/start-services` classifies every error into `bad_mode` / `binary_missing` / `timeout` / `sudoers_missing` / `unit_missing` / `unit_failed` вҖ” the systemd-specific kinds (`sudoers_missing` / `unit_missing`) are derived from stderr heuristics (`"a password is required"` / `"no tty present"` / `"sorry, user"` вҶ’ sudoers; `"unit not found"` / `"not loaded"` / `"no such file"` вҶ’ unit; everything else вҶ’ unit_failed). **Frontend client (`lib/api.ts`)** вҖ” new typed errors `BootstrapAdminPasswordError` / `BootstrapStartServicesError` with `kind` + `detail` + (for start-services) `mode` / `command` / `returncode` / `stdout_tail` / `stderr_tail`; new `bootstrapStartServices()` that bypasses `request<T>`'s Error-flattening and parses the JSON kind directly; matching `BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD_KIND_COPY` + `BOOTSTRAP_START_SERVICES_KIND_COPY` records (title+hint pairs); `BOOTSTRAP_PROVIDER_KEY_URL` map (anthropic / openai / azure dashboard URLs) for the LLM `key_invalid` follow-up. **Frontend UI (`app/bootstrap/page.tsx`)** вҖ” (1) `AdminPasswordStep` replaces the plain-text `localError` with a dedicated `AdminPasswordErrorBanner` that picks copy by kind; `password_too_weak` renders an extra zxcvbn-improvement tip panel (`bootstrap-admin-password-weak-tips`); unclassified plain-Error fall-through preserves the original `bootstrap-admin-password-error` testid with `data-kind="unclassified"`. (2) `ProvisionErrorBanner` takes a new `providerId` prop вҖ” on `key_invalid` it renders a one-click `bootstrap-llm-provider-key-url` link (target=_blank, rel=noopener noreferrer) to the provider dashboard, omitted for every other kind since minting a new key isn't the fix for quota / network / 5xx. (3) `ServiceHealthStep` gains a `StartServicesPanel` sub-component mounted between the 4 probe rows and the summary вҖ” exposes `bootstrap-start-services-button` that POSTs start-services, renders `bootstrap-start-services-error` on kind-keyed failure with hint + optional `bootstrap-start-services-stderr` pre block, and `bootstrap-start-services-ok` on success (labels `already running (dev mode)` vs `launched (systemd, rc=0)`). On success the panel calls `onStartResolved` which re-probes parallel-health so a freshly-launched backend flips green without waiting for the 3s interval. **Tests** вҖ” 7 new backend cases in `test_bootstrap_admin_password.py` (4 kind assertions + helper) + 7 new cases in `test_bootstrap_start_services.py` (sudoers_missing / unit_missing / unit_failed catch-all / binary_missing / timeout / bad_mode / docker-compose unit_failed) вҶ’ **183 backend bootstrap tests pass**; 16 new vitest cases in `test/components/bootstrap-page.test.tsx` (4 admin-password kinds + unclassified fall-through + 3 LLM providers key-url link + non-key_invalid no-link + 5 start-services kinds + green-path + dev-mode already_running) all pass when run in isolation вҖ” full frontend suite reports 246/248 with the 2 pre-existing failures unchanged.)*

### L9. Quick-start дёҖйҚөдҪҲзҪІи…іжң¬ (#336)
- [x] `scripts/quick-start.sh`пјҲ428 иЎҢпјҢе·Іж’°еҜ«пјүпјҡ6 жӯҘй©ҹиҮӘеӢ•еҢ–вҖ”вҖ”еүҚзҪ®жӘўжҹҘ вҶ’ .env дә’еӢ•з”ҹжҲҗпјҲLLM provider йҒёж“Ү + API key ијёе…ҘпјүвҶ’ `docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d --build` вҶ’ еҒҘеә·жӘўжҹҘ polling вҶ’ Cloudflare Tunnel иҮӘеӢ•е»әз«ӢпјҲCF API Token вҶ’ tunnel вҶ’ ingress вҶ’ DNS CNAME вҶ’ cloudflared connectorпјүвҶ’ й–ӢзҖҸиҰҪеҷЁ
- [x] WSL2 еҒөжё¬пјҡsystemd е•ҹз”ЁеүҮз”Ё `systemd service`пјӣжңӘе•ҹз”ЁеүҮ `nohup` иғҢжҷҜжЁЎејҸ + жҸҗзӨәдҪҝз”ЁиҖ…е•ҹз”Ё systemd *(done: detection already existed at lines 228-251 of `scripts/quick-start.sh` вҖ” probes `/proc/version` for `microsoft` (vs. binary-only `systemctl` check) + verifies `ps -p 1 -o comm= = systemd` as PID 1. Hardening applied to the downstream branching at lines 604-644: (1) **nohup idempotency** вҖ” persists `CFLARED_PID` to `/tmp/omnisight-cloudflared.pid` so re-runs reuse a live connector instead of spawning a parallel one (which would confuse CF's load balancer + consume double the tunnel slots); re-use path verifies `ps -p $OLD_PID -o comm=` actually matches `cloudflared` before trusting the PID (PID-recycling protection); stale file cleared on fail. (2) **disown** вҖ” backgrounded cloudflared is removed from the shell's job table so Ctrl-C on the script doesn't take the tunnel down; guarded with `|| true` because `disown` exits non-zero when job control is off (e.g. under `sh` or certain CI runners). (3) **namespaced log** вҖ” `/tmp/omnisight-cloudflared.log` instead of generic `/tmp/cloudflared.log` to avoid collision + easier to find. (4) **systemd-enablement guidance** вҖ” now a 3-step copy-pasteable block (edit `/etc/wsl.conf` with heredoc вҶ’ `wsl --shutdown` in PowerShell вҶ’ re-run script to auto-upgrade to systemd service), fired when `IS_WSL=true` but systemd not PID 1. (5) **user-facing copy** вҖ” both branches now state the runtime mode explicitly ("е°Үд»Ҙ systemd service еёёй§җ" vs "е°Үд»Ҙ nohup иғҢжҷҜзЁӢеәҸйҒӢиЎҢ") so the user immediately knows which lifecycle semantics apply. **Tests** вҖ” 5 new source-level + behavioral tests in `tests/test_quick_start_script.py`: `test_wsl_systemd_branching_both_modes_present` (asserts both `cloudflared service install` systemd branch AND `nohup cloudflared tunnel run` branch exist, gated by the same `WSL_SYSTEMD` flag), `test_nohup_branch_is_idempotent` (PID file + `ps -p` recycling check + `disown` all present), `test_systemd_enablement_guidance_is_copy_pasteable` (verifies `/etc/wsl.conf` + `systemd=true` + `wsl --shutdown` are all in the output path), `test_nohup_log_path_is_namespaced` (guards against regressing to generic log path), `test_wsl_detection_simulation` (runs the extracted detection block against stubbed `grep`+`ps` to simulate both non-WSL+systemd and WSL2+no-systemd scenarios вҖ” confirms `WSL_SYSTEMD` and `IS_WSL` get set correctly in each case AND that the systemd-enablement tips actually print to stdout in the WSL2-no-systemd case). All 19 quick-start tests pass (14 prior + 5 new) in 1.55s; `bash -n` clean; live `--dry-run` on WSL2+systemd host prints the new "cloudflared е°Үд»Ҙ systemd service еёёй§җ" message.)*
- [x] GoDaddy NS йҒ·з§»жҢҮеј•пјҡи…іжң¬е…§еҚ°еҮәжё…жҘҡжӯҘй©ҹпјҲGoDaddy Dashboard вҶ’ Nameservers вҶ’ еЎ« CF NSпјүпјҢйҖҷжӯҘз„Ўжі•иҮӘеӢ•еҢ– *(done: Step 5 of `scripts/quick-start.sh` rewritten from a bare 4-line guide into a tri-state, idempotent, fully-guided walkthrough. **What's auto-detected** вҖ” the script probes current NS via `dig +short +time=3 +tries=1 NS $DOMAIN @1.1.1.1` (forces 1.1.1.1 so a broken WSL2 `/etc/resolv.conf` doesn't poison detection; falls back to resolv.conf resolver on timeout) with `host -t NS`/`nslookup -type=NS` fallbacks if `dig` isn't installed. Tolerates trailing dots (`dig` returns `ns.cloudflare.com.`). Counts how many NS are `ns.cloudflare.com` vs total вҶ’ classifies into 4 states: `all-cf` (done вҖ” skip with `вң“ NS йҒ·з§»е·Іе®ҢжҲҗпјҢи·ійҒҺжӯӨжӯҘй©ҹ` + detected NS list), `mixed` (mid-propagation вҖ” show ratio like `1/2 е·ІжҢҮеҗ‘ Cloudflare` + advise waiting 30min-4h + verify-by-`dig`/whatsmydns link), `non-cf` (still on GoDaddy вҖ” fire full walkthrough), `unknown` (no lookup tool available / query failed вҖ” also fire full walkthrough as safe default). **What's new in the walkthrough** вҖ” (A) The script prints the actual two Cloudflare NS values verbatim (no tab-switch to CF dashboard needed). These are captured by a new `CF_NAMESERVERS=()` global initialized at line 423 alongside `CF_READY=false`, populated in the CF setup block at line 491 via `jq -r '.result[0].name_servers // [] | .[]'` with output captured to `CF_NS_RAW` first (so jq failure doesn't silently null the array through process-substitution + set -e), then `while IFS= read -r` loop (not `readarray` вҖ” bash 3.2 compat for macOS hosts). If the CF block was skipped (no token / zone not found / empty), `CF_NAMESERVERS` stays empty and Step 5 gracefully falls back to "look them up in CF dashboard" with explicit URL. (B) GoDaddy UI walkthrough updated to the 2024+ portfolio URL `https://dcc.godaddy.com/control/portfolio` (old `manage/${DOMAIN}/dns` URL kept as secondary) with exact modern menu path: `вӢ® вҶ’ DNS вҶ’ Nameservers tab вҶ’ Change Nameservers вҶ’ "I'll use my own nameservers"`. Includes the "this will temporarily break your domain" warning GoDaddy shows (confirming it's normal). (C) Verification section prints `dig NS ${DOMAIN} +short` and links https://www.whatsmydns.net/#NS/${DOMAIN} with Cyan highlighting + notes CF zone status flipping `Pending вҶ’ Active` is the authoritative signal. (D) Two footgun warnings, both hit us previously: ${BOLD}DNSSEC must be disabled at GoDaddy before cutover${NC} (DNSSEC mismatch will brick the domain globally вҖ” verify with `dig DS $DOMAIN +short`), and ${BOLD}MX/TXT email records must be preserved${NC} on CF (email silently breaks on NS switch otherwise вҖ” tell users to confirm CF auto-import in CF Dashboard вҶ’ DNS before cutover). (E) A post-cutover `curl -I https://${DOMAIN}` smoke check printed only when `CF_READY=true` (ingress + DNS CNAME already created by the script). **Tests** вҖ” 6 new source+behavioral tests in `tests/test_quick_start_script.py`: `test_cf_nameservers_captured_from_zones_api` (asserts CF_NAMESERVERS=(), .result[0].name_servers in jq query, no `readarray` for bash 3.2 compat), `test_ns_auto_detect_uses_multiple_tools` (asserts bounded dig `+time=3 +tries=1`, `@1.1.1.1` public resolver, + host/nslookup fallbacks), `test_ns_state_classification_is_tri_state` (asserts all 3 states all-cf/mixed/non-cf + idempotent skip copy), `test_godaddy_ui_walkthrough_has_current_menu_path` (asserts 2024+ portfolio URL + exact radio label `I'll use my own nameservers`), `test_verification_commands_are_printed` (asserts `dig NS ${DOMAIN} +short`, whatsmydns.net, DNSSEC warning, MX warning), `test_cf_nameservers_graceful_fallback_when_empty` (source-level assert of `${#CF_NAMESERVERS[@]} -gt 0` guard + dash.cloudflare.com fallback copy, PLUS behavioral test: stubs `command() { return 1; }` to simulate no-lookup-tools host with empty CF_NAMESERVERS, runs the extracted Step 5 block, asserts "жңӘеҸ–еҫ— CF API Token" fallback copy fires + CF dashboard URL printed вҖ” guards the degradation path most likely to regress silently). All 25 tests pass in 1.60s (19 prior + 6 new); `bash -n` clean; live `--dry-run` on WSL2+systemd host clean; 4-scenario smoke of the extracted block (all-cf / mixed / non-cf / unknown-nolookup) visually confirmed correct output for each branch.)*
- [x] еҶӘзӯүпјҡйҮҚиӨҮеҹ·иЎҢдёҚеЈһпјҲ.env е·ІеӯҳеңЁи·ійҒҺ / tunnel е·ІеӯҳеңЁиӨҮз”Ё / DNS CNAME е·ІеӯҳеңЁ skipпјү *(done: full idempotency contract audited + hardened + pinned with 7 new tests. **Audit result** вҖ” 3 of the 3 explicit criteria were already implemented: `.env` skip at `scripts/quick-start.sh:291` (`if [ -f ".env" ]; then` вҶ’ log е·ІеӯҳеңЁпјҢи·ійҒҺз”ҹжҲҗ), tunnel reuse at `scripts/quick-start.sh:520-528` (queries `tunnels?name=${TUNNEL_NAME}&is_deleted=false` + extracts `.result[0].id` + fetches token for existing tunnel via `/tunnels/${CF_TUNNEL_ID}/token` + announces "Tunnel е·ІеӯҳеңЁ: ${CF_TUNNEL_ID}пјҢиӨҮз”Ё"), and DNS CNAME "already exists" skip via `grep -qi "already exists"` on the error path. **Hardening applied** вҖ” the CNAME path had a subtle silent-failure mode: if a prior run created a CNAME pointing at tunnel-A, then tunnel-A got deleted out-of-band (e.g. via CF dashboard) and re-running created tunnel-B, the POST would fail with "already exists", the script would log "и·ійҒҺ" вҖ” but the CNAME would still point at dead tunnel-A and the site would return 1016 forever. The only fix was a manual DNS edit. Rewrote `scripts/quick-start.sh:572-621` as a three-case state machine: (POST succeeds вҶ’ log е·Іе»әз«Ӣ) / (POST fails + already exists + GET returns matching `.content` вҶ’ log "е·ІеӯҳеңЁдё”жҢҮеҗ‘з•¶еүҚ tunnelпјҢи·ійҒҺ" as true no-op) / (POST fails + already exists + GET returns DIFFERENT `.content` вҶ’ log "еҒөжё¬еҲ° CNAME жјӮз§»пјҡзӣ®еүҚжҢҮеҗ‘ XпјҢжӣҙж–°зӮә Y" + PATCH the record by id вҶ’ log "CNAME е·Іжӣҙж–°иҮіз•¶еүҚ tunnel"). Drift-repair uses `/dns_records?type=CNAME&name=${HOSTNAME}` to fetch the existing record, `jq -r '.result[0].content // empty'` to extract content (empty fallback so set -u doesn't explode), and `curl -X PATCH /dns_records/${EXISTING_ID}` with identical body shape as the original POST. Defensive: if GET returns empty id (record gone between POST and GET вҖ” impossible in practice but set -e would crash without the guard), fall back to "з„Ўжі•жҹҘи©ўе…§е®№пјҢдҝқе®Ҳи·ійҒҺ" and warn instead of attempting a PATCH with null id. **Other idempotent paths audited + confirmed safe** вҖ” Ingress uses `-X PUT` (replace-style; CF semantics вҶ’ same body in = same state out, so re-runs are a no-op by API design) not POST; cloudflared `.deb` install gated by `if ! command -v cloudflared` with "cloudflared е·Іе®үиЈқ" in the else branch; cloudflared systemd install uses `|| true` on the install step + `systemctl restart` to pick up new config; cloudflared nohup branch already hardened in the prior L9#4 task with `/tmp/omnisight-cloudflared.pid` + `ps -p $OLD_PID -o comm=` recycling check + `disown`. `docker compose up -d --build` is idempotent by design (won't create duplicates; will rebuild if config changed). Error-path banner at line 100 explicitly tells users "йҮҚж–°еҹ·иЎҢжӯӨи…іжң¬пјҡе•ҸйЎҢдҝ®еҫ©еҫҢйҮҚи·‘еҚіеҸҜпјҲи…іжң¬ж”ҜжҸҙеҶӘзӯүпјү" so they know a clean re-run is safe. **Tests** вҖ” 7 new source-level + behavioral tests in `tests/test_quick_start_script.py`: (1) `test_idempotency_env_skip_source_guard` вҖ” asserts the `.env` existence guard + "е·ІеӯҳеңЁпјҢи·ійҒҺз”ҹжҲҗ" copy + crucially, that no `cp .env.example .env` appears BEFORE the guard (regression guard against accidentally clobbering a user's customized .env on re-run). (2) `test_idempotency_tunnel_reuse_source_guard` вҖ” asserts `is_deleted=false` in the probe URL (catches regressions that would reuse a soft-deleted tunnel), the `.result[0].id // empty` extraction, the "Tunnel е·ІеӯҳеңЁ" copy, the `/tunnels/${CF_TUNNEL_ID}/token` fetch for existing tunnels, and exactly 1 POST to `.../accounts/{id}/tunnels` (a regression adding a second POST URL would duplicate tunnel creation). (3) `test_idempotency_ingress_uses_put_not_post` вҖ” asserts `-X PUT` within the ingress block (slices between "иЁӯе®ҡ Tunnel ingress" and "DNS CNAME" markers so drift elsewhere doesn't pollute the assertion). (4) `test_idempotency_cname_already_exists_branch_source_guard` вҖ” asserts `grep -qi "already exists"` detection, the GET URL with `type=CNAME&name=${HOSTNAME}`, `EXISTING_CONTENT=` capture, `-X PATCH` drift-repair call, both success-skip copy ("е·ІеӯҳеңЁдё”жҢҮеҗ‘з•¶еүҚ tunnelпјҢи·ійҒҺ") and drift-detection breadcrumb ("еҒөжё¬еҲ° CNAME жјӮз§»"). (5) `test_idempotency_cloudflared_install_skipped_if_present` вҖ” gates on `if ! command -v cloudflared` + "cloudflared е·Іе®үиЈқ" else-branch copy. (6) `test_idempotency_full_rerun_announces_support_in_error_copy` вҖ” guards the "ж”ҜжҸҙеҶӘзӯү" string in the error banner so users aren't left wondering if a failed run corrupted state. (7) `test_idempotency_cname_drift_repair_behavioral` вҖ” the crown-jewel behavioral test: extracts the CNAME loop, wraps it in a bash harness that stubs `curl` with scenario-driven canned responses (happy / already_match / drift), logs every curl invocation to a temp file, and asserts the exact (method, URL) sequence for each case. Scenario 1 (happy): 2 POSTs, 0 GETs, 0 PATCHes вҶ’ logs "CNAME е·Іе»әз«Ӣ". Scenario 2 (already-match): 2 POSTs (fail) + 2 GETs (verify matching content), 0 PATCHes вҶ’ logs "е·ІеӯҳеңЁдё”жҢҮеҗ‘з•¶еүҚ tunnelпјҢи·ійҒҺ" (critical: guards against a regression that would PATCH unnecessarily and churn DNS). Scenario 3 (drift): 2 POSTs (fail) + 2 GETs (detect drift) + 2 PATCHes (repair) вҶ’ logs "еҒөжё¬еҲ° CNAME жјӮз§»" AND "CNAME е·Іжӣҙж–°иҮіз•¶еүҚ tunnel". Full `tests/test_quick_start_script.py` suite: 32 passed in 1.63s (25 prior + 7 new); `bash -n scripts/quick-start.sh` clean; `./scripts/quick-start.sh --dry-run` on WSL2+systemd host passes all preflight checks + announces "жүҖжңүеүҚзҪ®жўқд»¶йҖҡйҒҺ вң“".)*
- [x] еҹҹеҗҚеҸҜиЁӯе®ҡпјҡ`OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN=sora-dev.app` env жҲ–и…іжң¬е…§ default *(done: audited the existing `DOMAIN="${OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN:-sora-dev.app}"` baseline at `scripts/quick-start.sh:36` вҶ’ extended to a full override+validation+banner contract across all three related knobs. **What was already working** вҖ” `DOMAIN` honored the `OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN` env with `sora-dev.app` default via the `:-` expansion, and the value propagated to every downstream consumer (CF zone lookup at line 483, ingress config at 557-558, CNAME loop at 584, NS probe at 759-767, GoDaddy walkthrough at 818-884, final summary at 922). **What was missing / added** вҖ” (1) **`API_SUBDOMAIN` + `TUNNEL_NAME` also env-overridable** вҖ” `API_SUBDOMAIN="${OMNISIGHT_API_SUBDOMAIN:-api}"` + `TUNNEL_NAME="${OMNISIGHT_TUNNEL_NAME:-omnisight-prod}"` so users running staging+prod on one CF account can pick distinct tunnel names without forking (`TUNNEL_NAME` collision in the previous implementation would silently reuse one account's tunnel from the other вҶ’ classic "why is my staging traffic hitting prod?" footgun). (2) **`_strip_ws` helper** вҖ” `OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN=" sora-dev.app "` from a quoted .env value (common paste-error from dashboard snippets) now gets leading/trailing whitespace stripped before validation. Uses the POSIX `${s#"${s%%[![:space:]]*}"}` + `${s%"${s##*[![:space:]]}"}` idiom (bash 3.2 portable вҖ” no `[[ :-: ]]` extended globs). (3) **`_validate_domain`** вҖ” rejects (a) empty, (b) > 253 chars (RFC 1035 upper bound), (c) values containing `://`, `/`, or whitespace via `case` glob so `OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN=https://foo.com` (URL pasted by mistake) fails with a friendly message BEFORE any CF API call, (d) uppercase (CF would normalize but we reject for clarity вҖ” users should see the exact value they typed reflected back), (e) single-label hostnames like `localhost` via an RFC 1035-ish regex `^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?(\.[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?)+$` which requires at least one dot and per-label length 1-63. (4) **`_validate_api_subdomain`** вҖ” single DNS label, no dots (so `OMNISIGHT_API_SUBDOMAIN=foo.bar` which would create a weird `foo.bar.DOMAIN` nested host is rejected), 1-63 chars. (5) **`_validate_tunnel_name`** вҖ” 1-32 chars (mirrors CF dashboard UI limit; the API is more permissive but we mirror the UI so the tunnel name in CF dashboard doesn't look weird), `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+`. Each validator emits a per-knob diagnostic that names WHICH env var is wrong + WHY + shows a valid example вҖ” generic "invalid config" was explicitly avoided because users reading it would blame the wrong variable. (6) **Placement matters** вҖ” the validation calls run AFTER `--help`/`--uninstall` CLI parsing (so `OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN=bogus ./script --help` still works) and AFTER `LOG_FILE` init (so `err()`'s `tee -a "$LOG_FILE"` has a real file to write to). Fails fast (exit 1) with no trap noise because validation runs before the `trap _cleanup_on_exit EXIT` is installed. (7) **Deployment banner** вҖ” echoes the three resolved values (`Domain:`, `API subdomain:`, `Tunnel name:`) right after validation so the operator sees the effective config before any side-effecting Docker/CF work; when all three are defaults, a trailing hint mentions how to override them вҖ” gives the "last chance to Ctrl-C" checkpoint that caught two footguns during local testing. (8) **`--help` extended** вҖ” from 3 lines to a 12-line block listing every env var with its default and a concrete combined-usage example `OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN=app.example.com OMNISIGHT_TUNNEL_NAME=omnisight-staging ./scripts/quick-start.sh`. **Tests** вҖ” 8 new tests in `tests/test_quick_start_script.py` (extends the L9 contract-pin pattern; counts are source+behavioral): (A) `test_domain_env_override_source_guard` вҖ” pins the `${VAR:-default}` expansion for all three knobs, catches regressions that hardcode the default. (B) `test_validators_exist_for_all_three_knobs` вҖ” asserts the 3 validator defs + their call sites + the 3 `_strip_ws` calls are all wired (catches half-done refactors). (C) `test_help_documents_env_vars` вҖ” runs `--help` end-to-end and greps for all 3 env vars, their defaults, and the usage-example form. (D) `test_deployment_banner_prints_resolved_values` вҖ” behavioral: runs `--dry-run` twice (once with defaults, once with overrides) and asserts the banner reflects both correctly + the "е…ЁйғЁзӮәй җиЁӯеҖј" hint fires only on the defaults path. (E) `test_invalid_env_values_rejected_with_clear_message` вҖ” 6-case parametrize exercising every rejection branch (URL paste, single-label host, uppercase, two-label subdomain, space in tunnel name, 33-char tunnel name) + asserts the per-knob diagnostic string fires. (F) `test_whitespace_stripped_from_env_values` вҖ” feeds `"  sora-dev.app  "` via env and asserts validation accepts + banner echoes the stripped form with no trailing spaces (regression guard). (G) `test_domain_propagates_to_all_downstream_consumers` вҖ” pins that `$DOMAIN` flows into the CF zones API URL + CNAME iterator + NS probe + final summary; also asserts no lingering literal `sora-dev.app` appears in any downstream consumer with an explicit allowlist for (i) the default-assignment line, (ii) `--help` copy, (iii) validator error-message example text. (H) `test_validators_accept_common_valid_domains` вҖ” sources the validator block into a harness and exercises each against a realistic positive set (`sora-dev.app`, `app.example.com`, `a.b.c.d`, `foo-bar.example.co.uk`, 60-char label; api/v2/app/a/x-y; omnisight-prod/omnisight_staging/abc123/32-char name) вҖ” catches regex-regression cases where source-grep passes but real input rejects. Full `tests/test_quick_start_script.py` suite: **45 passed in 4.26s** (32 prior + 13 new вҖ” note: 13 instead of 8 because the parametrize adds 6 cases counted individually); `bash -n scripts/quick-start.sh` clean; `./scripts/quick-start.sh --help` shows the new env-var section; `./scripts/quick-start.sh --dry-run` announces banner + preflight passes; 4 live negative-path dry-runs (`OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN=https://foo.com`, `localhost`, `OMNISIGHT_API_SUBDOMAIN=foo.bar`, `OMNISIGHT_TUNNEL_NAME='bad name'`) all reject with correct per-knob diagnostic; 1 live positive-path dry-run (`OMNISIGHT_DOMAIN=app.example.com OMNISIGHT_TUNNEL_NAME=omnisight-staging`) shows banner reflecting overrides without the "е…ЁйғЁзӮәй җиЁӯеҖј" hint.)*

projectпјҲ`.env.example` + `docker-compose.prod.yml` + pre-existing `.env`пјүгҖҒstub `docker` commandпјҲsubcommand йғҪ fake-successпјҢ`compose ps --services --status=running` еҚ° `backend\\nfrontend` еҲҶж”ҜпјүгҖҒ**ж•…ж„ҸдёҚ**жҸҗдҫӣ `explorer.exe` жҲ– `xdg-open`пјҲй©—иӯү headless fallbackпјүгҖҒ`PATH=$stubs:$PATH` + `stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL`пјҲйқһ TTY вҶ’ и§ёзҷј `NON_INTERACTIVE=true`пјүпјҢи·‘е®Ңж•ҙи…іжң¬ assert exit 0 + гҖҢжүҖжңүеүҚзҪ®жўқд»¶йҖҡйҒҺгҖҚ+гҖҢ.env е·ІеӯҳеңЁгҖҚ+гҖҢе®№еҷЁе·Іе•ҹеӢ•гҖҚ+гҖҢBackend е°ұз·’гҖҚ+гҖҢFrontend еӮійҒҺеҺ»гҖӮ**й—ңйҚөйҷ·йҳұ**пјҡжңҖеҲқеҳ—и©Ұ `pip wheel --require-hashes` е…ҲеңЁ builder з·ЁеҮә /wheels еҶҚеңЁ runner `pip install --no-index --find-links=/wheels` вҖ”вҖ” еӨұж•—пјҢеӣ зӮә `pip wheel` жңғи®“жҜҸеҖӢ request еӣһ 502пјӣ`${PORT:-8000}` зҡ„ fallback и®“ `railway up` еңЁжң¬ең° Railway CLI жЁЎејҸпјҲдёҚжңғжіЁе…Ҙ $PORTпјүд»Қз„¶еҸҜд»Ҙиө·дҫҶпјҢ`OMNISIGHT_WORKERS:-2` дҝқз•ҷ basic tier зҡ„ sizing й җиЁӯпјӣ(d) `deploy.healthcheckPath: "/api/v1/health"` вҖ” е°ҚйҪҠ `backend/main.py:551` зҡ„ `app.include_router(health.router, prefix=settings.api_prefix)` еҠ дёҠ `backend/config.py` зҡ„ `api_prefix: str = "/api/v1"`пјҲеҘ‘зҙ„жё¬и©Ұ `test_deploy_healthcheck_points_at_real_endpoint` pin жӯ»пјҢtypo жңғи®“ Railway rollout ж°ёйҒ  redпјүпјӣ(e) `deploy.restartPolicyType: "ON_FAILURE"` + йғЁзҪІеҚұйҡӘпјүпјӣ(f) `numReplicas: 1` + `sleepApplication: false` жҳҺзўә pin и®“ live spec з…§жҳ еҮәдҫҶ вҖ” operator еңЁ Railway UI ж„ҸеӨ–й–ӢдәҶ auto-scale жҷӮжңғйҖ жҲҗ drift дҪҶ config з„Ўж„ҹпјҢйҖҷиЈЎ pin и®“дёӢж¬Ў deploy жҠҠе®ғжӢүеӣһгҖӮ**env var зӯ–з•ҘеӨ§иҪүжҸӣ** вҖ” Railway зҡ„ config-as-code жІ’жңү env/variables schemaпјҲ**е®Ңе…Ёз”ұ dashboard/CLI з®Ў**пјҢдёҚиғҪеңЁ JSON - [x] жҜҸеҖӢе№іеҸ°е®ҡзҫ© servicesпјҲbackend + frontendпјү+ env varsпјҲfrom `.env.example`пјү+ build commands *(done: L11 #4 вҖ” cross-platform parity contract `tests/test_deploy_parity_across_platforms.py` (22 cases, **22 passed in 0.02 s**, 93 passed combined with three sibling L11 suites вҖ” 27 DO + 16 Railway + 28 Render + 22 parity) attacks the one invariant the per-platform suites **structurally cannot catch**: drift between the three platforms. Per-platform tests each see exactly one spec file, so if a future PR adds a new production-required env to DO + Render but silently forgets Railway's dashboard-managed README matrix (Railway's `railway.json` schema has no env block вҖ” env vars live exclusively in `deploy/railway/README.md`'s operator-facing table), every per-platform suite stays green while the Railway operator gets a broken first-boot config-validation failure. **Triple-dimension contract** mirrors the TODO text: **(1) services** вҖ” `test_digitalocean_declares_backend_and_frontend_services` + `test_render_declares_backend_and_frontend_services` parse the spec `services:` list and assert both service names are present (DO uses bare `backend`/`frontend`, Render uses `omnisight-backend`/`omnisight-frontend` for pserv DNS determinism); `test_railway_topology_documents_both_services` checks the README text for both service names + both Dockerfile paths (Railway's asymmetric `railway.json` only configures backend, so frontend topology lives in the README вҖ” this is the sibling `test_spec_does_not_embed_env_block` in the Railway suite's mirror at the parity layer). **(2) env vars from .env.example** вҖ” module-level constant `CRITICAL_BACKEND_ENVS` lists the 8 production-required envs sourced directly from `.env.example`'s Internet-exposure block (OMNISIGHT_DEBUG / AUTH_MODE / COOKIE_SECURE / ADMIN_EMAIL / ADMIN_PASSWORD / LLM_PROVIDER / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / FRONTEND_ORIGIN) + `CRITICAL_FRONTEND_ENVS` (NODE_ENV + BACKEND_URL); `test_env_example_defines_the_critical_backend_envs` is a sanity gate that the list hasn't drifted from the source-of-truth; then per platform: DO + Render parse `envs:` / `envVars:` keys and `issubset` against the critical list (DO/Render); Railway scans `deploy/railway/README.md` text for each env name (README is Railway's only env matrix). **Symmetric frontend coverage** вҖ” `test_digitalocean_frontend_covers_critical_frontend_envs` / `test_railway_frontend_env_matrix_covers_critical_frontend_envs` / `test_render_frontend_covers_critical_frontend_envs` вҖ” catches the footgun "platform X defines BACKEND_URL but forgets Y" (Railway's frontend has NO JSON so README is the only place that can miss this). **(3) build commands** вҖ” `test_both_dockerfiles_exist_at_repo_root` pins the foundation (the entire tri-platform parity collapses if `Dockerfile.backend` / `Dockerfile.frontend` are renamed/deleted); then per platform: DO asserts `dockerfile_path == "Dockerfile.backend"` / `"Dockerfile.frontend"` (DO schema key); Railway asserts `build.builder == "DOCKERFILE"` (else NIXPACKS auto-detect rebuilds from source ignoring the Dockerfile) + `build.dockerfilePath == "Dockerfile.backend"` + README documents `Dockerfile.frontend` (Railway's frontend Dockerfile selection happens via dashboard); Render asserts both `dockerfilePath` values (with `lstrip("./")` to accept either `./Dockerfile.backend` or `Dockerfile.backend` вҖ” Render accepts both); `test_backend_start_commands_all_name_uvicorn_on_backend_main` parses Railway `deploy.startCommand` + Render `dockerCommand` and asserts both contain `uvicorn` + `backend.main:app` (DO inherits Dockerfile CMD verbatim, pinned by `test_dockerfile_image_size.py`). **Production hard-pin value parity** вҖ” `PRODUCTION_HARD_PIN_VALUES` dict maps the three `.env.example` Internet-exposure envs to their required values (DEBUG=false / AUTH_MODE=strict / COOKIE_SECURE=true), then **three symmetric tests** вҖ” `test_digitalocean_production_hard_pins_match_env_example` + `test_render_production_hard_pins_match_env_example` parse spec envs and compare values with `_normalize_env_value()` helper that handles YAML bool vs quoted-string ambiguity (Render allows `value: "false"` OR `value: false` вҖ” both load to different Python types; normalize to lowercase string); `test_railway_production_hard_pins_documented_with_values` scans the README with line-level regex (every line containing the env name must ALSO contain the expected value вҖ” catches "row drops the value cell while keeping the name"). **Meta discovery tests** вҖ” `test_all_three_platform_dirs_exist` (fails BEFORE any per-platform suite can fixture-skip вҖ” so deleting `deploy/<platform>/` is caught at the parity layer) + `test_all_three_deploy_buttons_present_in_root_readme` (checks `cloud.digitalocean.com/apps/new` / `railway.com/new/template` / `render.com/deploy` URLs all in main README вҖ” users land on README not the subdirs). **Cross-platform secret hygiene** вҖ” `test_no_live_api_keys_leaked_across_any_platform_spec` scans all 6 deploy files (3 specs + 3 READMEs) for real key prefixes (`sk-ant-api` / `sk-proj-` / `AIzaSy` / `xai-`) with context-aware filter: the README instructional string `"sk-ant-..."` passes (after-prefix char is `.`), `"sk-ant-apiabc123..."` fails (after-prefix first-non-dot char is alnum). Per-platform suites each have `test_no_plaintext_api_keys_in_spec` вҖ” this parity-layer check surfaces the violation with all-three paths in one error message for one-grep triage. **Module-level `CRITICAL_BACKEND_ENVS` list + `CRITICAL_FRONTEND_ENVS` list + `PRODUCTION_HARD_PIN_VALUES` dict** вҖ” these are the contract **surface area**: add a new env to `.env.example`'s Internet-exposure block and you update ONE list here; the three per-platform assertions then trigger and point each platform's owner at the diff. This replaces the alternative of "duplicate the env name in three per-platform suites" which would have immediate drift. **Zero new runtime dep** вҖ” PyYAML already in backend/requirements.txt + stdlib `json`/`pathlib`/`pytest`. **Scope иҮӘеҫӢ** вҖ” one new file `tests/test_deploy_parity_across_platforms.py` (344 иЎҢпјҢеҢ…еҗ« docstringгҖҒfixturesгҖҒ20 еҖӢ assertion tests + 2 еҖӢ meta test + shared helpers `_do_service` / `_do_env_keys` / `_render_service` / `_render_env_keys` / `_normalize_env_value`)пјӣжІ’еӢ•д»»дҪ•жҮүз”Ёзўј / Dockerfile / compose / workflow / quick-start.sh / .env.example / ж—ўжңү DO/Railway/Render spec жҲ–дёүд»Ҫ README жҲ–дёүд»Ҫ sibling test вҖ” зҙ”зІ№ add-only зҡ„ parity еҘ‘зҙ„еұӨгҖӮ**Verification run**пјҡ`python3 -m pytest tests/test_deploy_parity_across_platforms.py tests/test_digitalocean_app_spec.py tests/test_railway_spec.py tests/test_render_blueprint.py -v` вҶ’ **93 passed in 0.10 s**пјҲ22 new + 71 siblingпјүпјҢйӣ¶ regression)*
- [x] README.md еҠ  Deploy жҢүйҲ• badgesпјҲone-click и·іиҪүеҲ°е№іеҸ°дҪҲзҪІй Ғпјү *(done: L11 #5 вҖ” badges themselves already landed incidentally during L11 #1/#2/#3 (each spec phase co-shipped its own button), but the **button UX was only pinned by a single presence-check assertion** in `test_deploy_parity_across_platforms.py::test_all_three_deploy_buttons_present_in_root_readme` that checks bare URL substrings вҖ” which passes even if someone reduces the rendered affordance to a text link, swaps to an off-brand badge mirror, drops alt text, or points the one-click URL at the wrong repo/branch. This step closes that coverage gap with a dedicated `tests/test_readme_deploy_buttons.py` (11 cases, **11 passed in 0.03 s**, 104 passed combined with 4 L11 siblings) focused **only on the button surface itself**, partitioned into 6 sections: (1) **dedicated section** вҖ” `test_deploy_section_is_near_top_of_readme` extracts the `### One-click cloud deploy` block with a single regex (`r"###\s+One-click cloud deploy.*?(?=\n##\s|\n###\s|\Z)"`) and asserts it starts before line 200 (currently at line 94; >200 lines means the section drifted into the appendix where first-time visitors won't see it), `test_only_one_deploy_section` asserts exactly 1 occurrence (prevents a future PR from duplicating the block into an appendix and splitting user attention); (2) **image-badge markdown** вҖ” `BADGE_MARKDOWN_RE = r"\[!\[(?P<alt>[^\]]+)\]\((?P<badge>https?://[^\s)]+)\)\]\((?P<href>https?://[^\s)]+)\)"` parses each `[![alt](badge.svg)](deploy-url)` triple and keys it by deploy-URL host (digitalocean.com / railway.com / render.com) вҖ” `test_all_three_platforms_have_image_badges` asserts all three platforms produce image-badge matches (catches the regression where someone "fixes" a rendering bug by converting `[![alt](svg)](url)` вҶ’ `[text](url)` and loses the visual affordance entirely), `test_each_badge_has_non_empty_alt_text` asserts non-empty alt text вүҘ6 chars (accessibility + graceful SVG-404 fallback); (3) **official provider SVG hosts** вҖ” `EXPECTED_BADGE_HOSTS = {digitalocean: "www.deploytodo.com", railway: "railway.com", render: "render.com"}` вҖ” `test_badges_served_from_official_provider_hosts` asserts each badge URL contains the expected host AND ends with `.svg` (provider-rebrand survival: Render rebranded its badge in 2024 and Railway in 2025 вҖ” pointing at the provider's own URL auto-updates; img.shields.io approximations or third-party mirrors rot); (4) **canonical-repo URL targets** вҖ” three per-platform tests (`test_digitalocean_deploy_url_points_at_canonical_repo` / `test_railway_deploy_url_points_at_canonical_repo` / `test_render_deploy_url_points_at_canonical_repo`) each verify the URL shape specific to that provider: DO asserts `cloud.digitalocean.com/apps/new` prefix + `limit5/OmniSight-Productizer` slug + `tree/master` branch (matching `app.yaml`'s `github.repo`/`github.branch`), Railway asserts `railway.com/new/template` prefix + `template=` query + canonical slug accepting either raw or URL-encoded (`limit5%2FOmniSight-Productizer`), Render asserts `render.com/deploy` prefix + `repo=` query + canonical slug (no branch segment because Render reads the default branch from the Blueprint file itself вҖ” DO and Render have different URL shapes and this test encodes each); **why this matters** вҖ” a rename of the GitHub org (or a fork) would currently let the sibling parity test pass while silently routing clicks to the wrong repo; (5) **companion runbook + spec links** вҖ” `COMPANION_RUNBOOKS` + `COMPANION_SPECS` dicts map each platform to its `deploy/<platform>/README.md` + spec file (`app.yaml` / `railway.json` / `render.yaml`), then `test_deploy_section_links_to_every_companion_runbook` + `test_deploy_section_links_to_every_platform_spec` assert each path appears in the section text AND `.is_file()` on disk (closes the gap where a file rename passes every per-platform test вҖ” they read the file directly by its new path вҖ” but leaves a 404 in README); (6) **visual hierarchy** вҖ” `test_badges_appear_before_runbook_links_in_section` uses `.find("[![")` + `.find("\n- ")` and asserts the badge offset < bullet offset (primary CTA before reference material; reversing the order buries the one-click button below a wall of text). **Contract partition rationale** вҖ” `test_deploy_parity_across_platforms.py::test_all_three_deploy_buttons_present_in_root_readme` keeps the substring-level presence check (simple regression detector), this new file handles structural/UX/target-integrity concerns; the two files address different regression classes and neither is redundant. **Zero new runtime dep** вҖ” stdlib `re` + `pathlib` + `pytest`. **Scope иҮӘеҫӢ** вҖ” one new file `tests/test_readme_deploy_buttons.py` (277 иЎҢ) + 0 changes to `README.md` (badges already present and pass all 11 assertions as-shipped from L11 #1/#2/#3, which is the ideal outcome вҖ” the section reached the target state before the coverage assertions landed, confirming the three earlier phases delivered the desired UX); 0 changes to any spec / runbook / application code. **Verification run**пјҡ`python3 -m pytest tests/test_readme_deploy_buttons.py tests/test_deploy_parity_across_platforms.py tests/test_digitalocean_app_spec.py tests/test_railway_spec.py tests/test_render_blueprint.py -v` вҶ’ **104 passed in 0.11 s**пјҲ11 new + 93 siblingпјүпјҢйӣ¶ regression)*

user-readable platform name вҖ” е…©еұӨйҳІзҰҰиҰҶи“ӢдёҚеҗҢ regressionпјүгҖӮ**еҘ‘зҙ„еҲҶеұӨ rationale** вҖ” L11 #1-#5 жҜҸеҖӢ sibling еҗ„зңӢиҮӘе·ұзҡ„дёҖи§’пјҲsingle platform / UX surfaceпјүпјҢжӯӨ capstone **зөҗж§ӢдёҠдёҚйҮҚиӨҮ** sibling зҡ„е·ҘдҪңпјҢеҸӘзңӢ sibling **зө„еҗҲиө·дҫҶжҳҜеҗҰж»ҝи¶ій©—ж”¶**пјҡbudget arithmetic / acceptance text preservation / дёүе№іеҸ°е°ҚзЁұжҖ§ / K1 bootstrap wiring / sibling inventory / no new dep / emergent 3-min chain вҖ” йҖҷ 7 еҖӢз¶ӯеәҰжІ’жңүд»»дҪ•дёҖеҖӢеҸҜд»Ҙеҫһ single sibling е°ҺеҮәгҖӮ**Scope иҮӘеҫӢ** вҖ” еҸӘж–°еўһ 1 еҖӢжӘ”жЎҲ `tests/test_l11_budget_capstone.py`пјҲ~420 иЎҢпјҢ27 assertion tests + 3 pytest.fixture + 2 module constant + 1 shared PLATFORMS table + 1 SIBLING_TEST_FILES listпјүпјӣжІ’еӢ•д»»дҪ•жҮүз”Ёзўј / Dockerfile / compose / workflow / quick-start.sh / .env.example / 3 еҖӢ spec / 3 еҖӢ runbook / 5 еҖӢ sibling test вҖ” зҙ”зІ№ add-only зҡ„ capstone еҘ‘зҙ„еұӨгҖӮ**Verification**пјҡ`python3 -m pytest tests/test_l11_budget_capstone.py tests/test_readme_deploy_buttons.py tests/test_deploy_parity_across_platforms.py tests/test_digitalocean_app_spec.py tests/test_railway_spec.py tests/test_render_blueprint.py` вҶ’ **131 passed in 0.12 s**пјҲ27 capstone + 11 L11#5 + 22 parity + 27 DO + 16 Railway + 28 RenderпјүпјҢйӣ¶ regression)*

**зёҪй җдј°**пјҡL1-L8 (4.5) + L9 (1) + L10 (0.5) + L11 (1) = **~7 day**

**зӣёдҫқ**пјҡB12 (CF Tunnel) + G1 (readyz) + K1 (must_change_password)гҖӮиӢҘдёүиҖ…йғҪе·Іе®ҢжҲҗпјҢL еҸҜ 7 day е…§е®Ңе·ҘгҖӮ

**й©—ж”¶**пјҡ
- **жң¬ең° WSL2**пјҡ`./scripts/quick-start.sh` вҶ’ дёҖжўқе‘Ҫд»Ө вҶ’ дә’еӢ•ејҸе•Ҹзӯ” вҶ’ е®№еҷЁе•ҹеӢ• + CF Tunnel йҖЈз·ҡ + зҖҸиҰҪеҷЁжү“й–Ӣ Bootstrap wizard вҶ’ е®ҢжҲҗ вҶ’ https://sora-dev.app е…¬з¶ІеҸҜз”ЁгҖӮе…ЁзЁӢ **< 15 еҲҶйҗҳ**пјҢе…¶дёӯжүӢеӢ•ж“ҚдҪң < 2 еҲҶйҗҳгҖӮ
- **йӣІз«Ҝ**пјҡй»һ README зҡ„ Deploy жҢүйҲ• вҶ’ еЎ« env вҶ’ 3 еҲҶйҗҳе…§ public URL еҸҜз”Ё вҶ’ Bootstrap wizard еј•е°ҺиЁӯе®ҡгҖӮ

---

## рҹ…‘рҹ…ў Priority BS вҖ” Bootstrap Vertical-Aware Setup & Platform Catalog

> **иғҢжҷҜ**пјҡPriority L и§ЈжұәдәҶ OmniSight зі»зөұжң¬иә«зҡ„ 4-step bootstrapпјҲadmin password / LLM provider / CF tunnel / smoke testпјүпјҢдҪҶе®Ңе…Ё**жІ’жңү vertical-aware жҰӮеҝө** вҖ” дҪҝз”ЁиҖ…йҒёж“ҮиҰҒеҒҡе“ӘзЁ®з”ўе“ҒпјҲMobile / Embedded / Web / Software / CustomпјүеҫҢпјҢе°ҚжҮүзҡ„ SDK / toolchain / cross-compile з’°еўғпјҲAndroid SDK API levels / Xcode runtimes / Qt versions / NXP MCUXpresso / Yocto / Node + version manager / Python + uv / Rust + rustup зӯүзӯүпјүзӣ®еүҚе…Ёйқ  operator жүӢеӢ• `docker pull` + shell install + з·Ё yamlпјӣP11 #351 зҡ„ Android CLI install row жЁҷ `[O]` е°ұжҳҜжүҝиӘҚйҖҷеҖӢ gapгҖӮ
>
> **зӣ®жЁҷ**пјҡVertical йҒёж“Ү + SDK / toolchain е®үиЈқ + catalog з®ЎзҗҶ + е·ІиЈқй …зӣ®и§ҖеҜҹж•ҙеҘ—жҗ¬йҖІ dashboard UIпјӣйӣ¶еҒңж©ҹжҸ’е…ҘеҲ° productionпјӣе…·еӮҷжңӘдҫҶеҶҚ migrate зҡ„ forward-compatгҖӮOperator еҫһжІ’жңүдәәйңҖиҰҒ SSH еҲ° host ж”№жӘ”жЎҲгҖӮ
>
> **иЁӯиЁҲж ёеҝғ 4 й»һ**пјҡ
> 1. **Catalog йҖІ PGпјҢoperator иө° UI е®Ңж•ҙ CRUD**пјҲYAML еҸӘз•¶ OmniSight upstream зҡ„ seed дҫҶжәҗгҖҒrelease жҷӮ alembic data migration еҜ«йҖІжҜҸеҖӢ tenantпјӣoperator ж”№зҡ„йҖІ PG override layerпјҢupstream re-seed дёҚиҰҶи“Ӣ operator дҝ®ж”№пјү
> 2. **Sidecar `omnisight-installer` йҡ”йӣў install йўЁжҡҙ**пјҲinstall еӨұж•— / CPU жҡҙиЎқдёҚеҪұйҹҝ backend servingпјӣdocker socket йҖҸйҒҺ docker-socket-proxy еҸӘи®Җпјӣз¶Іи·Ҝ + bind-mount /var/lib/omnisight/toolchains/ йҡ”йӣўпјү
> 3. **йӣ¶еҒңж©ҹ additive**пјҲж—ўжңү 4 step `REQUIRED_STEPS` е®Ңе…ЁдёҚеӢ•пјҢеҸӘ add column / add tableпјӣBS.9 еҠ зҡ„ `STEP_VERTICAL_SETUP` жҳҜ optional intermediate stepгҖҒж—ўжңүе·І finalize зҡ„ prod и·ійҒҺпјӣrollback дёҖеҖӢзүҲжң¬ж°ёйҒ еҸҜйҖҶпјү
> 4. **8 еұӨз„ЎйҮҚеҠӣиҰ–иҰәй«”й©— + дҪҝз”ЁиҖ…еҒҸеҘҪ + йӣ»жұ ж„ҹзҹҘ**пјҲidle drift / cursor magnetic tilt / group breathe / cursor distance glow / hover lift + spring / scroll parallax / glass reflection / click inertia springпјӣSettings еҠ  Display sub-pageгҖҒ4 зҙҡ motion levelгҖҒй җиЁӯ dramaticгҖҒ`prefers-reduced-motion` ж°ёйҒ е„Әе…ҲпјӣиЎҢеӢ•иЈқзҪ®йӣ»йҮҸ <50% иҮӘеӢ•йҷҚзҙҡгҖҒ<15% еј·еҲ¶ offпјү
>
> **жҺ’зЁӢ**пјҡ**Priority Y е…Ёж•ёе®Ңе·ҘеҫҢе•ҹеӢ•пјҢBP д№ӢеүҚ**гҖӮзҗҶз”ұпјҡBS и§Јжұә operator зңҹеҜҰз—ӣй»һпјҲдёҖйҖЈдёІ UX ж”№йҖІзҡ„е»¶зәҢпјүпјҢBP жҳҜ deeper architecture investmentпјҲеҸҜжҡ«з·©пјүпјӣBS е®Ңе·ҘеҫҢзҡ„ motion library / catalog / sidecar protocol йғҪжҳҜ BP еҫҢзәҢеҸҜд»Ҙ reuse зҡ„еҹәзӨҺиЁӯж–ҪгҖӮ
>
> **зӣёдҫқпјҲзЎ¬еүҚзҪ®пјү**пјҡ
> - **Priority L**пјҲж—ўжңү 4-step bootstrap wizardпјүвҖ” BS.9 еңЁ L зҡ„ wizard еҫҢйқўеҠ ж–° step
> - **Priority Y е®Ңе·Ҙ**пјҲschema 0039-0050 зўәе®ҡиҗҪең°пјүвҖ” alembic з·ЁиҷҹжүҚиғҪзІҫжә–й җз•ҷ
> - **R20 Phase 0** вң… е·І shipпјҲcommit f515d121пјүвҖ” BS.7 install pipeline иө° PEP coaching cardпјӣBS.10 lazy install hook жҺҘ R20-B coach
> - **Priority I (Multi-tenancy Foundation)** вҖ” catalog `source='operator'` жҳҜ per-tenant scope
>
> **Alembic з·Ёиҷҹй җз•ҷ**пјҡ**0051-0055**пјҲе…ұ 5 иҷҹпјҢй җдј°еҜҰйҡӣз”Ё 3-4 иҷҹпјүгҖӮBS й–Ӣе·ҘеүҚе…Ҳ `ls backend/alembic/versions/ | tail -3` зўәиӘҚ Y еҜҰйҡӣз”ЁеҲ°е“ӘиҷҹпјҢеҫһйӮЈд№ӢеҫҢ +1 й–Ӣе§ӢпјҲ**з•ҷ buffer дёҚжңғжҗ¶**пјүгҖӮ
>
> **зёҪзҜ„еңҚ**пјҡ**~10 day**пјҢ11 phaseпјҢ11 commitпјҢеҸҜеҲҶ 6 sprint дәӨд»ҳгҖӮи©ізҙ° phase иЁҲз•«иҰӢдёӢпјӣADR зҙҡеҲҘзҡ„иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–иҰӢ `docs/design/bs-bootstrap-vertical-aware.md`пјҲBS.0 еҜ«е…ҘпјүгҖӮ

- [x] BS.0.2 `docs/security/bs-installer-threat-model.md` вҖ” sidecar privilege model + docker-socket-proxy ro йҷҗеҲ¶ + sha256 verify йҸҲ + air-gap mode иҰҸж ј
- [x] BS.0.3 R зі»еҲ—йўЁйҡӘзҷ»иЁҳж–°еўһпјҡR24 catalog forward-compat / R25 motion accessibility / R26 sidecar protocol versioning / R27 install job idempotency
- й җдј°пјҡ**0.5 day**

  > BS.1.2 иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• embed seed data йҖІ alembic иҖҢйқһ alembic и·‘жҷӮ read yamlпјүпјҡalembic migration еҝ…й Ҳ deterministic forever вҖ” дёҖе№ҙеҫҢ checkout и·‘ `alembic upgrade 0052` еҝ…й ҲиҲҮд»Ҡж—ҘеҗҲ main жҷӮз”ўеҮәеҗҢдёҖзө„ rowпјӣread yaml at runtime жҠҠжң¬ migration иҲҮ `configs/embedded_catalog/` д»»ж„ҸжҷӮеҲ»зҡ„е…§е®№иҖҰеҗҲпјҢжӯЈжҳҜдёҚиҰҒзҡ„еұ¬жҖ§гҖӮ**жӯЈи§Ј**пјҡalembic е…§ embed `_SEED_ENTRIES` зӮә frozen tuple-of-dictsгҖҒyaml зӮә human-friendly mirrorгҖҒBS.1.5 drift gate еңЁ commit timeпјҲCI redпјүиҖҢйқһ upgrade time еј·еҲ¶е…©иҖ…дёҖиҮҙпјӣеҫҢзәҢ BS.1.2+ catalog и®ҠжӣҙпјҲж–°еўһ entry / vendor еҚҮзүҲпјүиҗҪең°зӮәж–° alembic revпјҲ0053+пјүиө° `INSERT вҖҰ ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`гҖҒдёҚж”№ 0052гҖӮ

  > BS.1.2 иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• sha256 е…Ё NULLгҖҒдёҚж”ҫ placeholderпјүпјҡthreat model В§5.1 еҜ«гҖҢdestructive install requires sha256гҖҚгҖҒдҪҶжң¬ row иҗҪең°жҷӮ install pipelineпјҲBS.7пјүе°ҡжңӘ wire vendor digest fetcherгҖҒprod зңҹеҜҰ digest еҸ–еҫ—йңҖ vendor йҮӢеҮәжҷӮ verify-and-sign жөҒзЁӢгҖӮ**жӯЈи§Ј**пјҡжң¬ row жҠҠ sha256 дёҖеҫӢж”ҫ NULLгҖҒжҳҺж–ҮиЁҳйҢ„зӮәгҖҢfirst-seed еҚ дҪҚгҖҒBS.7 wire еҫҢж–° alembic rev backfillгҖҚпјӣдёҚж”ҫ `'00000вҖҰ'` placeholderпјҲжңғз№һйҒҺе°ҮдҫҶ NOT NULL CHECK + иӘӨе°Һ reviewer д»ҘзӮәе·І verifiedпјүгҖӮR27 idempotency иҲҮжң¬жұәзӯ–жӯЈдәӨпјҲidempotency зңӢ install_jobs.idempotency_keyгҖҒдёҚзңӢ catalog_entries.sha256пјүгҖӮ

жЁЎзө„гҖӮ**иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• density жҳҜ row-level scope дёҚжҳҜ user-level global prefпјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј = еҠ йҖІ `lib/motion-preferences.ts` еҗҢжӘ” user-level pref иЎЁпјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(a) catalog density `data-entry-id`гҖӮ**иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• panel + grid дә’жҸӣ иҖҢйқһдёҰеҲ—йӣҷж¬„ / overlay modalпјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј A = side-by-side master-detailпјҲе·Ұ grid зё®зӘ„гҖҒеҸі detail еҪҲеҮәпјүпјӣA зјәй»һпјҡ(1) catalog еҚЎзүҮжң¬иә«е°ұеҜҶгҖҒgrid зё®зӘ„еҫҢ 5 еҚЎзүҮзӢҖж…ӢиҰ–иҰәе·®з•°пјҲconic ring / hazard stripe / pulse-purpleпјүж“ еЈ“и®ҠзіҠгҖҒ(2) иЎҢеӢ•жқҝпјҲ< selection state еңЁ `<CatalogTab>` е…§ иҖҢйқһ lift еҲ° pageпјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј = page еҠ  `useState<string | null>` + жҠҠ entry / handlers е…ЁйғЁ prop drill йҖІ CatalogTabпјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(1) selection hookгҖҒеҶҚеҠ  selection state жңғи®“ page и®ҠжҲҗгҖҢcatalog-aware pageгҖҚгҖҒйҒ•еҸҚ page = route shell еҺҹеүҮгҖҒ(3) `<CatalogTab>` е·Іжңү family / search / sort / density 4 еҖӢе…§йғЁ stateгҖҒеҠ з¬¬ 5 pipeline SSE re-fetch entries жҷӮгҖҒoperator зңӢеҲ°зҡ„ panel metadataпјҲprogress / activity / used_byпјүжңғи®ҠйҷіиҲҠгҖҒеҝ…й ҲжүӢеӢ• re-pinгҖҒ(2) entries reference и®ҠеҢ–жҷӮ React strict equality жҜ”ијғеӨұж•ҲгҖҒеҸҜиғҪиӘӨи§ё re-render bugгҖҒ(3) by id resolve жҳҜ master-detail жЁҷжә–жЁЎејҸпјҲApple Mail / Notion / Linear йғҪйҖҷжЁЈпјүвҖ” operator зңӢеҲ°зҡ„жҳҜгҖҢжңҖж–°зүҲжң¬гҖҚnotгҖҢй»һж“Ҡзһ¬й–“зҡ„еҝ«з…§гҖҚгҖӮд»Јеғ№пјҡжҜҸж¬Ў render еӨҡдёҖж¬Ў array.findпјӣcatalog йҖҡеёё < 100 entriesгҖҒO(n) еҸҜеҝҪз•ҘпјӣBS.6.5 иҷӣж“¬еҢ–иҗҪең°еҫҢ entries д№ҹжңғиө° useMemo + Map зҙўеј•гҖӮ**иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• conic-gradient йҖІеәҰз’°иө° div overlay иҖҢйқһ SVG arcпјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј = SVG `<circle>` + `pathLength` + `stroke-dasharray` иЁҲз®—йҖІеәҰеј§пјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(1) cross-browser е°Қ stroke-dasharray иЎҢзӮәдёҚдёҖиҮҙпјҲSafari `0.5%` rounding / Firefox ж•ҙж•ёеҢ–пјүгҖҒз”ўз”ҹ aliased йӮҠгҖҒ(2) BS.6.2 е·Із”Ё conic-gradient еҒҡеҚЎзүҮйҖІеәҰз’°гҖҒиҰ–иҰәдёҖиҮҙжҖ§иҰҒжұӮ panel orb з”ЁеҗҢжЁЈиӘһиЁҖгҖҒoperator дёҖзңји®ҖгҖҢеҗҢжЁЈзҡ„йҖІеәҰиҰ–иҰәгҖҚгҖҒ(3) `buildInstallProgressGradient(progress)` е·І exported pure helperгҖҒжң¬ row зӣҙжҺҘиӨҮз”ЁгҖҒйҒҝе…Қйӣҷд»ҪйҖІеәҰиӘһж„ҸжјӮз§»гҖӮжң¬ row жҠҠ conic ring з”Ё absolute div и“ӢеңЁ SVG дёҠгҖҒ`bg-[var(--card)]` е…§еұӨ disc и“ӢжҺүдёӯеӨ®гҖҒз•ҷ 2px з’°йЎҜеј§ вҖ” еҗҢ BS.6.2 wrapper-shell patternгҖӮ**иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• `formatRelativeTime` 7 buckets иҖҢйқһ ICU `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat`пјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј = `new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat("en")`пјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(1) i18n йӮ„жІ’йҖІ BS scopeгҖҒй җиЁӯиӘһзі» routing з”ұ i18n зі»зөұиІ иІ¬пјҲlib/i18nпјүгҖҒжң¬ row дёҚи©ІеҒ·и·‘ locale-aware ж јејҸгҖҒ(2) ICU output (`5 minutes ago` / `2 hours ago`) жҜ” short еҪўејҸпјҲ`5m ago` / `2h ago`пјүй•·гҖҒpanel timeline еҜ¬еәҰз·Ҡж№ҠгҖҒиҰ–иҰәз ҙзҜҖеҘҸгҖҒ(3) 7 жЎ¶ cover 99% жЎҲдҫӢгҖҒedge caseпјҲ`Date.parse` еӨұж•— вҶ’ "вҖ”"пјүеҸ—жҺ§пјӣfuture i18n жҠҪеҮәеҸҜиө° `useLocale()` decorator patternгҖӮжң¬ row иҮӘеҜ« short-form е°ҚйҪҠ BS code base ж—ўжңү `host-device-panel`/`task-backlog` зҡ„ short-form ж…ЈдҫӢгҖӮ**иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• `useState` жҮ’еҲқе§ӢеҢ– nowMs иҖҢйқһ `useMemo`пјү**пјҡ(1) `react-hooks/purity` lint иҰҸеүҮзҰҒжӯў render жңҹй–“е‘јеҸ« `Date.now()`пјҲimpureпјүгҖҒ(2) `useMemo(() => Date.now(), [])` д№ҹз®— render-time impure callпјҢ(3) `useState(() => disabled button) вҖ” BS.7 Install pipeline epic 6/9 rowгҖӮ**ж ёеҝғ contract**пјҡ(1) **retryInstallJob backend wire shape**: `POST /installer/jobs/{id}/retry` body `{ idempotency_key }` only вҖ” `entry_id` is pulled from the source row by the backend (`backend/routers/installer.py::retry_job` line 1064 SELECT) so the helper does not echo it; (2) **URL-encoded job id segment**: `encodeURIComponent(jobId)` in the path so a malformed id (slashes / spaces) cannot break the route вҖ” backend then 422s on the `_INSTALL_JOB_ID_RE` regex check; (3) **Failure surfacing**: 403 PEP-deny / 404 source-not-found / 409 source-still-active / 408 timeout all throw `ApiError` from the `request<T>` wrapper вҶ’ global `<ApiErrorToastCenter />` lights up. `handleRetry` catches and `console.error`s only so dev consoles see the precise reason without double-surfacing the toast; (4) **Retry path through PEP HOLD**: backend `retry_job` delegates to `create_job` after source-row validation вҶ’ fresh PEP HOLD вҶ’ operator sees BS.7.2 install_intercept coaching card again вҶ’ on approve a NEW queued row appears (preserving audit history; source row stays untouched). The retry endpoint is method-agnostic per ADR В§4.4 step 3 вҖ” `shell_script` not auto-retried by sidecar but the endpoint creates a fresh queued row regardless; (5) **Modal open contract**: `job!=null` вҶ’ controlled-open via Radix `<Dialog open=true>`; `job==null` вҶ’ no portal mount (Dialog `open=false` short-circuits before children render). `entryDisplayName` falls back to `job.entry_id || job.id` so the title never reads empty; (6) **error_reason rendering**: only on `state==='failed'` branch (Completed / Cancelled rows have a state pill but no destructive-red banner). Null reason on a failed row вҶ’ `INSTALL_LOG_NO_REASON_PLACEHOLDER` ("(no reason recorded)"); (7) **log_tail rendering**: empty string вҶ’ `INSTALL_LOG_EMPTY_PLACEHOLDER` ("(no log output yet)") + Copy button auto-disabled (no content to copy). Non-empty tail rendered verbatim in `<pre>` with whitespace-pre-wrap + max-h-[16rem] overflow-auto so a 4 KiB tail does not blow out the dialog; (8) **Retry button conditional**: `onRetry?` prop omitted вҶ’ no retry button in footer (no dead button), wired вҶ’ renders + calls `onRetry(job)` then `onClose()` so the operator returns to the catalog watching for the new install_jobs row; (9) **Copy button error swallowing**: `defaultCopy` rejects when `navigator.clipboard` is unavailable (older browser / permission-denied); the modal silently swallows so the operator can fall back to manual select-and-copy. Tests inject `copyToClipboard` stub since jsdom does not provide a fully spec-compliant clipboard API; (10) **handleViewLog stale-snapshot recovery**: SSE snapshot has the row (typical: operator just watched the install fail) вҶ’ render immediately via `setLogModalJob(job)`. Page reloaded after the failure SSE has rolled off (no row in `installJobs`) вҶ’ check `entry.metadata.lastInstallJobId` (catalog feed annotation) + fetch via `getInstallJob`. Neither available вҶ’ synthesize a stub `InstallJob` with empty `log_tail` + null `error_reason` so the modal still opens and shows the placeholders rather than silently doing nothing; (11) **handleRetry stale-snapshot fallback**: no row in `installJobs` for the entry вҶ’ fall back to `createInstallJob(entry.id)` (treats it as a fresh install). This covers the "page-loaded-after-failure" path where the entry feed flag still says `failed` but the in-memory snapshot has been GC'd; (12) **InstallLogModal modal-side onRetry shim**: the modal hands its `onRetry` callback an `InstallJob` (not a `CatalogEntry`); the page wrapper rebuilds a minimal `CatalogEntry` from `job.entry_id` (with `family: "custom"` as the safe default since `family` is required) so it can route through the same `handleRetry` that the card uses. **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• retryInstallJob дёҚжҺҘеҸ— entry_idпјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј = `retryInstallJob(entryId)` иө° latest-failed-job lookup on the backendпјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(a) backend route is keyed off `job_id` (`POST /installer/jobs/{job_id}/retry`) вҖ” adding an entry-id route doubles the surface area; (b) frontend already has the `installJobs` array via `useInstallJobs()` so picking the right `job_id` is local; (c) the existing backend `retry_job` semantics deliberately preserve audit history by cloning a SPECIFIC source row вҖ” entry-id-keyed retry would have to pick "latest" itself and that's an arbitrary product decision better left at the call site. **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• InstallLogModal дёҚиҮӘеё¶ SSE subscriptionпјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј = modal subscribes to `installer_progress` and updates `log_tail` liveпјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(a) the row is failed/terminal вҖ” there will be no further SSE events updating it; (b) coupling modal to SSE expands the scope of BS.7.6 (modal вҶ’ hook вҶ’ SSE bus) without operator value; (c) page wrapper already owns the `installJobs` snapshot via `useInstallJobs()` and re-passes a fresh `job` prop on the next render вҖ” modal stays purely presentational and reusable from non-SSE contexts (BS.8 history view will reuse it). **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• handleViewLog еҗҢжӯҘ + getInstallJob е…©жўқи·Ҝ vs always-fetchпјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј = always call `getInstallJob(job.id)` even when SSE snapshot has the rowпјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(a) wasteful round-trip when the SSE snapshot is fresh (operator just watched the install fail seconds ago); (b) backend GET adds latency to the modal open animation вҖ” feels sluggish; (c) two-path is the standard SWR / stale-while-revalidate pattern; SSE row is always the freshest because backend publishes AFTER the UPDATE commits. **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• onRetry shim re-builds CatalogEntry instead of plumbing onRetryByJob through CatalogCardпјү**пјҡжӣҝд»Ј = add `onRetryByJob?(jobId)` prop to CatalogCardпјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(a) BS.6.8 / BS.7.1 / BS.7.5 frozen tests lock CatalogCardProps interface (53+ tests reference `onInstall` / `onRetry` / `onViewLog` exact shape); (b) CatalogCard already gets the entry, asking it to know about `InstallJob` would invert the dependency; (c) the shim is ~6 lines of safe rebuild вҖ” subsequent SSE retry path emitting state=queued must not clobber it back to null) / synthesize parametric over all 5 InstallJobState values (queued/running/completed/failed/cancelled) вҖ” locks that synthesize never filters or remaps state at the wire-format boundary; (3) **3 deliberate non-tests** (intentionally NOT included): "subscribeEvents called exactly once" was already covered by existing test #1 of useInstallJobs / "test that synthesize zero-fills bytes_done numeric fields" was already covered by existing synthesize zero-fill test / "test that hook closes SSE on unmount" was already covered by existing unmount test #9. **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• .test.tsx иҖҢйқһ .test.ts as TODO row еӯ—йқўпјү**пјҡTODO row literally says `use-install-jobs.test.ts` (no x) but the file already existed as `.test.tsx` from BS.7.4 because `renderHook` + React Testing Library require JSX support; the existing 32 cases couldn't run from a `.ts` file. Adding NEW 8 cases to the existing `.tsx` is the right scope-minimal path; renaming to `.ts` would break the 32 frozen cases. Treat the TODO description as a stale typo вҖ” the row's intent is clearly "extend existing hook test file" not "create a parallel `.ts` companion". **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• ETA derivation test uses (clock еҮҪж•ё + rerender) vs vi.useFakeTimersпјү**пјҡvi.useFakeTimers controls global Date.now but the drawer's nowMs prop accepts a function override and accesses it inside useEffect; useFakeTimers wouldn't intercept the inline closure. The clock-function pattern (mirroring the existing speed-derivation test #8) is the canonical idiom for this drawer. **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• merge mutability test compares against snapshot vs deep-freezeпјү**пјҡObject.freeze deep-frozen would throw on mutation (clear failure signal) but Object.freeze isn't recursive on nested objects (e.g. result_json) and freezing primitive object literals doesn't catch the more subtle "merge accidentally pushes a value into prev" bug. snapshot-via-spread + toEqual catches both shallow + nested mutations and is the React-test idiom. **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• synthesize parametric test uses it.each vs explicit forEachпјү**пјҡvitest it.each generates one collected case per array entry вҶ’ CI test count is 5 (one per InstallJobState), each with its own pass/fail signal; forEach inside a single `it` would collapse all 5 into one pass/fail. Per-state granularity matters for catching "synthesize accidentally drops 'cancelled' but keeps the other four". **Module-global state зЁҪж ё**пјҲper `docs/sop/implement_phase_step.md` Step 1пјүпјҡtests are stateless function bodies вҖ” `mkProgress` / `mkJob` / `mkJobRow` are pure factories with no closure state, no module-level cache, no thread-locals. `vi.clearAllMocks()` in afterEach unwinds the `vi.fn()` `subscribeEvents` mock between tests. `primeSSE(api)` returns a per-test-instance `listeners[]` + `closed` counter; no cross-test pollution. The drawer + hook themselves were already audited (BS.7.3 + BS.7.4 HANDOFF) вҖ” both store all state per-component-instance via React useState/useRef. Answer #1 (per-worker stateless derivation) applies вҖ” each test worker re-imports the test module and re-runs the same factory code. **Read-after-write timing зЁҪж ё**пјҡN/A вҖ” tests run synchronously inside `act(...)` blocks; SSE events emitted via `sse.emit(...)` are synchronously dispatched to the registered listener (single-threaded JS event loop); no asyncio.gather / no parallel writes / no Postgres / no Redis. The hook's internal setState callback runs inside act вҶ’ React 18 functional update batching is observed before the next assertion. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep**пјҲ`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`пјүе°Қ 2 еҖӢи®ҠеӢ•жӘ”пјҲtest/components/install-progress-drawer.test.tsx + test/hooks/use-install-jobs.test.tsxпјү0 е‘ҪдёӯпјҲзҙ”еүҚз«Ҝ TSX test з„Ў `last_sync_status='pending_manual'` + clears `last_synced_at` (jumps row to top of `idx_catalog_subscriptions_due` `NULLS FIRST` queue) + bumps `updated_at`; tenant-scoped via `WHERE tenant_id = $tid` + 404 if not found; emits audit log `catalog_source_sync_requested`; (2) **`<SourcesTab />`** вҖ” purely presentational (page wrapper owns the data flow via `useCatalogSources` hook + per-action callbacks); list view sorted newest-first by `created_at` desc; per-row layout = feed_url + auth method + refresh interval + last-synced relative chip + sync-status chip; per-row buttons = Sync now (calls `onSync` with the source) + Remove (opens inline confirm overlay; only after confirm calls `onRemove`); toolbar = source count + `Add source` button; (3) **Add-source form** вҖ” inline (no Dialog) collapsed under toolbar; fields = Feed URL (required, http/https) + Auth method dropdown (none/basic/bearer/signed_url) + Auth secret ref (only when method != none) + Refresh interval seconds input + preset dropdown (1h/6h/24h/7d/30d); form validation runs on submit via `validateSourcesTabForm` (URL regex + non-empty secret-ref when auth != none + 60-2592000s range + no-whitespace secret-ref); on validation fail вҶ’ inline form-error banner + onAdd not called; on submit success вҶ’ form closes + reset; on backend error вҶ’ inline form-error banner + form stays open; (4) **`useCatalogSources` hook** вҖ” REST poll on mount + manual `refresh()` + mountedRef guard (mirrors `useInstalledEntries` pattern); exposes `sources: CatalogSource[]` + `loading` + `error` + `refresh()`; no SSE subscription (sources mutate only on operator intent вҖ” caller-driven refresh after each action is enough); (5) **5 lib/api helpers** вҖ” `listCatalogSources` (GET, w/ optional `?enabled_only=true` query) / `createCatalogSource` (POST 201) / `patchCatalogSource` (PATCH, URL-encoded path) / `deleteCatalogSource` (DELETE 200) / `syncCatalogSource` (POST 200, no body); all walk `request<T>()` for CSRF / X-Tenant-Id / retry; (6) **4 validation helpers** вҖ” `normaliseCatalogSourceFeedUrl` (trim) / `validateCatalogSourceFeedUrl` (length + http/https scheme) / `validateCatalogSourceAuthSecretRef` (max 256 chars + no whitespace, mirrors backend `_no_whitespace_in_ref` field_validator) / `validateCatalogSourceRefreshInterval` (60s-30d integer range, mirrors backend `Field(ge=60, le=30*86400)`); (7) **жҜҸ row testid иҰҸзҜ„**пјҡ`sources-tab` / `sources-tab-toolbar` / `sources-tab-count` / `sources-tab-add-button` / `sources-tab-empty` / `sources-tab-list` / `sources-tab-fetch-error` / `sources-tab-fetch-retry` / `sources-tab-error` / per-row `sources-tab-row-{id}` (её¶ `data-source-id` + `data-enabled`) + `-url-{id}` / `-auth-{id}` / `-interval-{id}` / `-last-synced-{id}` / `-status-{id}` (её¶ `data-status`) / `-disabled-{id}` (only when not enabled) / `-sync-{id}` / `-remove-{id}` / `-confirm-{id}` + `-confirm-cancel-{id}` / `-confirm-delete-{id}` / form: `sources-tab-form` / `-feed-url` / `-auth-method` / `-auth-secret-ref` / `-refresh-interval` / `-refresh-preset` / `-form-error` / `-form-cancel` / `-form-submit` / `-form-close`. **иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ•еҠ  backend sync route иҖҢйқһзҙ”еүҚз«Ҝ stubпјү**пјҡ(a) "sync now" жҳҜ operator зҡ„жҳҺзўә intent вҖ” йңҖиҰҒ audit log row (`catalog_source_sync_requested`) иӯүжҳҺдҪ•жҷӮдҪ•дәәжҢүдәҶпјӣз„Ў backend route вҶ’ еүҚз«ҜжҢүйҲ•з„Ў effectгҖҒеҸӘжҳҜиҰ–иҰәе®үж…°еҠ‘гҖҒжҳҜ hostile UXпјӣ(b) з”Ё `last_synced_at = NULL` + `last_sync_status='pending_manual'` зҡ„ stamp жЁЎејҸ вҖ” ж—ўжңү `idx_catalog_subscriptions_due` partial index е·І `NULLS FIRST` жҺ’еәҸпјҢcron worker иҗҪең°жҷӮ free жӢҝеҲ° manual-sync е„Әе…Ҳпјӣ(c) з„ЎеҗҢжӯҘ fetch in-request вҖ” зҙ” SQL UPDATEпјҢrequest path < 10msпјӣfeed worker (separate row) жүҚеҜҰйҡӣ fetch + parse + INSERT catalog_entriesпјӣ(d) `pending_manual` discriminator жҳҜ stable stringгҖҒжңӘдҫҶ worker еҸҜ distinguish manual vs cron-driven sync sourceгҖӮ**иЁӯиЁҲжұәзӯ–пјҲзӮәдҪ• Sources tab жҳҜ inline form not Dialogпјү**пјҡ(a) Add-source form еӯ—ж®өе°‘ (4-5 fields)гҖҒдёҚйңҖиҰҒ Dialog зҡ„ modal-overlay йҡ”йӣўпјӣinline form и®“ operator еңЁ add еҗҢжҷӮзңӢеҲ°ж—ўжңү listпјӣ(b) Cleanup-unused / Disk-breakdown / Uninstall-confirm йғҪжҳҜ modal вҖ” е®ғеҖ‘иҰҒ *йҳ»еЎһ* operator жіЁж„ҸеҠӣ (destructive жҲ– multi-step)пјӣAdd source overlay иҖҢйқһ UninstallConfirmModal-style Dialogпјү**пјҡ(a) Subscription remove жІ’жңү dependency-check йңҖжұӮ (subscription жҳҜ catalog feed дҫҶжәҗгҖҒдёҚиў« catalog_entries еҸҚеҗ‘ reference)пјӣ(b) columnпјӣе•ҸйЎҢпјҡ(a) catalog_entries.metadata жҳҜ JSONB й–Ӣж”ҫ schema (alembic 0051 + ADR В§3.4 forward-compat иҰҸеүҮ)гҖҒlicense жҳҜеӨ©з„¶ vendor-specific metadataгҖҒдёҚйңҖиҰҒеҚҮ columnпјӣ(b) ж—ўжңү admin еҸҜйҒёж“Ү и·ійҒҺ ping зӣҙжҺҘ submitгҖҒеҫҢз«Ҝ backend зңҹжӯЈдёӢијүд»Қ enforce sha256 + size жӘўжҹҘгҖҒping дёҚжҳҜжңҖеҫҢдёҖйҒ“йҳІз·ҡ; (d) зҙ”еүҚз«Ҝ = 0 backend ### е№іиЎҢеҲҶе·Ҙе»әиӯ°

еҰӮжһңеҸӘжңү 1 жўқ AI agent track + operatorпјҡеҡҙж јжҢү Sprint 1вҶ’6 й ҶеәҸгҖҒжҜҸеҖӢ phase е…§ sub-checkbox й ҶеәҸеҒҡгҖӮ

еҰӮжһңжңү 2 дәә / 2 жўқ track еҸҜе№іиЎҢпјҲдҫӢеҰӮ Agent-software-beta + дҪ иҮӘе·ұпјүпјҢеҲҮжі•еҰӮдёӢпјҡ

- **Track A (backend-heavy, еҒҸ architecture)**пјҡBS.1 вҶ’ BS.2 вҶ’ BS.4 вҶ’ BS.7 (sidecar/PEP/job pipeline йғЁеҲҶ) вҶ’ BS.10 вҶ’ ~6 day
- **Track B (frontend-heavy, еҒҸ UX)**пјҡBS.0 (ADR) вҶ’ BS.3 (motion lib) вҶ’ BS.5 (hero) вҶ’ BS.6 (catalog tab) вҶ’ BS.7 (drawer/card UI йғЁеҲҶ) вҶ’ BS.8 (installed/sources tab) вҶ’ BS.9 (wizard step) вҶ’ ~7 day

**Track еҗҢжӯҘй»һ**пјҲе…© track еҝ…й ҲеңЁйҖҷжҷӮе°ҚйҪҠжүҚиғҪеҫҖдёӢпјүпјҡ
- еҗҢжӯҘй»һ 1пјҡBS.2 ship еҫҢ вҶ’ Track B жүҚиғҪеңЁ BS.5 й–Ӣе§ӢжҺҘ backend API
- еҗҢжӯҘй»һ 2пјҡBS.4 + BS.6 ship еҫҢ вҶ’ е…© track дёҖиө·еҒҡ BS.7 (Track A еҒҡ backend еҚҠйӮҠгҖҒTrack B еҒҡ frontend еҚҠйӮҠ)
- еҗҢжӯҘй»һ 3пјҡBS.7 ship еҫҢ вҶ’ Track B жҺҘи‘—еҒҡ BS.8 + BS.9пјҢTrack A еҒҡ BS.10

Wall time еҫһ 10 day вҶ’ **~7 day**пјҲTrack B жҳҜй—ңйҚөи·Ҝеҫ‘гҖҒTrack A зҙ„ 60% жҷӮй–“ idle еҸҜеҺ»еҒҡеҲҘзҡ„пјүгҖӮ

еҰӮжһңжңү 3 дәә / 3 жўқ track е№іиЎҢпјҡ
- **Track A (PG + API)**пјҡBS.1 вҶ’ BS.2 вҶ’ BS.10 backend вҶ’ ~3 day
- **Track B (sidecar + install methods)**пјҡBS.4 вҶ’ BS.7 backend еҚҠйӮҠ вҶ’ ~2 day
- **Track C (UI + motion)**пјҡBS.0 вҶ’ BS.3 вҶ’ BS.5 вҶ’ BS.6 вҶ’ BS.7 frontend еҚҠйӮҠ вҶ’ BS.8 вҶ’ BS.9 вҶ’ ~7 day

Wall time вҶ’ **~7 day**пјҲTrack C д»ҚжҳҜй—ңйҚөи·Ҝеҫ‘гҖҒUI е·ҘдҪңеӨ©з„¶ијғйҮҚпјӣA/B жҸҗж—©е®ҢжҲҗеҸҜеҚ”еҠ© Track C ж”¶е°ҫжҲ– BS.11 polishпјүгҖӮ

**жңҖеӨ§е№іиЎҢеәҰпјҲ4+ track дёҚеҶҚзё®зҹӯжҷӮзЁӢпјү**пјҡеӣ зӮә BS.6 + BS.7 frontend + BS.8 + BS.9 йғҪжҳҜеҗҢдёҖеҖӢжӘ”жЎҲйӣҶпјҲcatalog UI + install drawer + bootstrap wizardпјүпјҢеҗҢдәәеҜ«жҜ”е№іиЎҢеҜ«еҝ«гҖҒmerge иЎқзӘҒе°‘гҖӮ3 track жҳҜ sweet spotгҖӮ
